title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: erifunctions Carter Center ERI team’s R package — API Data Analysts (DAs) Epidemiologists (Epis) interact TCC’s Azure-centred data system across countries (Haiti, DR, Uganda, OEPA) diseases (malaria, oncho, LF, SCH, STH). Users domain experts, software developers: install install_github() / renv, authenticate via interactive browser auth, call functions — edit package. Optimise every change clarity reliability.

title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: Data lives data/ Azure blob canonical path built eri_data_path(): eri_approve() human gate. Nothing reaches processed/ without ; writes approval log registers file data catalog (_catalog/data_catalog.yaml). projects/ blob legacy contractor (hsp-mal) space; V2 migrating authority data/ (see roadmap Phase 3). Metadata stores (catalog, ODK registry, artifact registry) YAML blobs — see ADR-0002 concurrency rules touching .

title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: Naming: exported functions eri_* (domain verbs like run_dq_checks); internal helpers .eri_* @keywords internal, exported. Style: cli::cli_* user-facing messages; roxygen2 markdown; functions get @examples (wrap live Azure/ODK calls \dontrun{}). Source layout: one domain per file R/ (dal.R, dq.R, catalog.R, odk*.R, research.R, reports*.R, spatial.R, epi*.R, onboarding.R, …). Schemas bundled YAML inst/schemas/; templates inst/templates/. changing exports roxygen, regenerate NAMESPACE/man/ devtools::document().

title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: codebase grew project-driven development, scattered local fixes global problems. adding function pattern: Search existing helper first — reuse azure_io() / eri_read() / eri_write() / eri_data_path() DQ/catalog/research machinery rather re-implementing. problem general (countries/diseases/users hit ), solve generally put decision ADR — don’t bury one-single workflow. Keep roadmap ADRs current: change alters plan decision, update docs/roadmap.md / add ADR PR.

title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: devtools::document() exports/docs changed; devtools::check() pass clean. devtools::test() — unit tests run offline; live integration tests tests/testthat/test-smoke.R run Sys.setenv(ERI_SMOKE_TESTS = "true"). Update NEWS.md bump DESCRIPTION Version shipping — **Version:** line near top README.md, track DESCRIPTION automatically drifted (twice). Note: cloud dev container local R/quarto — R CMD check pkgdown::build_site() verified GitHub Actions CI, locally.

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: Open issue first. Every change starts GitHub issue scoped ~1-2 hours work. Branch dev {issue-number}-{short-slug} (e.g. 54-quarto-template). PR targets dev, main. main receives version-bumped phase releases. devtools::check() must 0 errors, 0 warnings requesting review. Issues closed manually PR merges.

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: Schemas live inst/schemas/ (surveillance) inst/schemas/cmr/ (CMR). Run eri_onboard_country() eri_onboard_cmr() generate template working directory. Fill TODO sections. Run eri_schema_validate("your_schema.yaml") — fix issues reports. Copy finished file inst/schemas/ (inst/schemas/cmr/) open PR.

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: exported functions named eri_*() — verb-first, tab-completable. Internal helpers prefixed . documented #' @keywords internal. Use cli::cli_abort() / cli::cli_inform() / cli::cli_alert_success() user-facing messages — message(), warning(), stop(). non-ASCII characters R source files (em dashes, smart quotes, tab literals). Prose files rule; see Documentation prose . withr::local_mocked_bindings — use testthat::local_mocked_bindings(). Temp files cleaned withr::defer(unlink(tmp)).

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: Vignettes (vignettes/*.Rmd), README.md, files docs/ public pkgdown site. Keep prose consistent voice: em dashes connectors. Use comma, semicolon, colon, parentheses, period instead. one hard rule; em dashes common drift. Oxford commas lists; specific hedging vague (“may reflect X rather Y”, “might possibly”). end--paragraph recaps reader just read, triadic lists used reflex rather three items actually true. Register file. -guides address reader directly (“”, imperatives); keep . narrative docs (README.md, docs/vision.md, docs/roadmap.md) carry declarative, caveat-forward voice (“X”, “caution Y”). Bolding fine UI terms, role names, layer names; navigational cue, emphasis sake.

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: issues phase closed: 1. Update NEWS.md README.md. 2. Bump Version: DESCRIPTION. 3. Merge dev → main via squash PR. 4. Create GitHub release tagged v{version}.

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: eri_onboard_disease() writes one YAML file per data type: Use dry_run = TRUE first preview written:
code: library(erifunctions)  eri_onboard_disease(   disease    = "schisto",   country    = "ug",   data_types = c("mda", "prevalence"),   output_dir = "schemas/"     # write locally, not to the package ) # Writes schemas/ug_schisto_programmatic_treatment.yaml and #        schemas/ug_schisto_research_prevalence.yaml (ADR-0012: mda -> programmatic/ #        treatment, prevalence -> research/prevalence) eri_onboard_disease("rb", "eth", dry_run = TRUE)

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: Open skeleton file complete TODO sections: Key decisions make column: required – pipeline hard-error column missing? type – numeric, character, categorical, date aliases – column name variants found raw data files range – numeric columns; flag implausible values allowed_values – categorical columns; triggers flag value unexpected translations – map raw variants canonical value (e.g. "YES" -> "Yes")
code: # ug_schisto_programmatic_treatment.yaml (excerpt) columns:   species_target:     required: true     type: categorical     aliases: [Species, species, schisto_species]     allowed_values:       - S.mansoni        # fill in the species relevant to this country       - S.haematobium    school_name:           # add country-specific columns here     required: false     type: character     aliases: [School, school_name, etablissement]

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: Run eri_schema_validate() check structural problems: Fix flagged issues moving . Common problems: Missing required type column definition consistency rule referencing column name defined columns temporal.year_col temporal.period_col pointing column columns
code: result <- eri_schema_validate("schemas/ug_schisto_mda.yaml") # Prints: "Schema ug_schisto_mda.yaml is valid." if no issues found # Or:     a summary of issues with field names and messages print(result)

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: submitting, test schema real synthetic data: sample data passes cleanly (nrow(result$flags) == 0), schema ready real data.
code: schema <- load_dq_schema("ug", "schisto", "programmatic", "treatment", azcontainer = NULL) # azcontainer = NULL tells it to use the local file, not Azure  # Create a small synthetic data frame that matches your schema sample_data <- data.frame(   year       = c(2023L, 2023L, 2024L),   round      = c(1L, 2L, 1L),   district   = c("Kampala", "Wakiso", "Kampala"),   target_pop = c(5000L, 3200L, 5100L),   treated    = c(4800L, 3100L, 4950L),   coverage_pct = c(96.0, 96.9, 97.1) )  result <- run_dq_checks(sample_data, schema) cat("Flags:", nrow(result$flags), "\n")

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: Copy validated schema file inst/schemas/ using four-part identity {country}_{disease}_{data_source}_{data_type}.yaml (ADR-0012): PR checklist: Schema file inst/schemas/ correct name eri_schema_validate() returns 0 issues load_dq_schema("ug", "schisto", "programmatic", "treatment", azcontainer = NULL) works least one load_dq_schema + eri_schema_validate test added tests/testthat/test-onboarding.R following existing pattern NEWS.md entry next version section

Confirm works listing top container, get tibble back, : name column holds full path container root (deeper listing like eri_list("uga/demo/research/raw", …) returns uga/demo/research/raw/<file> names); pass full_names = FALSE just leaf filenames. automation / CI (headless). scheduled job browser. Set service principal, ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_ID ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_SECRET environment (pass creds_yaml_path=), get_azure_storage_connection() signs non-interactively instead. secret true secret ; tenant, app, endpoint non-sensitive defaults. 403 Forbidden? bug, Azure telling account access storage. Session expires 2026-06-26T20:00:45.690Z. list_odk_projects(con = con) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   project_id project   description #>        <int> <chr>     <chr> #> 1          5 Uganda    NA #> 2          7 Training  NA #> 3         11 testing   NA

text: eri_query() gives two ways choose SQL can see, combine: Catalog-driven: name data axes (country, disease, data_type, …) eri_query() finds matching processed files, stamps row country/disease/data_source/data_type/period, unions one table called data. roll across countries periods. Explicit tables: hand named tables directly (tables = list(name = …)), data.frame, local .parquet, data/ blob path. join inputs (population table, lookup) approved data.

title: Answering ad-hoc data requests with SQL (eri_query)
text: catalog index approved data; eri_query() reads . total malaria cases country every approved aggregate dataset: (Illustrative numbers, catalog differ. -memory examples real output can reproduce.) every row stamped provenance, can GROUP country, period, data_type, etc. without joining anything. (five names reserved mode, see ?eri_query.) sure ’s available? eri_catalog_query() filters lists files attached.
code: eri_query(   "SELECT country, SUM(total_cases) AS cases      FROM data GROUP BY country ORDER BY cases DESC",   disease = "malaria", data_type = "aggregate", data_con = data_con ) #> ℹ Querying 1 table: "data". #> ✔ Returned 2 rows. #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   country  cases #>   <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 ht      12345 #> 2 dr       9876

title: Answering ad-hoc data requests with SQL (eri_query)
text: explicit mode runs entirely memory, can run right now. Say request lands two small tables, weekly case counts population denominator: Aggregate: total cases province: Join: incidence per 1,000, combining two tables: Window functions: week--week change, awkward base R one line SQL: can mix modes, pass catalog filters tables = list(pop = …) join approved cases population table supply.
code: cases <- data.frame(   province = c("North", "North", "South", "South", "East"),   epiweek  = c(1, 2, 1, 2, 1),   cases    = c(40, 55, 12, 9, 30) ) pop <- data.frame(   province = c("North", "South", "East"),   pop      = c(120000, 45000, 80000) ) eri_query(   "SELECT province, SUM(cases) AS cases      FROM cases GROUP BY province ORDER BY cases DESC",   tables = list(cases = cases) ) #> ℹ Querying 1 table: "cases". #> ✔ Returned 3 rows. #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   province cases #>   <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 North       95 #> 2 East        30 #> 3 South       21 eri_query(   "SELECT c.province, SUM(c.cases) * 1000.0 / p.pop AS incidence_per_1000      FROM cases c JOIN pop p USING (province)      GROUP BY c.province, p.pop ORDER BY incidence_per_1000 DESC",   tables = list(cases = cases, pop = pop) ) #> ℹ Querying 2 tables: "cases" and "pop". #> ✔ Returned 3 rows. #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   province incidence_per_1000 #>   <chr>                 <dbl> #> 1 North                 0.792 #> 2 South                 0.467 #> 3 East                  0.375 eri_query(   "SELECT province, epiweek, cases,           cases - LAG(cases) OVER (PARTITION BY province ORDER BY epiweek) AS wow_change      FROM cases ORDER BY province, epiweek",   tables = list(cases = cases) ) #> ℹ Querying 1 table: "cases". #> ✔ Returned 5 rows. #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   province epiweek cases wow_change #>   <chr>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 East           1    30         NA #> 2 North          1    40         NA #> 3 North          2    55         15 #> 4 South          1    12         NA #> 5 South          2     9         -3

what: The data system in one picture
title: Data Analyst cheat sheet
text: Everything lives Azure data/ blob canonical 5-axis path. Build eri_data_path(): never hand-type . data_source = channel (data arrives): surveillance · programmatic · research. data_type = measure (counts): case · aggregate · treatment · mmdp · training · survey · tas · prevalence · entomology. Run eri_data_model() print full, current vocabulary. Unknown values warn, error, new country/disease/source/measure normal gap, bug. golden rule: nothing reaches processed/ without eri_approve(), human gate. moves staged → processed, writes approval log, registers file catalog. Never hand-edit delete processed/ data.
code: data/ {country} / {disease} / {data_source} / {data_type} / {layer} / file         dr         malaria     surveillance    case          raw        as-received         uga        oncho       programmatic    treatment     staged     DQ-checked, awaiting sign-off         ht         lf          research        prevalence    processed  approved, the team trusts this

title: Data Analyst cheat sheet
text: Run eri_do() pick menu (CMR / surveillance ingest / ODK / onboard new space / review something staged) – picks right functions calls , order, don’t hold decision tree head (see eri_do() tour). table ’re scripting steps , deciding pipeline reach . general primitive pipeline (works data, incl. practice sandbox):
code: raw    <- eri_read(eri_data_path(c, d, src, mea, "raw", file), azcontainer = data_con) schema <- load_dq_schema(c, d, src, mea)            # bundled YAML under inst/schemas/ res    <- run_dq_checks(raw, schema); dq_report(res) eri_write(res$data, eri_data_path(c, d, src, mea, "staged", file), azcontainer = data_con) eri_approve(c, d, src, period, data_type = mea, azcontainer = data_con)   # the gate

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: filled monthly template lands projects blob, per-country, per-period folder. period six-digit YYYYMM: R eri_upload(), keeps whole monthly run one place one tool. (file already arrived via Azure Storage Explorer SharePoint, ’s fine ; can skip ahead staging.) Connect projects blob upload file path:
code: projects: health-rb-country-expansion-dev/raw/filled_templates/{country}/{period}/{file}.xlsx e.g.      …/raw/filled_templates/uga/202406/uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx # Connect to the projects blob explicitly (the same blob eri_stage_cmr reads from). projects_con <- get_azure_storage_connection(storage_name = "projects")  eri_upload(   "uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx",   "health-rb-country-expansion-dev/raw/filled_templates/uga/202406/uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx",   azcontainer = projects_con ) # (uploads the file; no console output)

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: eri_stage_cmr() pulls filed Excel(s) projects blob copies governed data blob’s staged/ layer, ready review. Give country period: Omit period stages recent one , says : (CMR data lives rblf disease folder, RB onchocerciasis, LF lymphatic filariasis, two programmes reports cover. registered RB-expansion countries, eth, nga, sdn, ssd, uga, mad, tcd, can staged.) Heads-rblf coordinate. CMR always uses disease = "rblf", combined RB + LF programme code, paths {country}/rblf/cmr/… approve eri_approve(country, "rblf", "cmr", period). Scaffolding new country’s CMR eri_onboard_cmr(create_dirs = TRUE) creates {country}/rblf/cmr/ folders. rblf/cmr per-disease. rblf/cmr interim coordinates: source ≠ measure model (ADR-0012), CMR format programmatic channel, eri_split_cmr() () routes sheet disease measure, e.g. RB Treatment → {country}/oncho/programmatic/treatment/. approve disease/measure, one combined rblf/cmr bucket.
code: eri_stage_cmr("uga", "202406") #> ✔ Staged: uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx #> ── ✔ Staged CMR to data blob ────────────────────────────── #> Files: 1 #> Location: uga/rblf/cmr/staged #> ℹ Operation log: uga/rblf/cmr/logs/20240705_141500_eri_stage_cmr_202406.yaml eri_stage_cmr("uga") #> ℹ No period specified; staging most recent: 202406 #> ✔ Staged: uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx #> …

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: Now offline heart job. First, see sheets country’s template : (real monthly file many sheets, treatments, MMDP, various trainings, surveys. bundled cmr-example.xlsx small two-sheet subset, RB Treatment + SCH Treatment, can run parse split offline.) read sheet eri_ingest_cmr(). reads row 5 (field codes), keeps #-coded columns, drops template’s blank spacer rows, , pass country, tags row . (use package’s bundled synthetic example ; real work ’d point staged file.) sheet programme, parsed way: column names field codes, stable identifiers can rely across every monthly file. moment eyeball numbers (treated vs target, missing districts) sign . codes, language. field codes (#rbtrt_…) language-neutral, eri_ingest_cmr() parses French template . French file, sheet named French (e.g. "Oncho Traitement"), pass canonical slug country, schema’s sheet_aliases resolves :
code: schema <- load_cmr_schema("uga") names(schema$sheets) #>  [1] "RB Treatment"                  "SCH Treatment" #>  [3] "LF MMDP"                       "VHT Training" #>  [5] "Parish Supervisors Training"   "Local Leaders Training" #>  [7] "Subcounty Supervisor Training" "MMDP (surgery) Training" #>  [9] "MMDP (patient) Training"       "Field Ento Training" #> [11] "Lab Training"                  "LF Surveys" #> [13] "RB Epi Surveys"                "RB Ento Surveys" report <- system.file("extdata", "cmr-example.xlsx", package = "erifunctions")  rb <- eri_ingest_cmr(report, sheet = "RB Treatment", country = "uga") #> ✔ CMR sheet "RB Treatment": 3 data rows, 6 field codes.  rb #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   country `#rbtrt_year` `#rbtrt_month` `#rbtrt_adm1` `#rbtrt_adm2` `#rbtrt_target` `#rbtrt_treated` #>   <chr>   <chr>         <chr>          <chr>         <chr>         <chr>           <chr> #> 1 uga     2024          06             Central       Kampala       12000           11400 #> 2 uga     2024          06             Western       Mbarara       8000            7600 #> 3 uga     2024          06             Eastern       Soroti        6000            5800 sch <- eri_ingest_cmr(report, sheet = "SCH Treatment", country = "uga") #> ✔ CMR sheet "SCH Treatment": 2 data rows, 6 field codes. eri_ingest_cmr("tcd_cmr_2024_06.xlsx", sheet = "rb_treatment", country = "tcd") #> ✔ CMR sheet "Oncho Traitement": 24 data rows, 6 field codes.

what: 4. Split it by disease and measure
title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: sheets per-programme, canonical store per-disease. eri_split_cmr() reads every routable sheet routes rows {country}/{disease}/programmatic/{measure}/staged/: disease sheet (RB Treatment → oncho, SCH Treatment → sch, LF MMDP → lf), measure category. reads Excel directly (bundled example; real work file uploaded), independently rblf/cmr/staged/ copy eri_stage_cmr() made, copy raw archive; parse + route. Preview routing first dry_run: sheet schema routes workbook doesn’t contain skipped warning, expected , since example two-sheet subset. (none routable sheets present, eri_split_cmr() errors instead silently nothing.) sheet, row? sheet carries per-row #…_disease field whose values programme-coverage codes (RB / RBLF / RBLFSCH, programmes run location), single disease. eri_split_cmr() keeps field data column takes routing disease sheet, treatment numbers never duplicated across diseases. Cross-programme Training sheets, serve programmes , route together combined rblf disease. dry run comes back nothing skipped warnings, ’ll see plain “Dry run clean – ready run real” instead infer absence complaints. flag something (skipped sheet, real template defect like duplicate field code), ’s also logged stable reference attach note later, ’ve looked fixed whatever needed fixing: stage real, one parquet per sheet, disease/measure folder. country’s raw file still also needs reach legacy contractor pipeline Phase-3 parallel run, mirror_pipeline uploads call – one step instead separate manual upload: Keep plan around next two steps. Lost , ran split earlier session? Recover without rerunning anything (routing table persisted op-log every real run writes): Re-splitting corrected file. Fixed something upstream re-splitting _fixed.xlsx copy period already split? default eri_split_cmr() warns now-superseded prior staged file(s) – never deletes anything without opting , since one place package can remove previously-staged data: Leave FALSE (default) ’d rather review warning remove old file hand.
code: eri_split_cmr(report, "uga", dry_run = TRUE) #> ✔ CMR sheet "RB Treatment": 3 data rows, 6 field codes. #> ✔ CMR sheet "SCH Treatment": 2 data rows, 6 field codes. #> ℹ Dry run -- no data written. Routing plan: #>   "RB Treatment"  -> uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/staged/cmr-example_rb_treatment.parquet (3 rows) #>   "SCH Treatment" -> uga/sch/programmatic/treatment/staged/cmr-example_sch_treatment.parquet (2 rows) #> Warning: Sheet "LF MMDP" not found in cmr-example.xlsx; skipping.   # ...and the #> Warning: Sheet "VHT Training" not found in cmr-example.xlsx; skipping.  # other #> # ... (the example has only the two treatment sheets; a real file routes them all) # ... after investigating and correcting something upstream ... eri_logs_resolve("uga/rblf/cmr/logs/20260710_150903_eri_split_cmr_dryrun.yaml",                  note = "confirmed the skipped training sheets aren't part of this country's report") plan <- eri_split_cmr(report, "uga")   # add mirror_pipeline = "rb-expansion" if this country needs it #> ✔ "RB Treatment"  -> uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/staged/cmr-example_rb_treatment.parquet #> ✔ "SCH Treatment" -> uga/sch/programmatic/treatment/staged/cmr-example_sch_treatment.parquet #> ── ✔ Split CMR by disease/measure ─────────────────────── #> Sheets: 2 routed #> Diseases: oncho, sch plan <- eri_cmr_last_plan("uga", "202406") eri_split_cmr(fixed_report, "uga", supersede_staged = TRUE) #> ✔ Superseded a prior staged file for this period: cmr-example_rb_treatment.parquet

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: Prefer interactively? Sections 5 6 scriptable core – eri_dq_review() walks check → fix → re-check → approve loop one guided, menu-driven session (fix value workbook, adjust schema, mark flag important/noted, force-approve typed confirmation, submit schema fix maintainer fold – one prompt): eri_dq_review("uga", "202406"). ’s built entirely functions , everything still applies – interactive-(refuses run script CI, functions use directly), holds state : close mid-review running picks exactly logs say things . See DQ review guide full worked session. CMR routing auto-run DQ checks – CMR review manual, purpose. eri_cmr_dq_report() runs logs DQ checks every measure plan routed, one call, gives back one combined tibble – every flag every measure – instead twelve separate dq_report() printouts: Work issue issue, measure measure: mark flag "not_important", "fixed", "noted", note explaining decided: eri_dq_flag_resolve() updates logged flag, run’s -memory flags tibble – update copy way eri_dq_review() tracks triage loop iterations, anything print export reflects decision: every flag measure’s report triaged, close measure’s whole log entry – actually unblocks eri_approve_cmr() . Skip note auto-summarizes per-flag decisions just made instead leaving entry’s note blank: ’d rather scan logged state across team’s whole backlog (just run’s -memory tibble), eri_logs() still works way, one row per measure: Handing back country pasting Teams thread? eri_dq_export() renders flags (whatever status/note triage ’ve done ) self-contained HTML markdown file, organised sheet – see QC/feedback guide general pattern. eri_dq_review()’s “Print report” menu option calls automatically – see DQ review guide whole session end end.
code: flags <- eri_cmr_dq_report("uga", "202406") #> ✔ Logged 1 DQ flag (needs_review). #> ✔ Logged 0 DQ flags (clean). flags #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   sheet        disease data_type log_path      flag_id        row column value issue         status note #>   <chr>        <chr>   <chr>     <chr>         <chr>        <int> <chr>  <chr> <chr>         <chr>  <chr> #> 1 RB Treatment oncho   treatment uga/oncho/... uga/oncho...     4 target 0     out of range  open   <NA> eri_dq_flag_resolve(flags$flag_id[1], "not_important",                    note = "confirmed with the country: target is genuinely 0 this period") flags$status[1] <- "not_important" flags$note[1]   <- "confirmed with the country: target is genuinely 0 this period" eri_logs_resolve(unique(flags$log_path)[1]) #> ✔ Marked ...yaml handled. # note auto-filled as "1 not important" from the per-flag triage above eri_logs("uga", data_source = "programmatic", operation = "dq_flags", since = "2026-07-01") #> # A tibble: 2 × 15 #>   log_path       timestamp  operation status       disease data_type n_issues … #>   <chr>          <chr>      <chr>     <chr>        <chr>   <chr>     <int> #> 1 uga/oncho/...  2026-07-...dq_flags  needs_review oncho   treatment 1 #> 2 uga/sch/...    2026-07-...dq_flags  clean        sch     treatment 0 eri_dq_export(flags, country = "uga", period = "202406") #> ✔ DQ report (1 flag · 0 open) written to '.../dq-report-uga-202406-2026-07-11.html'.

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: One CMR workbook fans several diseases/measures; eri_approve_cmr() approves one call every one clean – reviewed via DQ-check step , outstanding flags: anything’s still outstanding (measure never DQ-checked, unresolved flags), nothing approved – get task list back instead: disease’s data now canonical discoverable catalog coordinates. (can still approve one measure time plain eri_approve() – eri_approve_cmr() convenience wrapper around exactly , looped, DQ gate front.) eri_approve_cmr() also writes log entry recording exactly dq_flags log(s) verified clean approval (dq_reviewed) – audit trail “data now processed” back “’s every flag raised, decided one” traceable via eri_logs(), just bare approval stamp.
code: eri_approve_cmr("uga", "202406") #> ✔ Approved 2 measures for "uga" / "202406". eri_approve_cmr("uga", "202406") #> x 1 measure still needs attention -- approving nothing. #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   disease data_type log_path                                     issue #>   <chr>   <chr>     <chr>                                        <chr> #> 1 oncho   treatment uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/logs/x.yaml 1 unresolved DQ flag(s)  # Review the flag, then close it out with a note explaining what you did/decided: eri_logs_resolve("uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/logs/x.yaml",                  note = "confirmed with the country: target was genuinely 0 this period") eri_approve_cmr("uga", "202406")   # re-run -- now clean, approves everything

what: The rare escape hatch: force-approving anyway
title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: Every often something outstanding reason never resolve cleanly – known template quirk confirmed country, say – data genuinely needs go . Pass force = TRUE justification explaining : silently resolve bypassed flag – ’s annotated (handled, marked forced) open backlog stays clean without pretending ever actually reviewed, approval’s log records justification exactly bypassed. Reach eri_logs_resolve() first; force-approving flag never close, shortcut around reviewing . ’s monthly loop: upload → stage → split → DQ-check → approve.
code: eri_approve_cmr("uga", "202406", force = TRUE,                 justification = "Known template quirk in RB Treatment; confirmed with country lead.") #> x FORCE-APPROVING "uga" / "202406" despite 1 outstanding measure. #> i Justification: Known template quirk in RB Treatment; confirmed with country lead. #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   disease data_type log_path                                     issue #>   <chr>   <chr>     <chr>                                        <chr> #> 1 oncho   treatment uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/logs/x.yaml 1 unresolved DQ flag(s) #> ✔ Marked 'x.yaml' handled (bypassed by a forced approval). #> ✔ Force-approved 2 measures for "uga" / "202406". eri_catalog_query(country = "uga", data_source = "programmatic") #> # A tibble: 2 × 13 #>   path                              country disease data_source  data_type layer … #>   <chr>                             <chr>   <chr>   <chr>        <chr>     <chr> #> 1 uga/oncho/programmatic/treatmen…  uga     oncho   programmatic treatment proce… #> 2 uga/sch/programmatic/treatment/…  uga     sch     programmatic treatment proce…

(e.g. sdn; blank to cancel): atlantis ── Which disease?  1: malaria 2: oncho 3: lf 4: sch 5: sth 6: Other (type it) ── How did this data arrive?  1: surveillance 2: programmatic 3: research 4: Other (type it) ── What does each row count?  1: case 2: aggregate 3: treatment 4: mmdp 5: training 6: survey 7: tas 8: prevalence 9: entomology 10: Other (type it) ℹ Where is the file on your computer? ── Ready to bring this in  • Country: ATLANTIS • Disease: malaria • Channel: surveillance / Measure: case • File: atlantis_malaria_2024-01.csv ℹ I'll archive it, check data quality, and stage it for approval.  ── Go ahead?  1: Yes 2: No ! 1 log entry need review before this can be approved. ℹ Run `eri_logs()` to see them, `eri_dq_flag_resolve()` to triage each flag, then `eri_logs_resolve()` to close the entry out.  ── Approve anyway? (only if you've already reviewed this)  1: Yes 2: No ℹ Left staged -- run `eri_do()` again once you've triaged the flags.

what: 1. Build a practice submission with two real problems
title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)
text: bundled cmr-example.xlsx (used throughout CMR guide) clean data, ’s nothing DQ check catch. something actually review, deliberately introduce two realistic problems copy – kind thing real submission might contain: impossible value, locality schema doesn’t recognise yet. also drop SCH Treatment sheet: atlantis ships bundled DQ schema oncho/treatment, eri_approve_cmr() blocks routed measure ’s never DQ-checked (schema-less measure never gets one) – rule applies real, multi-sheet CMR, every routed measure needs schema whole workbook can approved.
code: library(openxlsx2)  wb <- wb_load(system.file("extdata", "cmr-example.xlsx", package = "erifunctions")) wb$remove_worksheet("SCH Treatment") wb <- wb_add_data(wb, sheet = "RB Treatment", x = -50,                   start_col = 6, start_row = 6, col_names = FALSE)             # Kampala's treated count wb <- wb_add_data(wb, sheet = "RB Treatment", x = "Atlantis City",                   start_col = 4, start_row = 8, col_names = FALSE)             # Soroti's district cell path <- file.path(tempdir(), "202608_atlantis_demo.xlsx") wb_save(wb, path, overwrite = TRUE)

what: 2. Get a workbook with something to review
title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)
text: eri_split_cmr() routes workbook atlantis’s oncho/programmatic/treatment measure; eri_cmr_dq_report() checks measure’s schema combines every flag one tibble – covered depth CMR guide. Two flags, one measure. Now forget tibble exists – , one function.
code: plan  <- eri_split_cmr(path, "atlantis", period = "202608") flags <- eri_cmr_dq_report("atlantis", "202608", plan = plan) #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 0 corrections, 2 flags for review. #> ✔ Logged 2 DQ flags (needs_review). flags[, c("excel_row", "column", "value", "issue", "status")] #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   excel_row column   value         issue                                  status #>       <int> <chr>    <chr>         <chr>                                  <chr> #> 1         6 treated  -50           Value outside expected range [0, 1000… open #> 2         8 district Atlantis City Value not in allowed_values list       open

title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)

Run live R console RStudio session. real session workbook built , one menu choice time – menu prompts come straight function; informational success messages real, captured output. console busier transcripts . DQ check also prints “renamed column” “corrections/flags” lines, ’ll likely see ! load schema Azure (Found...). Falling back local. – expected , since atlantis’s DQ schema ships inside package never uploaded Azure. Nothing broken; transcripts trimmed changes step, full console dump.
code: eri_dq_review("atlantis", "202608")

title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)
text: Two open flags need two different responses, one – ’s whole reason work one time rather bulk-resolving.
code: ── DQ review: atlantis / 202608 ──────────────────────────────────────────────── ✖ 1 of 1 sheet have open flags (2 flags total)  ── RB Treatment (oncho/treatment) -- 2 open ──   excel_row   column         value                                      issue          6  treated           -50 Value outside expected range [0, 10000000]          8 district Atlantis City           Value not in allowed_values list  ── 2 open flag(s) across the workbook. What do you want to do?  1: Work through the open flags one at a time 2: Re-run the DQ check 3: Force-approve anyway 4: Print report 5: Exit

what: Flag 1: a real error -> fix in source
title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)
text: treated = -50 impossible – genuine data-entry mistake, judgement call. Choosing “Work open flags”, “Fix source” flag: first time fix anything session, eri_dq_review() forks _fixed working copy touching – file submitted never edited place. Open copy, correct value, save . (Picking back fresh R session? Point _fixed file, original – detects _fixed sibling reuses rather forking second one.) RStudio, working copy opens directly editor instead printing “open file” message – eri_dq_review() uses rstudioapi ’s available.
code: ── Flag 1/2: RB Treatment row 6 treated: "-50" -- Value outside expected range [0, 10000000] ── What do you want to do with this flag?  1: Fix in source (open/copy the workbook) 2: Adjust the schema (alias, allowed value, range...) 3: Mark not important 4: Mark noted 5: Skip to the next flag  Path to the local source workbook for this period (or a '_fixed' copy you've already started, if you have one): <you type the path here> ✔ Made a working copy: '202608_atlantis_demo_fixed.xlsx' (original preserved) ℹ Fix treated on Excel row 6 in the "RB Treatment" sheet. ℹ Issue: Value outside expected range [0, 10000000] ℹ Open this file to review/edit: '.../202608_atlantis_demo_fixed.xlsx' ℹ When you're done with this and any other fixes, choose "Re-run the DQ check" from the main menu -- it re-splits '202608_atlantis_demo_fixed.xlsx' and re-checks.

what: Flag 2: not wrong, just not catalogued yet -> defer it
title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)
text: "Atlantis City" isn’t typo – ’s real locality schema’s allowed_values list hasn’t caught yet. ’s nothing fix. Choose “Skip next flag” now (’ll come back actual error way):
code: ── Flag 2/2: RB Treatment row 8 district: "Atlantis City" -- Value not in allowed_values list

what: Re-run: only what changed gets re-checked
title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)
text: Back main menu (still 2 open – fixing workbook doesn’t retroactively change logged flags), choose “Re-run DQ check”: treated flag gone – underlying value fixed, never comes back. "Atlantis City" flag reappears exactly , ’s genuinely still . workbook routes several measures, ones actually re-split get re-checked; every measure’s -session decisions left alone (see CMR guide worked multi-measure example).

title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)
text: “clean” means every flag triaged, data now flawless – one flag just noted rather open. What next?  1: Approve 2: Print report 3: Exit ── RB Treatment  excel_row   column         value                            issue status          8 district Atlantis City Value not in allowed_values list  noted                                                                                                                                                            note  Confirmed with the country lead: Atlantis City is a real, if not-yet-catalogued, locality -- flagging for the schema maintainer to add it to the allowed list.  ✔ DQ report (1 flag · 0 open) written to '.../dq-report-atlantis-202608-2026-07-12.html'.

title: Triaging and handing back DQ flags, interactively (for data analysts)
text: Everything lives atlantis’s namespace, ’s safe leave – following along want tidy sandbox behind , three things created: Azure-side staged/log/processed files, local practice workbook (path) _fixed working copy (forked Flag 1), exported HTML report (eri_dq_export() writes working directory default, temp folder – see QC/feedback guide – won’t clean ): Real submissions disposable like practice run – never delete real country’s staged, processed, log files way.
code: con <- get_azure_storage_connection(storage_name = Sys.getenv("ERIFUNCTIONS_DATA_STORAGE_NAME", "data")) eri_catalog_remove(   "atlantis/oncho/programmatic/treatment/processed/202608_atlantis_demo_fixed_rb_treatment.parquet",   data_con = con ) all_files <- AzureStor::list_storage_files(con, "atlantis", recursive = TRUE) mine      <- all_files$name[!all_files$isdir & grepl("202608", all_files$name)] for (f in mine) AzureStor::delete_storage_file(con, f, confirm = FALSE)  unlink(path)                                                    # the original practice workbook unlink(sub("\\.xlsx$", "_fixed.xlsx", path))                     # its "fix in source" working copy unlink(list.files(getwd(), pattern = "^dq-report-atlantis-202608-.*\\.html$", full.names = TRUE))

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: Data moves three layers, nothing becomes official without sign-. Every dataset travels raw/ → staged/ → processed/. raw/ file exactly arrived. staged/ cleaned, quality-checked version awaiting review. processed/ canonical data whole team builds , file gets call eri_approve(). approval human gate.

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: need two things, : R RStudio installed laptop. package, installed Carter Center repository: Azure access needs configuration. first time command needs Azure, browser opens sign Carter Center account; sign-remembered rest session. (keys, environment variables, see ?get_azure_storage_connection.) erifunctions narrates . Load leave verbosity default "full" walkthrough, step--step output shown blocks :
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions") library(erifunctions)  # The outputs shown in this guide are at the default "full" verbosity. # Once you know the steps, eri_verbosity("quiet") trims them to headline # results, warnings, and errors only.

what: 1. Connect to the data blob and name your sandbox
title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: surveillance pipeline lives data blob. reading writing functions (eri_read(), eri_write(), eri_upload()) talk default connection, make one pointed data pass explicitly everywhere , way every step targets right place: Every dataset identified five coordinates (ADR-0012): country, disease, data_source (channel, data arrives), data_type (measure, captures), layer. key idea channel separate measure: surveillance data can case line-list one country aggregate weekly count another. extract line-list, one row per case, measure case. Never hand-type folder paths, let eri_data_path() build canonical path , every step agrees things go: data_source data_type extensible, eri_data_model() lists known values, unregistered one warns rather blocks, layer one "raw", "staged", "processed". Country disease free text, exactly can stand atlantis sandbox next real countries without disturbing . Create two folders write (third, processed/, created us approval):
code: data_con <- get_azure_storage_connection(storage_name = "data") # Our make-believe country and its malaria surveillance data. country     <- "atlantis" disease     <- "malaria" data_source <- "surveillance"   # the channel data_type   <- "case"           # the measure (one row per case)  eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "raw") #> [1] "atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/raw"  eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "staged") #> [1] "atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/staged" eri_dir_create(eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "raw"),    azcontainer = data_con) eri_dir_create(eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "staged"), azcontainer = data_con) #> ✔ Directory created!

what: 2. Simulate an as-received extract and store it in raw/
title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: real life surveillance officer emails spreadsheet. make one , tiny line-list malaria cases, one row per case. Notice deliberately little messy, way real data always : sex values Spanish (Masculino/Femenino/M), couple district names mis-cased use old spelling (coralia, Nerei City): first thing file preserve untouched, always record actually arrived. Write raw/: raw/ archive source. work toward staged/, original safe ever need start .
code: extract1 <- data.frame(   Year     = rep(2024, 10),   EpiWeek  = 1:10,   District = c("Coralia", "Nerei", "coralia", "Tritonis", "Marisa",                "Nerei City", "Coralia", "Nerei", "Tritonis", "Marisa"),   Sex      = c("Male", "Female", "Masculino", "Femenino", "M",                "Female", "Male", "Female", "Masculino", "Male"),   Age      = c(34, 27, 5, 60, 41, 19, 73, 8, 52, 30),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) head(extract1) #>   Year EpiWeek   District       Sex Age #> 1 2024       1    Coralia      Male  34 #> 2 2024       2      Nerei    Female  27 #> 3 2024       3    coralia Masculino   5 #> 4 2024       4   Tritonis  Femenino  60 #> 5 2024       5     Marisa         M  41 #> 6 2024       6 Nerei City    Female  19 raw_path1 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "raw",                            "atlantis_malaria_2024-01.csv") eri_write(extract1, raw_path1, azcontainer = data_con)

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: erifunctions know Masculino become Male, Age 250? tell , , DQ schema, small YAML file describing column: type, allowed range allowed values, known fixes, translations. schema contract every future extract dataset checked . Open text editor save following atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml working directory, filename four-part identity {country}_{disease}_{data_source}_{data_type}, load_dq_schema() finds . (intentionally short, real schema like dr_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml columns, shape exactly .) One source, many measures. stood space onboarding guide, scaffold defaults aggregate measure (weekly counts), atlantis_malaria_surveillance_aggregate.yaml. extract line-list, author case measure alongside . whole point separating channel measure: surveillance source can hold case aggregate data. Run eri_data_model() time see known measures. Read slowly, block rule: distinction matters: corrections translations applied recorded; flags problems can resolve. Now publish schema Azure load_dq_schema() can find . looks schemas/{country}_{disease}_{data_source}_{data_type}.yaml, filename matters:
code: country: atlantis disease: malaria data_source: surveillance data_type: case language: en time_grain: case  temporal:   year_col: Year   period_col: EpiWeek  preprocessing:   - remove_smart_quotes   - drop_rows_missing_year  columns:   Year:     required: true     type: numeric     aliases: [year, YEAR, Año]     range: [2000, 2035]    EpiWeek:     required: true     type: numeric     aliases: [week, Week, Semana]     range: [1, 53]    District:     required: true     type: categorical     aliases: [district, Distrito]     allowed_values: [Coralia, Nerei, Tritonis, Marisa]     corrections:       coralia: Coralia       CORALIA: Coralia       "Nerei City": Nerei    Sex:     required: true     type: categorical     aliases: [sex, SEX, Sexo]     allowed_values: [Male, Female]     translations:       Masculino: Male       Femenino: Female       M: Male       F: Female    Age:     required: true     type: numeric     aliases: [age, AGE, Edad]     range: [0, 120] eri_upload("atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml",            "schemas/atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml", azcontainer = data_con)

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: Load raw file back raw/, load schema, run checks. run_dq_checks() applies every rule schema hands back cleaned data plus two ledgers, fixed, wants look : Print result full report: Six things fixed automatically nothing needs review, clean extract. can always see exactly changed $log ledger: cleaned data lives result1$data, Spanish sexes mapped Male/Female, district names canonicalised. stage.
code: raw1    <- eri_read(raw_path1, azcontainer = data_con) schema  <- load_dq_schema("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", "case", azcontainer = data_con)  result1 <- run_dq_checks(raw1, schema) #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 6 corrections, 0 flags for review. result1 #> ── Data Quality Report ────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Shape: 10 rows x 5 columns #> #> ── Automated Corrections (6 total) ── #> • "Sex": 4 corrections (translation) #> • "District": 2 corrections (correction) #> #> ── Flags Requiring Review (0 total) ── #> ✔ No flags. result1$log #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>     row column   original_value corrected_value rule        action #>   <int> <chr>    <chr>          <chr>           <chr>       <chr> #> 1     3 Sex      Masculino      Male            translation corrected #> 2     9 Sex      Masculino      Male            translation corrected #> 3     4 Sex      Femenino       Female          translation corrected #> 4     5 Sex      M              Male            translation corrected #> 5     3 District coralia        Coralia         correction  corrected #> 6     6 District Nerei City     Nerei           correction  corrected

what: 5. Write the cleaned data to staged/
title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: staged/ holds DQ-checked data awaiting approval. store parquet (compact, typed, fast). Give file name contains reporting period, use period approve next step: Nothing official yet. data checked parked, appear canonical sign .
code: staged_path1 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "staged",                               "atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet") eri_write(result1$data, staged_path1, azcontainer = data_con)

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: gate. eri_approve() finds every staged file whose name contains period give , moves processed/, writes human-readable approval log (approved , ), registers file data catalog teammates can discover : Passing data_type records measure catalog entry. (Omit eri_approve() files data channel level data_type = NA, says , fine channel-data, line-list case.) catalog team’s index canonical data. Confirm dataset now : read canonical file straight back, exactly teammate : whole loop: raw → DQ → staged → approved.
code: eri_approve(country, disease, data_source, "2024-01", data_type = data_type, azcontainer = data_con) #> ✔ Catalog: registered atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet. #> ✔ Approved: atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet #> ✔ Approval log: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed/2024-01_approval_log.yaml #> ── ✔ Approved "2024-01" ───────────────────────────────── #> Dataset: atlantis / malaria / surveillance / case #> Files: 1 moved to processed #> Approver: your.name #> Location: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed #> ℹ Operation log: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/logs/20240115_142233_eri_approve_2024-01.yaml eri_catalog_query(country = "atlantis", data_con = data_con) #> # A tibble: 1 × 13 #>   path                        country disease data_source data_type layer  period  … #>   <chr>                       <chr>   <chr>   <chr>       <chr>     <chr>  <chr> #> 1 atlantis/malaria/surveill…  atlantis malaria surveillance case      proce… 2024-01 processed_path1 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "processed",                                  "atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet") official <- eri_read(processed_path1, azcontainer = data_con) nrow(official)   # 10 approved cases

what: 7. A second extract arrives, this time with errors
title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: next reporting file lands, real data rarely clean. one two genuine problems analyst must judge: Age 250 (impossible), district Atlantica one four (typo, new district schema know yet): Archive raw/, run checks: time two flags. schema fixed safely , guess age 250 unknown district, call. Look exactly rows: investigate (call surveillance officer, check source form) decide: age data-entry slip 25, Atlantica really Marisa. Fix cleaned data, re-run checks confirm clean bill health: just edit schema? Atlantica real new district, right fix add allowed_values re-upload schema, every future extract accepts . Use correction/translation genuine fixes; use flag--edit one-data errors like impossible age. Now stage approve corrected second period, exactly : now two approved periods catalog atlantis, approval log recording signed . Going : cross-period anomalies. Range allowed-value checks catch bad cells. catch suspicious trend, district whose case count suddenly triples, see add_anomaly_pct_change(), can chain onto run_dq_checks() result several periods stacked together.
code: extract2 <- data.frame(   Year     = rep(2024, 10),   EpiWeek  = 11:20,   District = c("Coralia", "Atlantica", "Nerei", "Tritonis", "Marisa",                "coralia", "Nerei", "Coralia", "Tritonis", "Marisa"),   Sex      = c("Male", "Female", "Masculino", "Female", "M",                "Female", "Male", "Femenino", "Male", "Female"),   Age      = c(34, 27, 250, 60, 41, 19, 73, 8, 52, 30),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) raw_path2 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "raw",                            "atlantis_malaria_2024-02.csv") eri_write(extract2, raw_path2, azcontainer = data_con)  raw2    <- eri_read(raw_path2, azcontainer = data_con) result2 <- run_dq_checks(raw2, schema) #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 4 corrections, 2 flags for review.  result2 #> ── Data Quality Report ────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Shape: 10 rows x 5 columns #> #> ── Automated Corrections (4 total) ── #> • "Sex": 3 corrections (translation) #> • "District": 1 correction (correction) #> #> ── Flags Requiring Review (2 total) ── #> ! Value not in allowed_values list: 1 row [District] (e.g. Atlantica (row 2)) #> ! Value outside expected range [0, 120]: 1 row [Age] (e.g. 250 (row 3)) #> #> ℹ See `result$flags` for the full row-level detail. result2$flags #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>     row column   value     issue #>   <int> <chr>    <chr>     <chr> #> 1     3 Age      250       Value outside expected range [0, 120] #> 2     2 District Atlantica Value not in allowed_values list fixed <- result2$data fixed$Age[3]      <- 25 fixed$District[2] <- "Marisa"  result2b <- run_dq_checks(fixed, schema) #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 0 corrections, 0 flags for review. staged_path2 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "staged",                               "atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet") eri_write(result2b$data, staged_path2, azcontainer = data_con)  eri_approve(country, disease, data_source, "2024-02", data_type = data_type, azcontainer = data_con) #> ✔ Catalog: registered atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet. #> ✔ Approved: atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet #> ── ✔ Approved "2024-02" ───────────────────────────────── #> Dataset: atlantis / malaria / surveillance / case #> Files: 1 moved to processed #> Approver: your.name #> Location: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: ran practice, remove everything created leave trace. deletes whole atlantis sandbox (three layers logs), schema, catalog entries: Confirm nothing remains, delete local files: delete freely : Atlantis invented data fake. Real Carter Center surveillance data different, approved country data must never deleted casually, copied Azure, committed git. discipline guide builds, raw kept untouched, DQ staging, human approval gate anything becomes canonical, exactly keeps protected data trustworthy. doubt, ask delete.
code: # Delete the entire sandbox namespace (raw + staged + processed + logs). # eri_dir_delete() removes a non-empty directory recursively in one call; using # the erifunctions helpers (not raw AzureStor) keeps every step in the package. eri_dir_delete("atlantis", azcontainer = data_con)  # Delete the schema you published. eri_delete("schemas/atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml", azcontainer = data_con)  # Remove the two catalog entries (the catalog is shared, so tidy up after yourself). eri_catalog_remove("atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed/atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet",                    data_con = data_con) eri_catalog_remove("atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed/atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet",                    data_con = data_con) #> ✔ Catalog: removed atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet. #> ✔ Catalog: removed atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet. eri_catalog_query(country = "atlantis", data_con = data_con) #> No catalog entries match the specified filters.  unlink("atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml")   # the local schema file

text: now done full DA ingest loop: store raw, quality-check schema, fix human can, stage, approve canonical layer, twice, including messy extract. One call, eri_ingest(), archive-DQ--stage steps (§2, §4, §5) single shot, data including sandbox just built (takes country / disease / data_source / data_type using). archives file hand raw/ timestamped name anything else, records MD5 hash source file operation log (later staged/processed copy can traced back exact bytes reviewed), runs DQ checks stages result. step hand see every layer; comfortable, eri_ingest() fast path, eri_approve(): sign-, always human gate end. (legacy hsp-mal cutover, passing mirror_pipeline = "hsp-mal" also mirrors output old projects blob, default.) one planned set short, task-oriented guides, one common job analyst epidemiologist . See guide index exists today coming, reference full menu functions grouped purpose. Data Analyst path, step 3 4. ← Onboarding new program · Next: Triaging error & DQ log backlog →

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Nothing fails silently, nothing stays open accident. Operations write structured logs go; data-quality flags persisted eri_dq_log() (eri_ingest() ). eri_logs() turns pile triage list, eri_logs_resolve() marks item handled, handled , drops open backlog without ever erasing record.

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Install package sign Azure (zero-config browser; see connections guide).
code: library(erifunctions)

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Two kinds log end {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/[{data_type}/]logs/ (measure level present data approved one, eri_logs() reads layouts): Operation logs: written automatically eri_ingest(), eri_approve(), eri_stage(), eri_odk_sync(), friends. records operation, ran , parameters, , failed, error. Data-quality logs: $flags run_dq_checks() R session persist . eri_dq_log() writes place (eri_ingest() calls automatically), data-quality issue someone hit last month still discoverable today. Let’s make one sandbox.

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Picking ingest guide, run_dq_checks() result couple flags. Persist :
code: # result <- run_dq_checks(raw, schema)   # from the ingest guide # Pass the same measure you approve with (here "case") so the DQ log co-locates # with the approval log under the measure level. eri_dq_log(result, "atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", data_type = "case", period = "2024-01") #> ℹ Operation log: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/logs/20260626_093000_dq_flags_2024-01.yaml #> ✔ Logged 2 DQ flags (needs_review).

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Now common slip: try approve period file staged. errors, leaves error log behind:
code: eri_approve("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", "2024-02", data_type = "case") #> ℹ Operation log: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/logs/20260626_100000_eri_approve_2024-02.yaml #> Error: No staged files found matching "2024-02" in atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/staged.

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: eri_logs() reads logs hands triage list, newest first. Scope dataset (fast), call bare system-wide view: (tibble also carries log_path, n_issues, handled* columns, shown trimmed .) Filter just failures: status = "needs_review" narrows data-quality items instead; operation =, analyst =, since = filter rest. tibble also carries data_source (channel) data_type (measure, data approved one). Leaving country/disease/data_source/data_type scans whole data blob system-wide backlog (scoping faster).
code: eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance") #> 2 logs (2 needing attention). #> # A tibble: 2 × 15 #>   timestamp            operation   status       period  summary           analyst … #>   <chr>                <chr>       <chr>        <chr>   <chr>             <chr> #> 1 2026-06-26T10:00:00Z eri_approve error        2024-02 No staged files…  da.one #> 2 2026-06-26T09:30:00Z dq_flags    needs_review 2024-01 2 flags           da.one eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", status = "error") #> 1 log (1 needing attention). #> # A tibble: 1 × 15 #>   operation   status period  summary #>   <chr>       <chr>  <chr>   <chr> #> 1 eri_approve error  2024-02 No staged files found matching '2024-02'.

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: investigate failure, stage file, re-approve successfully. Now close original error leaves open backlog, note whoever reads next: Re-list, error gone open backlog, data-quality item remains: Resolving never deletes log, adds triage record. Pass include_handled = TRUE see closed item, handled note:
code: backlog <- eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", status = "error")  eri_logs_resolve(   backlog$log_path[1],   note = "Staged the file, then re-approved 2024-02." ) #> ✔ Marked 20260626_100000_eri_approve_2024-02.yaml handled. eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance") #> 1 log (1 needing attention). #> # A tibble: 1 × 15 #>   operation status       period  summary #>   <chr>     <chr>        <chr>   <chr> #> 1 dq_flags  needs_review 2024-01 2 flags eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance",          status = "error", include_handled = TRUE)[, c("operation", "handled", "handled_by", "handled_at")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   operation   handled handled_by handled_at #>   <chr>       <lgl>   <chr>      <chr> #> 1 eri_approve TRUE    da.one     2026-06-26T10:15:00Z

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: point shared backlog. logs live Azure, anyone’s laptop, analyst away something needs attention, colleague can run one command see whole picture: see failures, error messages, , anything already handled, dealt note left. “going DR upload last week?” guesswork.
code: # Anyone on the team, across every dataset: eri_logs(status = "error")

what: 5. Reconstruct the full trail with eri_audit()
title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: eri_logs() gives one row per log file, newest first, right shape “needs attention.” different question, “actually happened dataset, order, signed ,” needs one row per event instead — flag’s resolution event actor timestamp, nested inside DQ log, triage close-separate event . eri_audit() reconstructs. 2024-01 data-quality flags §1 still just sitting , unresolved — let’s actually finish one trail something worth showing: fix one flag, note , approve: Now full trail: Oldest first, deliberately opposite order eri_logs() — timeline reads forward. result still tibble (log_path stays every row need drill raw YAML), can filter join like data; cli rendering just prints. eri_approve_cmr() records exactly DQ reviews backed CMR approval (dq_reviewed field); eri_audit() cashes — leave disease/data_source/data_type unscoped CMR workbook trail spans split/approve coordinate every fanned-measure automatically, CMR-specific audit function needed. forced approval (eri_approve_cmr(..., force = TRUE), rare case something outstanding must promoted anyway — see CMR guide) bypass annotation leaves measure overrode render red [FORCED] prefix , rather folding ordinary approval — one part trail genuine review.
code: eri_dq_flag_resolve("atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/logs/20260626_093000_dq_flags_2024-01.yaml::1",                      "fixed", note = "corrected typo in source") eri_dq_flag_resolve("atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/logs/20260626_093000_dq_flags_2024-01.yaml::2",                      "not_important", note = "known template quirk") eri_approve("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", "2024-01", data_type = "case") #> ✔ Approved: 2024-01_atlantis_malaria.parquet eri_audit("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", data_type = "case", period = "2024-01") #> 4 events across 2 logs. #> ── Audit trail ────────────────────────────────────────── #> atlantis / malaria / surveillance / case / period 2024-01 #> #> • 2026-06-26T09:30:00Z -- dq_flags (da.one): 2 flags (schema: bundled) #> • 2026-06-26T09:45:00Z -- flag_resolved (da.one): flag #1 -> fixed (corrected typo in source) #> • 2026-06-26T09:46:00Z -- flag_resolved (da.one): flag #2 -> not_important (known template quirk) #> • 2026-06-26T11:00:00Z -- eri_approve (da.one): 2024-01_atlantis_malaria.parquet

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: ODK Central front door; erifunctions brings data one governed pipeline. register form (registry team’s record forms tracked), sync submissions {country}/{disease}/research/raw/, flows raw → staged → processed lifecycle, eri_approve() human gate, every dataset. ODK lands. source ≠ measure model (ADR-0012), ODK research channel’s collection format (format: odk), eri_odk_sync() writes research source. measure (tas, prevalence, …) optional assigned later, clean form final dataset, paths carry measure level yet.

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: R RStudio installed. package: ODK Central credentials. Store .Renviron never end scripts. Open usethis::edit_r_environ(), add three lines, save restart R: Everything reads automatically, never type password script. Azure access zero-config: first command needs opens browser sign (see ?get_azure_storage_connection). One comfort setting, leave verbosity default "full" walkthrough (step--step output shown ):
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions") ODK_URL=https://your-odk-server.org/ ODK_USER=you@example.org ODK_PASS=your-password library(erifunctions) # eri_verbosity("quiet")   # later, to trim to headlines + warnings only

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Field staff collect app users, per-project data-collection accounts. can create one assign form straight R. (calls change live project; remove demo user Clean .) Check form’s assignments, new collector listed: row pairs actorId (app user) roleId holds form, new collector (actorId 250, roleId 2 = ODK’s App User role) now listed alongside anyone already assigned.
code: # Create an app user (a data collector) in the project. new_user <- update_odk_app_user_role(   action     = "create",   con        = con,   project_id = project_id,   actor_name = "Demo Collector" ) new_user #> $actor_name #> [1] "Demo Collector" #> $actor_id #> [1] 250 #> $project_id #> [1] 11 # Give that user access to our form. role_id 2 is ODK's "App User" data-collection role. update_odk_app_user_role(   action     = "assign",   con        = con,   project_id = project_id,   form_id    = form_id,   role_id    = 2,   actor_id   = new_user$actor_id ) #> [1] TRUE list_odk_form_users(con = con, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   actorId roleId #>     <int>  <int> #> 1     198      7 #> 2     250      2

what: Managing a whole team at once
title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Don’t click 40 collectors one time. Put actions CSV columns project_id, form_id, action, actor_name (action create, assign, remove) hand eri_odk_bulk_users(). Always run dry_run = TRUE first, validates every row live server reports problems together changing anything:
code: eri_odk_bulk_users("collectors.csv", con = con, dry_run = TRUE) #> ℹ Fetching project/form metadata for pre-flight... #> ✔ Pre-flight passed. 2 rows to process. #> ℹ Dry run -- no changes will be made. #>   [1] assign "Jane Fieldworker" on eri_test_river_prospection (project 11) #>   [2] create "John Fieldworker" on eri_test_river_prospection (project 11)

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: form deliberately simple, one row per submission, one table. real ODK forms repeat groups: section enumerator fills per submission, several larvae sampled one site, several household members one visit, several nets given one household. ODK Central exports repeat group table, form one repeat comes two tables: parent table, one row per submission (named {form_id}), child table, one row per repeat instance (named {form_id}-{repeat_name}), linked back parent PARENT_KEY column whose value matches parent row’s KEY. erifunctions captures , nothing silently dropped.

Submit two three entries, one, use ＋ Add button inside Larva sample group record two three samples sending. extra samples populate child table.

PARENT_KEY points back parent’s KEY: three submissions produced seven samples, submission 5e70e2a3… sampled three times, 621e3b01… three times, b3ce44d7… .

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: tables kept separate purpose, faithful, lossless shape data. want one flat table (one row per sample, carrying site context), join child parent PARENT_KEY = KEY: table flows quality-check → stage → approve gate extract. Approve parent repeats together stay step.
code: parent  <- eri_read("uga/demo/research/raw/eri_test_river_repeat.parquet", azcontainer = data_con) samples <- eri_read("uga/demo/research/raw/eri_test_river_repeat-larva_sample.parquet",                     azcontainer = data_con)  flat <- dplyr::left_join(   samples,   dplyr::select(parent, KEY, site_name, prospection_date, river_stage),   by = c("PARENT_KEY" = "KEY") ) flat #> # A tibble: 7 × 7 #>   species    larva_count PARENT_KEY                     KEY   site_name prospection_date river_stage #>   <chr>            <dbl> <chr>                          <chr> <chr>     <date>           <chr> #> 1 s_neavei             3 uuid:b3ce44d7-6666-4889-91dc-… uuid… ds        2026-06-09       medium #> 2 s_neavei             3 uuid:5e70e2a3-382a-4269-838c-… uuid… ld        2026-06-08       medium #> 3 s_damnosum           4 uuid:5e70e2a3-382a-4269-838c-… uuid… ld        2026-06-08       medium #> 4 other                4 uuid:5e70e2a3-382a-4269-838c-… uuid… ld        2026-06-08       medium #> 5 s_damnosum           3 uuid:621e3b01-4825-43c5-9cf3-… uuid… a         2026-06-25       medium #> 6 other                3 uuid:621e3b01-4825-43c5-9cf3-… uuid… a         2026-06-25       medium #> 7 s_neavei             3 uuid:621e3b01-4825-43c5-9cf3-… uuid… a         2026-06-25       medium

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Everything far moved data ODK Central. Sometimes need direction: stack records already exist, collected paper, living old spreadsheet, want land ODK Central sit alongside field submissions flow pipeline. eri_odk_upload() exactly : reads table creates one submission per row existing published form. mirror image download: columns matched form fields name, using flattening saw , field nested visit group column visit-date, repeat groups supplied {form_id}-{repeat} child tables linked PARENT_KEY. download_odk_form(tables = TRUE) result valid input, download upload round-trip. Say historical river-prospection records backfill. Build (read) table whose columns match form’s fields: Always dry-run first. dry_run = TRUE validates table live form, unknown columns, required fields, value types (dates, numbers, geopoints), select-value choice lists, sends nothing: non-empty tibble tells exactly cell fix (e.g. river_stage value "purple" → “value(s) choice list”). clean, drop dry_run send : instanceID submission derived deterministically key_col (record_id). makes upload safe re-run: second time, ODK Central recognises ids rejects , nothing duplicated, fix couple rows run , change rest skip. bad row never aborts batch: comes back failed (server’s message) neighbours load.
code: backfill <- data.frame(   site_name       = c("Old Ford", "Bend Camp"),   prospection_date = c("2025-11-03", "2025-11-04"),   river_stage     = c("low", "medium"),   blackfly_count  = c(12L, 7L),   collector       = c("paper-archive", "paper-archive"),   record_id       = c("hist-001", "hist-002")    # a stable key we'll hash for the instanceID ) eri_odk_upload(backfill, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id,                con = con, key_col = "record_id", dry_run = TRUE) #> ✔ Validation clean: all columns map to form fields. #> ℹ `dry_run` is on -- no submissions were sent. #> # A tibble: 0 × 4 #> # ℹ 4 variables: table <chr>, column <chr>, row <int>, issue <chr> eri_odk_upload(backfill, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id,                con = con, key_col = "record_id") #> ✔ Validation clean: all columns map to form fields. #> ✔ Uploaded to "eri_test_river_prospection": 2 created, 0 already present. #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   instance_id                           status  http_status message #>   <chr>                                 <chr>         <int> <chr> #> 1 uuid:682c06225e8949547e6c34a6b6834ee4 created         200 NA #> 2 uuid:80a4e16d30398e85e78b4c18799a63ab created         200 NA eri_odk_upload(backfill, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id,                con = con, key_col = "record_id") #> ✔ Uploaded to "eri_test_river_prospection": 0 created, 2 already present.

what: Headers that don’t match the form
title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: paper legacy spreadsheet rarely uses form’s exact column names. Rather editing source file, pass mapping, c(your_header = "field-column", …), eri_odk_upload() renames columns validates: Map columns differ, anything already matching field left alone, drop dry_run send. targets flattened field-column names (group-field).
code: paper <- data.frame(   village   = c("Old Ford", "Bend Camp"),   date_seen = c("2025-11-03", "2025-11-04"),   stage     = c("low", "medium"),   flies     = c(12L, 7L),   recorder  = c("paper-archive", "paper-archive"),   rec       = c("hist-101", "hist-102") )  eri_odk_upload(paper, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id, con = con,                mapping = c(village = "site_name", date_seen = "prospection_date",                            stage = "river_stage", flies = "blackfly_count",                            recorder = "collector"),                key_col = "rec", dry_run = TRUE) #> ✔ Validation clean: all columns map to form fields. #> ℹ `dry_run` is on -- no submissions were sent. #> # A tibble: 0 × 4 #> # ℹ 4 variables: table <chr>, column <chr>, row <int>, issue <chr>

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Repeat-group forms upload . Pass named-list shape download_odk_form(tables = TRUE) returns: parent table first, {form_id}-{repeat} child table PARENT_KEY column whose value matches parent row’s KEY. eri_odk_upload() rebuilds nested submission attaches repeat instance right parent: Downloaded back, first submission carries two larva_sample rows second one, child attached parent PARENT_KEY. (KEY just link column ; seeds deterministic instanceID via key_col otherwise treated system column.) Two limits know. form must published (can’t backfill draft), attachments can’t sent creation: ODK Central API constraint, photos/GPS traces scope upload.
code: repeat_data <- list(   eri_test_river_repeat = data.frame(     site_name        = c("Old Ford", "Bend Camp"),     prospection_date = c("2025-11-03", "2025-11-04"),     river_stage      = c("low", "medium"),     collector        = c("paper-archive", "paper-archive"),     KEY              = c("p1", "p2")            # link key for the children   ),   `eri_test_river_repeat-larva_sample` = data.frame(     species     = c("s_neavei", "s_damnosum", "s_neavei"),     larva_count = c(3L, 4L, 2L),     PARENT_KEY  = c("p1", "p1", "p2")           # two samples for p1, one for p2   ) )  eri_odk_upload(repeat_data, project_id = project_id, form_id = "eri_test_river_repeat",                con = con, key_col = "KEY") #> ✔ Validation clean: all columns map to form fields. #> ✔ Uploaded to "eri_test_river_repeat": 2 created, 0 already present. #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   instance_id                           status  http_status message #>   <chr>                                 <chr>         <int> <chr> #> 1 uuid:5b017f95628125dba44505b9e366dff9 created         200 NA #> 2 uuid:ec5ea8c7e5364fa80c6a68dceeb4916b created         200 NA

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Practice run done, remove everything created leave trace. Finally, ODK Central web interface, delete test form (Form ▸ ⋯ ▸ Delete) test project want gone . Deregister vs purge. real form, eri_odk_deregister() soft-deletes, flips registry entry active: false rather erasing , record tracked (sync history) survives audit; inactive entry harmless won’t show eri_odk_list_registered(). used eri_odk_purge() sandbox: hard-deletes entry (active already-inactive) shared registry left exactly found . delete freely : form submissions invented. Real ODK forms field submissions never deleted casually, primary record work done field. discipline guide builds, register, sync raw/, quality-check, approve human gate, keeps real data trustworthy.
code: # Remove the demo collector from ODK Central (revoke its form access, then delete the account). update_odk_app_user_role(action = "revoke", con = con, project_id = project_id,                          form_id = form_id, role_id = 2, actor_id = new_user$actor_id) update_odk_app_user_role(action = "delete", con = con, project_id = project_id,                          actor_id = new_user$actor_id) # This was a sandbox, so PURGE the registry entry (hard-delete) to leave no trace. # For a real form you'd use eri_odk_deregister() instead, which soft-deletes # (active: false) and keeps the sync history for audit. eri_odk_purge(project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id,               server_url = "https://your-odk-server.org/", data_con = data_con) #> ✔ Purged 1 registry entry for "eri_test_river_prospection" (project 11).  # If you also tried the repeat form in section 4, purge it too. eri_odk_purge(project_id = project_id, form_id = "eri_test_river_repeat",               server_url = "https://your-odk-server.org/", data_con = data_con) #> ✔ Purged 1 registry entry for "eri_test_river_repeat" (project 11). # Delete the whole uga/demo sandbox namespace (raw + staged + processed + logs). # eri_dir_delete() removes it recursively, the in-package path, no Storage Explorer. eri_dir_delete("uga/demo", azcontainer = data_con)

title: Onboarding a new country, disease, or data type (for data analysts)
text: eri_schema_validate() checks structure, required sections, valid column types, temporal/consistency rules reference columns actually exist. Run file just edited: empty tibble means ’s valid. something , get row per problem. Say fat- finger two column types numbr: Fix schema re-run get clean ✔. green light use .
code: eri_schema_validate("atlantis_malaria_surveillance_aggregate.yaml") #> ✔ Schema 'atlantis_malaria_surveillance_aggregate.yaml' is valid. #> # A tibble: 0 × 3 #> # ℹ 3 variables: issue_type <chr>, field <chr>, message <chr> eri_schema_validate("atlantis_malaria_surveillance_aggregate.yaml") #> ⚠ 2 issues found in 'atlantis_malaria_surveillance_aggregate.yaml': #> ✖ Column 'Year' has invalid type 'numbr'. Valid: numeric, character, categorical, date. #> ✖ Column 'EpiWeek' has invalid type 'numbr'. Valid: numeric, character, categorical, date.  # The returned tibble pinpoints each issue: #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   issue_type    field                message #>   <chr>         <chr>                <chr> #> 1 invalid_value columns.Year.type    Column 'Year' has invalid type 'numbr'. Valid: numeric, … #> 2 invalid_value columns.EpiWeek.type Column 'EpiWeek' has invalid type 'numbr'. Valid: numeric, …

title: Onboarding a new country, disease, or data type (for data analysts)
text: Case Management Reports (CMR) monthly Excel returns country programs. Onboarding one writes CMR schema template (pass create_dirs = TRUE) staging folders combined rblf disease code (RB + LF, programmes reports cover), eri_stage_cmr() lands raw Excel. Dry-run first: rblf/cmr staging archive, canonical home. source ≠ measure model (ADR-0012), CMR format programmatic channel. eri_split_cmr() reads staged Excel routes sheet disease measure, e.g. RB Treatment → {country}/oncho/programmatic/treatment/, approve disease/measure. rblf/cmr/ transitional landing/archive; canonical, approved data per-disease (shared sheets like Training route together combined rblf disease). See CMR guide full upload → stage → split → approve flow. real: CMR template ships common report sheets commented , uncomment ones country files, make sheet’s field_code_prefix match #tag_ row Excel file. test eri_ingest_cmr().
code: eri_onboard_cmr("atlantis", "Atlantis", create_dirs = TRUE, dry_run = TRUE) #> ℹ Dry run -- nothing will be written or created. #>   Would write: atlantis_cmr_schema.yaml #>     Would create: atlantis/rblf/cmr/raw/ #>     Would create: atlantis/rblf/cmr/staged/ #>     Would create: atlantis/rblf/cmr/processed/ eri_onboard_cmr("atlantis", "Atlantis", create_dirs = TRUE) #> ✔ CMR schema template written to atlantis_cmr_schema.yaml. #> ✔ CMR Azure directories created under atlantis/rblf/cmr/. #> ℹ Next steps: #>   1. Open atlantis_cmr_schema.yaml and uncomment the sheets your country uses. #>   2. Match field_code_prefix to the #tag_ row in your CMR Excel file. #>   3. Submit via pull request to: inst/schemas/cmr/atlantis.yaml #>   4. Test ingestion with eri_ingest_cmr('your_file.xlsx', country = 'atlantis').
