title: Quality-check an extract and give a country feedback
text: Auto-corrections applied logged; flags handed back. correction something schema can fix unambiguously (known spelling, translation). flag something country can resolve (impossible age, unknown locality). Never “fix” flag guessing, send back. offline, DQ engine runs plain data frame. DQ sits full pipeline see ingest guide; engine internals custom checks, DQ pipeline.

title: Quality-check an extract and give a country feedback
text: practice ’d read submitted file (eri_read() / raw layer). small DR malaria surveillance extract deliberate problems, two values schema can normalise, :
code: extract <- data.frame(   Year                   = c(2026, 2026, 2026, 2026, 2026),   EpiWeek                = c(10, 11, 12, 13, 60),          # 60 is out of range   Age                    = c(34, 5, 200, 41, 28),          # 200 is impossible   Sex                    = c("Masculino", "Femenino", "Male", "X", "F"),  # X is invalid   Province_Residence     = c("SANTIAGO", "Santo Domingo", "Azua", "Distrito Nacional", "Nowhere"),   Municipality_Residence = c("Santiago", "SD", "Azua", "DN", "X"),   SampleDate             = c("2026-03-01", "2026-03-08", "2026-03-15", "2026-03-22", "2026-03-29"),   EpiWeekYear            = c(2026, 2026, 2025, 2026, 2026)  # row 3 mismatches Year )

title: Quality-check an extract and give a country feedback
text: Load schema dataset’s four axes (azcontainer = NULL uses bundled schema, Azure), run checks print report:
code: schema <- load_dq_schema("dr", "malaria", "surveillance", "aggregate", azcontainer = NULL) res    <- run_dq_checks(extract, schema) #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 3 corrections, 5 flags for review.  dq_report(res) #> ── Data Quality Report ───────────────────────────────────────────── #> Shape: 5 rows x 8 columns #> #> ── Automated Corrections (3 total) ── #> • "Sex": 2 corrections (translation) #> • "Province_Residence": 1 correction (correction) #> #> ── Flags Requiring Review (5 total) ── #> ! Value not in allowed_values list: 2 rows [Sex, Province_Residence] (e.g. X (row 4); Nowhere (row 5)) #> ! Value outside expected range [0, 120]: 1 row [Age] (e.g. 200 (row 3)) #> ! Value outside expected range [1, 53]: 1 row [EpiWeek] (e.g. 60 (row 5)) #> ! Year extracted from SampleDate does not match EpiWeekYear: 1 row [SampleDate] (e.g. 2026-03-15 (row 3)) #> #> ℹ See `result$flags` for the full row-level detail.

title: Quality-check an extract and give a country feedback
text: res$log audit trail every auto-correction, changed, row, . Share country knows normalised behalf (error part, see ):
code: res$log #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>     row column             original_value corrected_value rule        action #>   <int> <chr>              <chr>          <chr>           <chr>       <chr> #> 1     1 Sex                Masculino      Male            translation corrected #> 2     2 Sex                Femenino       Female          translation corrected #> 3     1 Province_Residence SANTIAGO       Santiago        correction  corrected

title: Quality-check an extract and give a country feedback
text: res$flags feedback list, rows schema resolve. send back: Turn self-contained handback file eri_dq_export(), self-contained HTML (prints cleanly PDF browser) markdown file ready attach email paste Teams – one consistent format instead ad hoc table time:
code: res$flags #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>     row column             value      issue #>   <int> <chr>              <chr>      <chr> #> 1     5 EpiWeek            60         Value outside expected range [1, 53] #> 2     3 Age                200        Value outside expected range [0, 120] #> 3     4 Sex                X          Value not in allowed_values list #> 4     5 Province_Residence Nowhere    Value not in allowed_values list #> 5     3 SampleDate         2026-03-15 Year extracted from SampleDate does not match EpiWeekYear eri_dq_export(res$flags, country = "dr") #> ✔ DQ report (5 flags · 5 open) written to '.../dq-report-dr-2026-07-11.html'.

title: Quality-check an extract and give a country feedback
text: eri_notify_dq() posts one-glance summary (rows, corrections, flags, -flagged columns) Teams, handy shared channel tracks country’s submissions: posts compact, --glance summary channel webhook points : target specific Teams channel instead default webhook, pass team = / channel = token (Graph-API path), see connections guide.
code: eri_notify_dq(res, country = "dr", disease = "malaria") #> ℹ Sending Teams message via incoming webhook. [DQ Report] DR - MALARIA Rows processed : 5 Corrections    : 3 Flags          : 5 Top flagged    : Age, EpiWeek, Province_Residence, SampleDate, Sex

title: Quality-check an extract and give a country feedback
text: cleaned data (res$data) continues pipeline, see ingest guide stage → approve. DQ pipeline, check engine, schema rules, adding custom_checks. epi anomaly guide, spikes, gaps, cross-field/spatial anomalies beyond cell-level validity. Persist flags shared backlog eri_dq_log(), log-triage guide. CMR workbook specifically, eri_dq_export() also renders richer, per-sheet flags tibble eri_cmr_dq_report() – see CMR guide DQ review guide eri_dq_review(), interactive triage wrapper calls automatically. ```

title: Branded tables, figures, and decks for outputs
text: practice ’d pull approved data first, eri_query() roll-(see ad-hoc guide) eri_read() one dataset. use small frame every chunk runs -:
code: library(erifunctions) dat <- data.frame(   province   = c("North", "South", "East", "West"),   cases      = c(420, 180, 260, 95),   tested     = c(1200, 600, 900, 400) ) dat$positivity <- round(dat$cases / dat$tested * 100, 1)

title: Branded tables, figures, and decks for outputs
text: eri_table() turns data frame styled flextable: ERI navy header, banded rows, optional title footnote, ready drop HTML/Word/PowerPoint output: returns flextable object; print report, hand Word/PowerPoint/HTML helpers. highlight_cols named list mapping column fill colour (list(col = "#hex")), use shade column want eye land .
code: eri_table(   dat,   title          = "Malaria cases by province, 2026",   footnote       = "Source: sandbox data",   highlight_cols = list(positivity = "#FFC000") )

title: Branded tables, figures, and decks for outputs
text: ggplot becomes -brand adding eri_brand_ggplot_theme(): domain figures purpose-built helpers, eri_plot_theme("map" | "epicurve") eri_color_scheme() (e.g. standard "malaria.incidence" bins), eri_map_* family choropleths point maps. need boundary data; spatial workflow guide covers end--end, epi analytics covers eri_epidemic_curve().
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = province, y = cases)) +   geom_col() +   labs(title = "Cases by province", x = NULL, y = "Cases") +   eri_brand_ggplot_theme() p

title: Branded tables, figures, and decks for outputs
text: partner wants numbers, eri_report_excel() writes styled multi-sheet workbook one call, named element sheets becomes tab: Need finer control (multiple sheets, added titles)? Build eri_wb_create() → eri_wb_add_sheet() → eri_wb_save().
code: eri_report_excel(   sheets = list(Cases = dat),   path   = "malaria_summary_2026.xlsx",   title  = "Malaria summary 2026" ) #> ✔ Workbook saved to 'malaria_summary_2026.xlsx'

title: Branded tables, figures, and decks for outputs
text: eri_pptx_* family builds PowerPoint slide slide, title, sections, tables, plots, meeting deck reproducible code, manual copy-paste: eri_pptx_add_* call returns updated deck, pipe reassign go. eri_pptx_create() accepts template = start branded master.
code: deck <- eri_pptx_create() deck <- eri_pptx_add_title(deck, "Malaria Summary 2026", subtitle = "ERI") deck <- eri_pptx_add_table(deck, dat, title = "Cases by province") deck <- eri_pptx_add_plot(deck, p, title = "Cases by province") eri_pptx_save(deck, "malaria_summary_2026.pptx") #> ✔ Presentation saved to 'malaria_summary_2026.pptx'

title: Data-model decision card: channel vs. measure
text: Two , data_source data_type, ones people mix . independent: don’t determine . channel carries different measures, (channel, disease) gives different measure per country: One CMR (programmatic) fans 7 measures across 3 diseases (treatment, MMDP, training, surveys…). DR malaria surveillance case (one row per patient); Haiti malaria surveillance aggregate (facility × month counts). source, disease, different measure.
code: data / {country} / {disease} / {data_source} / {data_type} / {layer}

title: Data-model decision card: channel vs. measure
text: country: dr, ht, eth, uga, nga, sdn, ssd, tcd, mad, oepa, … disease: malaria, oncho, lf, sch, sth (free text; lowercase). surveillance: routine MoH feed (DR/Haiti malaria). Output: cases aggregate counts. programmatic: country-team activity/coverage data: CMR, direct MDA feed. Spans diseases (split per disease ingest). research: survey instruments launched + monitored via ODK, cleaned final analytic dataset (TAS, prevalence, entomology). data_type (measure): rows count: case · aggregate · treatment · mmdp · training · survey · tas · prevalence · entomology.
code: ┌─ case            (line-list, one row per patient)    surveillance ──────┤                       └─ aggregate       (facility/period counts)                        ┌─ treatment       (MDA coverage)    programmatic ──────┼─ mmdp · training                       └─ survey                        ┌─ tas    research (ODK) ────┼─ prevalence                       └─ entomology

what: Two things that trip people up
title: Data-model decision card: channel vs. measure
text: format ≠ data_source. “CMR” “ODK” input formats, recorded format field, channels. CMR programmatic; ODK form research. (cmr/odk path tokens may see older data transitional retired, ADR-0012.) missing combination normal. load_dq_schema() / eri_data_path() doesn’t recognise value, get warning, error, adding new country/disease/source/measure data change (schema + registry entry), code change. See onboarding new program guide.

title: Data Quality Pipeline
code: library(erifunctions)  # Load the bundled DR malaria case schema (falls back to local if Azure unavailable) schema <- load_dq_schema("dr", "malaria_case", azcontainer = NULL)  # Run all automated checks result <- run_dq_checks(raw_data, schema)  # Inspect result                # calls dq_report() automatically

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: schema YAML file inst/schemas/ (optionally Azure schemas/). key blocks :
code: country: dr disease: malaria_case version: "1.0"  preprocessing:   - remove_smart_quotes      # fix curly quotes from Excel   - strip_column_name_spaces # remove spaces/parens from header row  temporal:   date_col:  sample_date   year_col:  year   cross_check_year_col: year  # flags rows where year != year(sample_date)  columns:   year:     required: true     type: numeric     aliases: [Year, Año, ano]      # recognised alternative column names     range: [2000, 2035]    province:     required: true     type: character     aliases: [Province_Residence, Provincia_residencia]     allowed_values: [Distrito Nacional, Santo Domingo, ...]     translations:                  # auto-corrected silently       "sto. Domingo": "Santo Domingo"     corrections:                   # also auto-corrected       "Sto Domingo": "Santo Domingo"  derived:   imported_flag:     formula: "as.integer(province %in% c('Haiti', 'Extranjero', 'Otros'))"  consistency:   epiweek_valid:     lhs: epiweek     op: ">="     rhs_value: 1     message: "Epiweek below 1"   positives_le_tested:     lhs: n_positive     op: "<="     rhs: n_tested     message: "Positives exceed tested"

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: pipeline runs steps order: Preprocessing: removes smart quotes, optionally strips column name whitespace, drops rows missing year value. Alias resolution: renames recognised alternative column headers canonical name defined schema. Required-column check: adds NA-row flag column marked required: true completely absent data. Type coercion: converts columns numeric, date, character declared; flags values coerced. Range checks: flags numeric values outside [min, max] range. Translations corrections: applies lookup maps silently, logging change $log. Allowed-values check: flags values allowed_values list. NA fill: fills NA na_fill-annotated columns specified default logs change. Temporal cross-check: flags rows year(date_col) != year_col. Derived columns: evaluates formula (data, ...) adds result new column. Aggregate consistency: derived column, checks formula existing value flags discrepancies (useful pre-computed totals).

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: $log tibble records every automated correction: $flags tibble records everything requiring review:
code: # Full formatted report dq_report(result)  # Access components directly cleaned <- result$data    # tibble with corrections applied + derived columns log     <- result$log     # what was changed automatically flags   <- result$flags   # what needs analyst attention

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: baseline checks can chain additional anomaly detectors. three accept either plain tibble dq_result, return type, compose |>.

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Two columns added $data: pct_change_n_cases anomaly_pct_change_n_cases. Flagged rows also appended $flags.
code: # Aggregate first, then check for unusual week-to-week swings weekly <- dplyr::count(result$data, year, epiweek, province, name = "n_cases")  result <- result |>   add_anomaly_pct_change(     value_col  = "n_cases",     period_col = "epiweek",     year_col   = "year",     group_cols = "province",     threshold  = 0.5   # flag > 50% change   )

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Returns tibble missing epiweek/group combinations, appends $flags given dq_result.
code: gaps <- result |>   add_anomaly_gaps(     period_col  = "epiweek",     period_type = "week",     group_cols  = "province",     year_col    = "year"   )

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Evaluates every rule schema’s consistency: block cleaned data (including derived columns). Use run_dq_checks() derived columns available.
code: result <- result |> add_anomaly_consistency(schema)

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Downloads reference shapefile Azure flags province commune names appear canonical list. Skipped gracefully Azure unavailable.
code: # Requires Azure access and the sf/terra packages result <- result |> add_anomaly_spatial(schema)

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Pass list functions run_dq_checks() checks expressed YAML:
code: check_no_future_dates <- function(data, log, flags) {   future_rows <- which(data$sample_date > Sys.Date())   if (length(future_rows) > 0) {     flags <- dplyr::bind_rows(       flags,       tibble::tibble(         row    = future_rows,         column = "sample_date",         value  = as.character(data$sample_date[future_rows]),         issue  = "sample_date is in the future"       )     )   }   list(data = data, log = log, flags = flags) }  result <- run_dq_checks(raw_data, schema, custom_checks = list(check_no_future_dates))

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: satisfied cleaned data, export downstream use:
code: # Parquet for analysis pipelines arrow::write_parquet(result$data, "data/clean/dr_malaria_clean.parquet")  # Excel summary for review meetings eri_report_excel(   sheets = list(     data  = list(data = result$data,  title = "Cleaned data"),     flags = list(data = result$flags, title = "Flags for review"),     log   = list(data = result$log,   title = "Automated corrections")   ),   path  = "outputs/dq_review.xlsx" )

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Rows population <= 0 NA values automatically return NA avoid silent division errors.
code: library(erifunctions) library(dplyr)  weekly <- result$data |>   group_by(year, epiweek, province) |>   summarise(n_cases = n(), .groups = "drop") |>   left_join(population_data, by = c("year", "province")) |>   mutate(rate_per_1000 = eri_incidence_rate(n_cases, population, multiplier = 1000))

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Convert CDC epiweek numbers calendar dates time-series plots:
code: weekly <- weekly |>   mutate(week_start = eri_epiweek_date(year, epiweek, week_start = "Sunday"))  library(ggplot2) ggplot(weekly, aes(week_start, n_cases, colour = province)) +   geom_line() +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Cases", title = "Weekly malaria cases by province") +   eri_brand_ggplot_theme()

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: eri_study_week() expresses epiweeks relative index event, useful intervention analyses IRS campaigns:
code: irs_date <- as.Date("2023-06-15")  weekly <- weekly |>   mutate(study_week = eri_study_week(year, epiweek, index_date = irs_date))  # week < 0 = before IRS; week >= 1 = after IRS pre_post <- weekly |>   mutate(period = ifelse(study_week < 0, "Pre-IRS", "Post-IRS"))

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Convert individual case dates epiweek numbers aggregation:
code: # Add epiweek number from a sample date column cases <- cases |>   mutate(epiweek = eri_date_to_epiweek(sample_date))  # Filter to a cross-year epiweek range (week 40/2023 through week 10/2024) subset <- eri_epiweek_range(weekly, "year", "epiweek",                              start_year = 2023, start_week = 40,                              end_year   = 2024, end_week   = 10)

what: Branded ggplot theme and colour palette
title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: ERI figures use eri_brand_ggplot_theme() colours eri_brand_colors():
code: cols <- eri_brand_colors() # navy="#44546A"  blue="#4472C4"  orange="#ED7D31" # gold="#FFC000"  green="#70AD47" light_blue="#5B9BD5"  gray="#A5A5A5"  ggplot(weekly, aes(week_start, rate_per_1000)) +   geom_col(fill = cols[["blue"]]) +   eri_brand_ggplot_theme() +   labs(title = "Malaria incidence rate", y = "Cases per 1 000")

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: LF TAS data typically collected pools (FTS cards). Use eri_lf_pooled_prev() estimate individual-level prevalence pool results using standard formula 1 - ((1 - npos/npool)^(1/pool_size)). function warns (rather errors) npos > npool returns NA rows, batch processing interrupted.
code: # Scalar estimate for a single survey eri_lf_pooled_prev(npos = 3, npool = 100, pool_size = 5)  # Per-commune estimates commune_prev <- tas_data |>   group_by(commune) |>   summarise(     npos      = sum(fts_result == "Positive"),     npool     = n(),     pool_size = mean(pool_size, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups   = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(pooled_prev = eri_lf_pooled_prev(npos, npool, pool_size))

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Use ht_lf_programmatic_treatment dr_lf_programmatic_treatment schemas clean coverage data, compute programme indicators:
code: schema <- load_dq_schema("ht", "lf", "programmatic", "treatment", azcontainer = NULL) result <- run_dq_checks(raw_mda, schema)  coverage <- result$data |>   mutate(     coverage_pct = treated / target_pop * 100,     reached_80   = coverage_pct >= 80   )  dplyr::count(coverage, year, reached_80)

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Onchocerciasis uses standardised five-level programme status framework. eri_oncho_program_levels() returns ordered factor levels used across OEPA visualisations, eri_oncho_status_map() produces ready--insert ggplot object:
code: eri_oncho_program_levels() # [1] "Non-endemic" # [2] "Under surveillance" # [3] "MDA ongoing" # [4] "MDA stopped - under surveillance" # [5] "Verified free of transmission"  eus <- eri_spatial_load("oepa", level = 2)  status_map <- eri_oncho_status_map(   shapefile   = eus,   status_data = programme_status_2024,   eu_col      = "eu_name",   status_col  = "programme_status",   title       = "OEPA Onchocerciasis Programme Status 2024",   scale_bar   = TRUE,   north_arrow = TRUE )

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: eri_table() produces Carter Center-branded flextable renders consistently PowerPoint, HTML, Excel:
code: summary_tbl <- weekly |>   group_by(province) |>   summarise(     total_cases = sum(n_cases),     mean_weekly = round(mean(n_cases), 1),     peak_rate   = max(rate_per_1000, na.rm = TRUE)   )  ft <- eri_table(   summary_tbl,   title    = "Malaria summary by province",   footnote = "Source: DIGEPI surveillance data" )  # Insert into a PowerPoint presentation eri_pptx_create() |>   eri_pptx_add_title("Hispaniola Malaria 2024") |>   eri_pptx_add_table(summary_tbl, title = "Province summary") |>   eri_pptx_save("outputs/malaria_2024.pptx")

title: Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
text: remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions"). Everything offline.
code: library(erifunctions) library(dplyr)

title: Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
text: use bundled case-level malaria schema (Azure needed, azcontainer = NULL loads copy ships package) small synthetic line-list: two provinces six epiweeks, one row per case. three planted problems, species typo, case spike, missing week, kinds thing real extract hides:
code: # Load by the four-part identity (ADR-0012): country, disease, data_source (the # channel, surveillance/programmatic/research), data_type (the measure, here # "case", the case-level line-list). The legacy compound key still works too: # load_dq_schema("dr", "malaria_case") resolves via an alias during the migration. schema <- load_dq_schema("dr", "malaria", "surveillance", "case", azcontainer = NULL)  # A tiny helper to make `n` identical case rows for a province/week. mk <- function(province, week, n, species = "P. vivax") {   tibble::tibble(year = 2024, epiweek = week, province = province,                  municipality = paste0(province, " City"),                  sample_date = as.character(as.Date("2024-01-01") + (week - 1) * 7),                  species = species)[rep(1, n), ] }  cases <- dplyr::bind_rows(   mk("Azua", 1, 3), mk("Azua", 1, 1, species = "P.vivax"),   # 1 species typo   mk("Azua", 2, 3), mk("Azua", 3, 4), mk("Azua", 4, 3),   mk("Azua", 5, 15), mk("Azua", 6, 4),                        # week 5 SPIKE   mk("San Juan", 1, 3), mk("San Juan", 2, 3), mk("San Juan", 3, 3),   mk("San Juan", 5, 3), mk("San Juan", 6, 3)                  # week 4 MISSING ) nrow(cases)   # 48 cases across 2 provinces, epiweeks 1-6

title: Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
text: Start run_dq_checks(), engine ingest guide covers. catches cell problems (bad types, --range values, values allowed list): One flag, mistyped species. Useful, tells nothing whether epidemiology makes sense. , chain anomaly detectors.
code: result <- run_dq_checks(cases, schema) result #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 0 corrections, 1 flag for review. #> ── Data Quality Report ────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Shape: 48 rows x 7 columns #> #> ── Automated Corrections (0 total) ── #> ✔ No corrections applied. #> #> ── Flags Requiring Review (1 total) ── #> ! Value not in allowed_values list: 1 row [species] (e.g. P.vivax (row 4)) #> #> ℹ See `result$flags` for the full row-level detail.

title: Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
text: Epidemic data moves week week; sudden jump either real outbreak data error, need know . Aggregate weekly counts per province, flag period--period changes threshold: Azua jumped 3 cases 15 week 5, five-fold rise (detector reports pct_change = 4, .e. +400%), fell back week 6 (-73%). flagged, spike produces two large changes (, ). week-5 row one investigate: genuine cluster field respond , batch cases get entered twice? detector doesn’t decide, points week worth phone call. (aggregated result$data -, one still-flagged species row rolled , harmless per-week counts, real extract ’d resolve cell flags §2 rolling anything .)
code: weekly <- count(result$data, year, epiweek, province, name = "n_cases")  flagged <- add_anomaly_pct_change(   weekly,   value_col  = "n_cases",   period_col = "epiweek",   group_cols = "province",   # compare each province against itself   year_col   = "year",       # so weeks order correctly across years   threshold  = 0.5           # flag changes greater than 50% ) #> ! 2 rows flagged for % change anomaly in "n_cases" (threshold: 50%).  flagged[flagged$anomaly_pct_change_n_cases, c("epiweek", "province", "n_cases", "pct_change_n_cases")] #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   epiweek province n_cases pct_change_n_cases #>     <dbl> <chr>      <int>              <dbl> #> 1       5 Azua          15              4 #> 2       6 Azua           4             -0.733

title: Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
text: remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions"), plus sf package (install.packages("sf")). string-match step runs fully offline. optional geocoding step uses OpenStreetMap (Nominatim) default, keyless, setup, though makes network calls. (Higher-quality geocoding via Google available key; see end.)
code: library(erifunctions) library(sf)

what: 1. The reference boundary and your messy data
title: Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
text: Reconciliation needs authoritative boundary, sf object whose columns hold canonical admin names. real work load eri_spatial_load(); guide build tiny one hand (two real places live geocoder somewhere land): incoming data, five localities usual problems (case, typo, names boundary, one pure noise): describe hierarchy two parallel vectors, finest → coarsest: data’s columns (loc_cols) matching boundary columns (admin_cols).
code: box <- function(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) {   st_polygon(list(matrix(c(xmin,ymin, xmax,ymin, xmax,ymax, xmin,ymax, xmin,ymin),                          ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE))) } boundary <- st_sf(   adm3_name = c("Boston", "Cambridge"),   adm2_name = c("Suffolk", "Middlesex"),     # the authoritative county for each   adm1_name = c("Massachusetts", "Massachusetts"),   geometry  = st_sfc(box(-71.10, -70.99, 42.230, 42.350),                      box(-71.16, -71.06, 42.350, 42.400), crs = 4326) ) messy <- tibble::tibble(   locality = c("boston", "Cambrige", "Fenway Park", "MIT", "Zzqxborough Nowhere"),   county   = c("Suffolk", "Middlesex", "Suffolk",   "Suffolk", "Suffolk"),   state    = rep("Massachusetts", 5),   cases    = c(12L, 7L, 4L, 3L, 1L) ) messy #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   locality            county    state         cases #>   <chr>               <chr>     <chr>         <int> #> 1 boston              Suffolk   Massachusetts    12 #> 2 Cambrige            Middlesex Massachusetts     7 #> 3 Fenway Park         Suffolk   Massachusetts     4 #> 4 MIT                 Suffolk   Massachusetts     3 #> 5 Zzqxborough Nowhere Suffolk   Massachusetts     1

title: Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
text: First, match can network . Setting method = NULL disables geocoding entirely, max_dist = 1 allows one-character typo finest level: Two wins already, offline: "boston" matched Boston (case-insensitive, accent-insensitive), typo "Cambrige" matched Cambridge (within max_dist). matched row whole hierarchy rewritten boundary’s canonical spelling, just locality, county state , misspelled parent gets fixed well. three don’t appear boundary, ’re unresolved: ’re residual geocode.
code: matched <- eri_spatial_reconcile(   messy,   loc_cols   = c("locality", "county", "state"),   admin_cols = c("adm3_name", "adm2_name", "adm1_name"),   shapefile  = boundary,   method     = NULL,    # string match only, fully offline   max_dist   = 1L       # tolerate a 1-character typo in the finest name ) #> ✔ Reconciled 5 distinct localities: 2 matched, 0 geocoded, 0 need review, 3 unresolved.  matched[, c("locality", "county", "state", "reconcile_status")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   locality            county    state         reconcile_status #>   <chr>               <chr>     <chr>         <chr> #> 1 Boston              Suffolk   Massachusetts matched #> 2 Cambridge           Middlesex Massachusetts matched #> 3 Fenway Park         Suffolk   Massachusetts unresolved #> 4 MIT                 Suffolk   Massachusetts unresolved #> 5 Zzqxborough Nowhere Suffolk   Massachusetts unresolved

title: Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
text: Now let geocoding handle rows didn’t match. method = "osm" uses keyless OpenStreetMap; country_name appended address anchor : Three different outcomes three residual rows: Fenway Park → geocoded. geocoded point inside Boston/Suffolk polygon, agrees county supplied, ’s trusted: name rewritten canonical Boston get coordinates. MIT → geocoded_review. geocoded fine, point falls Cambridge/Middlesex polygon, claimed Suffolk. disagreement trust guard firing: coordinates kept inspect, names left exactly supplied (silently “corrected”). Maybe MIT really across county line expected, maybe county mis-entered. decide. Zzqxborough Nowhere → unresolved. geocoder returned nothing. coordinates, nothing changed. don’t take “Cambridge/Middlesex” faith. eri_spatial_reconcile() returns admin units geocoded point actually fell geocoded_<admin_col> columns, flagged row tells exactly disagreed , manual sf::st_join() re-derive : county still Suffolk (untouched, trust guard never overwrote ), geocoded_adm2_name shows point landed Middlesex. side--side whole conflict, ready confirm fix. (columns NA matched, unresolved, outside-polygon rows.)
code: result <- eri_spatial_reconcile(   messy,   loc_cols     = c("locality", "county", "state"),   admin_cols   = c("adm3_name", "adm2_name", "adm1_name"),   shapefile    = boundary,   country_name = "USA",   method       = "osm",   max_dist     = 1L ) #> ℹ Geocoding 3 unmatched localities via "osm"... #> ✔ Reconciled 5 distinct localities: 2 matched, 1 geocoded, 1 need review, 1 unresolved. #> ! 1 geocode flagged "geocoded_review" (low-confidence or parent mismatch) -- verify before use.  result[, c("locality", "county", "longitude", "latitude", "reconcile_status")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   locality            county    longitude latitude reconcile_status #>   <chr>               <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl> <chr> #> 1 Boston              Suffolk         NA      NA   matched #> 2 Cambridge           Middlesex       NA      NA   matched #> 3 Boston              Suffolk      -71.1     42.3  geocoded #> 4 MIT                 Suffolk      -71.1     42.4  geocoded_review #> 5 Zzqxborough Nowhere Suffolk         NA      NA   unresolved result[result$reconcile_status == "geocoded_review",        c("locality", "county", "geocoded_adm3_name", "geocoded_adm2_name")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   locality county  geocoded_adm3_name geocoded_adm2_name #>   <chr>    <chr>   <chr>              <chr> #> 1 MIT      Suffolk Cambridge          Middlesex

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: renv records exact version every package study uses, analysis runs way next year today: commit renv.lock (renv/ activation files). moment lockfile lands GitHub, reproducibility check goes green every push, restores pinned environment clean machine confirms erifunctions loads. Two renv gotchas worth avoiding. Install thecartercenter/erifunctions, personal fork, snapshot, otherwise lockfile pins wrong source collaborator restoring gets wrong package. Use renv::snapshot(type = "") packages use analysis (e.g. ggplot2) captured , just ones package imports.
code: install.packages("renv") renv::init()                                   # start tracking package versions renv::install("thecartercenter/erifunctions")  # install the package INTO the project renv::snapshot(type = "all")                    # write renv.lock

what: 3. Add your data and give it metadata
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Data live git repository, lives Azure, record . register file artifact: file plus metadata (name, type, description, version). teammates later discover trust . First, create initial dataset, automatic-transmission cars (== 0), save locally: Now upload artifact metadata: name, type, description, version metadata, travel file show eri_artifact_list() anyone can see without opening . “Metadata” “tags” two different things, don’t mix . Think metadata label jar, version tag photograph entire kitchen one moment. create first tag §10.
code: cars_v1 <- mtcars[mtcars$am == 0, ] nrow(cars_v1)   # 19 cars to start with #> [1] 19 write.csv(cars_v1, "data/cars.csv", row.names = TRUE) eri_artifact_upload(   local_path  = "data/cars.csv",   name        = "mtcars_study_data",   type        = "study_data",   description = "mtcars fuel-economy study data, automatic transmissions only.",   version     = "1.0" ) #> ✔ Artifact "mtcars_study_data" (study_data) uploaded to artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv.

what: 4. Source the data into your project
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Uploading put data Azure; now pull copy project work . use eri_research_pull() hand artifact’s path. Pulling way (rather just reading local file) records data came research.yaml, sets reversible updates ’ll rely §11 fresh data arrives: Two ways pull, purpose. eri_artifact_pull("mtcars_study_data") quick registry shortcut. pull path eri_research_pull() also records pull research.yaml, archives superseded version, counts updates, exactly lifecycle guide demonstrates §11. Sourcing canonical, team-approved data. study draws data analysts already approved (study artifact uploaded), discover catalog pull coordinates, eri_research_pull() takes four tokens eri_catalog_query() reports (country, disease, data_source, data_type): Discover exists, pull coordinates catalog handed , one vocabulary, end end. Never commit data/ git. scaffold’s .gitignore already excludes . Data lives Azure; repository holds code research.yaml record pulled. mtcars matter, habit keeps real, protected country data safe , see Clean .
code: eri_research_pull(path = "artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv", dest = "data") #> ✔ Pulled 1 file from artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv to data. eri_catalog_query(country = "dr", disease = "malaria", layer = "processed") #> # ... data_source = surveillance, data_type = case ... eri_research_pull(country = "dr", disease = "malaria",                   data_source = "surveillance", data_type = "case", dest = "data")

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: analysis goes analysis/workflow.qmd project, scaffold seeded , gets version-controlled reproducibility-checked. Open write ordinary R. Read data just pulled, fit model: whole analysis. Notice none used erifunctions, modelling . package’s job starts want save freeze found.

Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept) 30.6052577  2.6986421  11.341 9.32e-09 *** #> wt          -1.8050509  1.0698184  -1.687   0.1122     #> hp          -0.0401358  0.0160570  -2.500   0.0245 *   #> disp        -0.0007664  0.0100435  -0.076   0.9402     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 2.024 on 15 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.7677, Adjusted R-squared:  0.7212  #> F-statistic: 16.52 on 3 and 15 DF,  p-value: 5.09e-05

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Push results back Azure safe tied project. Save fitted model object, figure: make decisions, jot project’s logbook. notes timestamped stay project, “drop rows?” gets answered year later:
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(cars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +   labs(title = "Fuel economy vs. weight (automatic cars)",        x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon") ggsave("figs/mpg_vs_weight.png", p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 150) eri_research_upload_output(model, "mtcars_lm.qs2") #> ✔ Output "mtcars_lm.qs2" uploaded to research/mtcars_study/outputs/mtcars_lm.qs2.  eri_research_upload_figure(   "figs/mpg_vs_weight.png",   caption = "MPG vs. weight, automatic cars (initial dataset)." ) #> ✔ Figure "mpg_vs_weight.png" uploaded to research/mtcars_study/outputs/figs/mpg_vs_weight.png. eri_research_log("Started with automatic transmissions only (am == 0); 19 cars.") eri_research_log("Fit lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp). Weight is the dominant predictor.")

what: 7. Look at the state of your project
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: point, ask project stands. eri_research_status() prints data pulled, many times input updated, many outputs, snapshots, tags exist: eri_research_resume() shorter “?” view, last pull, last logbook entry, snapshot count. Use top every work session (§9).
code: eri_research_status() #> ── Research project: mtcars_study (us/demo) ── #> • 1 tracked input (1 pull, 0 artifacts) #> • 2 outputs, 0 snapshots, 0 tags, 0 promotions #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   kind  source                                          pulled_at            updates archived #>   <chr> <chr>                                           <chr>                  <int> <lgl> #> 1 pull  artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv 2026-06-17T15:01:00Z       0 FALSE

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Figures evolve. improve one, just re-upload filename, replaces previous version Azure project keeps pointing current figure. special “update” command; uploading name update. (exactly §11 data changes.)

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: walk away, take snapshot, timestamped freeze whole data/ directory Azure, exact inputs recoverable even file changes later. Leave note, commit code, done: commit code (usethis::use_git() first commit, Commit main button GitHub Desktop) close laptop. Nothing lost.
code: eri_research_snapshot(label = "before-break") #> ✔ Snapshot of 1 file saved to research/mtcars_study/snapshots/20260617T150500Z/.  eri_research_log("Pausing here. Initial model done; awaiting the fuller dataset.")

Initial model done; awaiting the fuller dataset. #>   Snapshots:  1  eri_research_status()   # the fuller picture: inputs, outputs, tags

what: 10. When, and how, to tag a version
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: tag freezes whole study moment, data snapshot, exact analysis code commit, outputs, name can cite. answer “reproduce numbers report dated March.” tag? milestones, schedule. Good moments: finished analysis share present; make big change; submitting publishing. tag every day, tag marks significant progress, tags immutable, flurry throwaway tags just adds noise. (Snapshots, §9, cheap, frequent safety net; tags rare, citable landmarks.) just finished first real result, tag . Make sure analysis code committed first (tag records commit, warns working tree uncommitted changes): tag now binds data + code + outputs together forever. Anyone label can reconstruct exactly .
code: eri_research_tag(   "mtcars-am0-v1",   description = "Initial model: mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, automatic transmissions only." ) #> ✔ Tagged "mtcars-am0-v1": snapshot 20260617T150500Z + code "a1b2c3d".

what: 11. New data arrives, update and re-run
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Months , fuller dataset lands: longer restricted automatic cars. moment workflows handle badly, overwriting old data losing trail. erifunctions handles cleanly: old version archived, change counted, earlier tag still reproduces original. Build expanded dataset (drop == 0 filter, cars now) re-upload artifact name, bumping version. Re-using name update, duplicate: Now pull new version project. pulled input , erifunctions archives previous copy, swaps new one, records input now updated: See updates ticked 1 archived now TRUE, original 19-car extract safely archived, gone. Re-run analysis fuller data, update figure (filename, §8), log change, tag new version: now two citable versions: mtcars-am0-v1 (original) mtcars-full-v1 (update). first still reproduces original analysis exactly, even though data moved , whole point.
code: cars_v2 <- mtcars          # the full dataset now, both transmission types nrow(cars_v2)              # 32 cars, up from 19 #> [1] 32 write.csv(cars_v2, "data/cars.csv", row.names = TRUE)  eri_artifact_upload(   local_path  = "data/cars.csv",   name        = "mtcars_study_data",      # same name → updates the existing artifact   type        = "study_data",   description = "mtcars fuel-economy study data, all transmissions (expanded).",   version     = "2.0" ) eri_research_pull(path = "artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv", dest = "data") #> ℹ Archived 1 prior version to data/_archive/20260701T090000Z. #> ✔ Pulled 1 file from artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv to data.  eri_research_status() #> ── Research project: mtcars_study (us/demo) ── #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   kind  source                                          pulled_at            updates archived #>   <chr> <chr>                                           <chr>                  <int> <lgl> #> 1 pull  artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv 2026-07-01T09:00:00Z       1 TRUE cars <- mtcars model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = cars) coef(model) #>   (Intercept)            wt            hp          disp  #> 37.1055052690 -3.8008905826 -0.0311565508 -0.0009370091 library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(cars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +   labs(title = "Fuel economy vs. weight (all cars)") ggsave("figs/mpg_vs_weight.png", p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 150)  # same name = update  eri_research_upload_output(model, "mtcars_lm.qs2") eri_research_upload_figure("figs/mpg_vs_weight.png",                            caption = "MPG vs. weight, all cars (expanded dataset).") eri_research_log("Expanded to the full dataset (32 cars). Re-fit and updated the figure.")  # Snapshot the new data first, so the tag binds *this* (full) dataset rather than the # earlier "before-break" snapshot. eri_research_snapshot(label = "full-data") eri_research_tag(   "mtcars-full-v1",   description = "Re-run on the full dataset (all transmissions)." )

Real Carter Center data different, country surveillance study data must never deleted casually, copied Azure, committed git. discipline guide builds (data stays Azure, pulled provenance, frozen tags) exactly keeps protected data safe. doubt, ask delete.
code: con <- get_azure_storage_connection(storage_name = "data")  # eri_dir_delete() removes a non-empty directory recursively in one call, the # in-package path, so cleanup stays in erifunctions instead of raw AzureStor. eri_dir_delete("research/mtcars_study", azcontainer = con) eri_dir_delete("artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data", azcontainer = con) eri_research_list()       # mtcars_study should be gone eri_artifact_list()       # mtcars_study_data should be gone  unlink("../mtcars_study", recursive = TRUE)   # remove the local project folder

what: 2. Learn the one idea: how data is addressed
title: Getting started with erifunctions
text: Every dataset lives data/ Azure blob five-axis path (ADR-0012): idea makes whole system click: data arrives (data_source) separate measures (data_type). surveillance channel case line-list one country aggregate weekly count another; one CMR fans treatment, mmdp, survey measures across diseases. Run eri_data_model() , prints whole vocabulary, never guess value: Everything moves three layers, raw/ (received) → staged/ (DQ-checked) → processed/ (canonical data whole team trusts). Nothing reaches processed/ without human calling eri_approve().
code: data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/ eri_data_model() #> ── Data-addressing model (ADR-0012) ── #> Path: 'data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/' #> #> ── data_source (channel / how the data arrives) ── #> • surveillance -- Direct disease-output feed (e.g. a MoH surveillance system). #> • programmatic -- Programmatic activity/coverage data (CMR, MDA feeds); spans diseases. #> • research     -- Research surveys/studies; DA-managed, flexible measure. #>   (… plus the measures, formats, and layers, and the transitional cmr/odk tokens.)

what: Days 2–4, The ingestion spine (do it live)
title: Onboarding a new Data Analyst
text: Run eri_do() sandbox ; ’s guided console wizard, pick country, pick file (ODK project/form), confirm period, function names memorize. Stop checkpoint, read matching guide happened underneath, scripting steps . Connections guide, connect four services. eri_do() → “Onboard new country, disease, data type” → stand new atlantis space. read onboarding guide fill schema’s TODOs. Checkpoint: scaffolded space validated schema. eri_do() → “Bring surveillance dataset” → raw → DQ → staged → approve. See ingest guide ’s happening underneath. Checkpoint: approved dataset saw catalog. eri_do() → “Bring month’s country report (CMR) system” → upload → split → approve. See CMR guide ’s happening underneath. Checkpoint: split CMR per disease. eri_do() → “Pull ODK survey submissions” → connect → register → sync. read ODK guide monitoring survey managing app users, wizard doesn’t cover. Checkpoint: synced submissions pipeline. Triage log backlog, triage resolve log. Checkpoint: closed item backlog. ✅ Gate: can take file three channels processed/ sandbox.

title: Orientation: the data system at a glance
text: ERI team’s R package, API TCC’s Azure data system across countries (Haiti, DR, Uganda, OEPA, …) diseases (malaria, oncho, LF, SCH, STH). domain expert, software developer: install call functions, don’t edit package. Every function built make work clearer reliable.

what: The data lives in three layers
title: Orientation: the data system at a glance
text: Data flows one way: raw → staged → processed. Raw untouched; staged cleaned provisional; processed canonical. direction never reverses.
code: data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/    raw/        as-received from the source    staged/     DQ-checked, awaiting sign-off    processed/  analyst-approved, the data the whole team trusts

title: Orientation: the data system at a glance
text: Nothing reaches processed/ without eri_approve(), human gate. eri_approve() moves staged → processed, writes approval log, registers file catalog. gate: nothing “official” sign , sign-stamped ERI_ANALYST_ID. Never hand-edit delete processed/ data.

title: Orientation: the data system at a glance
text: data_source = channel (arrives): surveillance · programmatic · research data_type = measure (counts): case · aggregate · treatment · mmdp · survey · … Run eri_data_model() print live vocabulary. Channel measure independent, channel carries different measures, (channel, disease) differs country (DR malaria surveillance case; Haiti malaria surveillance aggregate). missing combination normal, warns, doesn’t error. data-model card deep reference.
code: data / country / disease / data_source / data_type / layer         uga      oncho     programmatic  treatment   staged

what: Three golden rules to leave with
title: Orientation: the data system at a glance
text: gate sacred. Nothing real eri_approve(); never touch processed/ hand. Secrets live .Renviron: never script, never git. Set ERI_ANALYST_ID first. Real country data never leaves system. Practice sandbox; protect real thing.

title: SharePoint Workflow
text: eri_sharepoint_connect() opens browser window interactive authentication first time. token cached AzureAuth subsequent calls within session re-prompt.
code: library(erifunctions)  site <- eri_sharepoint_connect("https://cartercenter.sharepoint.com/sites/ERI")

what: 2. List files in a document library folder
title: SharePoint Workflow
text: Filter find recent weekly upload:
code: # List the root of the default document library files <- eri_sharepoint_list(site)  # List a specific subfolder weekly_files <- eri_sharepoint_list(site, "Shared Documents/Weekly Data/Haiti") print(weekly_files) # A tibble with columns: name, size, modified, is_folder, path library(dplyr)  latest <- weekly_files |>   filter(!is_folder, grepl("\\.xlsx$", name)) |>   arrange(desc(modified)) |>   slice(1)  cat("Latest file:", latest$name, "\n") cat("Modified:   ", format(latest$modified), "\n")

what: 3. Read a file directly into R
title: SharePoint Workflow
text: eri_sharepoint_read() downloads file temp location reads based extension (.xlsx, .csv, .parquet, .rds). unknown extension function returns local temp path can read manually:
code: # Read an Excel file df <- eri_sharepoint_read(site, "Shared Documents/Weekly Data/Haiti/ht_weekly_2024.xlsx") head(df) # Read a CSV file with extra readr arguments df_csv <- eri_sharepoint_read(   site,   "Shared Documents/Weekly Data/DR/dr_cases_2024.csv",   col_types = readr::cols(.default = "c") ) # Read a specific Excel sheet df_sheet <- eri_sharepoint_read(   site,   "Shared Documents/Reports/annual_summary.xlsx",   sheet = "Malaria" ) path <- eri_sharepoint_read(site, "Shared Documents/Data/custom_format.sav") # path is a character string -- use haven::read_sav(path) or similar

what: 4. Upload a finished report to SharePoint
title: SharePoint Workflow
text: destination folder created automatically exist. Use overwrite = FALSE prevent accidentally replacing file: function returns SharePoint item URL invisibly:
code: # Push a local Excel report to the team SharePoint eri_sharepoint_upload(   local_path  = "outputs/ht_malaria_weekly_summary.xlsx",   site        = site,   folder_path = "Shared Documents/Reports/Haiti/2024" ) eri_sharepoint_upload(   local_path  = "outputs/ht_malaria_weekly_summary.xlsx",   site        = site,   folder_path = "Shared Documents/Reports/Haiti/2024",   overwrite   = FALSE   # errors if the file already exists ) url <- eri_sharepoint_upload("outputs/report.xlsx", site, "Shared Documents/Reports") cat("Shared at:", url, "\n")

title: SharePoint Workflow
text: common pattern : pull data SharePoint, run DQ checks, generate report, push report back.
code: library(erifunctions) library(dplyr)  # ---- Step 1: connect ---- site <- eri_sharepoint_connect("https://cartercenter.sharepoint.com/sites/ERI")  # ---- Step 2: pull the latest weekly submission ---- files <- eri_sharepoint_list(site, "Shared Documents/Weekly Data/Haiti") latest_path <- files |>   filter(!is_folder, grepl("\\.xlsx$", name)) |>   arrange(desc(modified)) |>   slice(1) |>   pull(path)  raw <- eri_sharepoint_read(site, latest_path)  # ---- Step 3: run DQ checks ---- schema <- load_dq_schema("haiti", "malaria", azcontainer = NULL) result <- run_dq_checks(raw, schema)  # ---- Step 4: generate a summary Excel report ---- tmp_report <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx") eri_report_excel(   list(     data   = list(data = result$data),     flags  = list(data = result$flags)   ),   path = tmp_report )  # ---- Step 5: push back to SharePoint ---- week_label <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-W%V") eri_sharepoint_upload(   local_path  = tmp_report,   site        = site,   folder_path = paste0("Shared Documents/DQ Reports/Haiti/", week_label) )

title: SharePoint Workflow
text: Tokens cached ~/.AzureR/ AzureAuth. prompted every session, check ~/.AzureR/ writable. scripted / CI use, prefer service-principal auth configured Microsoft365R::create_sharepoint_site() auth_type = "client_credentials". eri_sharepoint_* functions guard Microsoft365R installed give clear install instruction absent.

title: Spatial Workflow
text: Admin boundaries stored Azure spatial/{country}/adm{level}.rds validated sf objects. Levels follow standard hierarchy: 0 = country, 1 = department/region, 2 = province/commune, 3 = municipality/locality, 4 = sub-locality. Every object loaded Azure validated : - defined CRS (WGS 84, EPSG 4326) - empty geometries - canonical name column adm{level}_name
code: library(erifunctions)  # Load Haiti commune (adm2) boundaries communes <- eri_spatial_load("ht", level = 2)  # Load DR province (adm2) boundaries provinces <- eri_spatial_load("dr", level = 2)

title: Spatial Workflow
text: adding new country updating boundaries, use eri_spatial_upload(). validates shapefile accepting upload: function blocks descriptive error validation check fails, explaining exactly fix.
code: # Prepare your shapefile first: #   - Set CRS to 4326 if not already set #   - Rename the admin name column to adm2_name #   - Remove any empty geometries  eri_spatial_upload(   local_path = "data/shapefiles/hti_adm2.shp",   country    = "ht",   level      = 2 )

what: Spatial join: GPS coordinates to admin units
title: Spatial Workflow
text: Use eri_spatial_join() assign admin unit names point data lat/lon columns. Rows missing coordinates dropped warning. result plain tibble, geometry dropped automatically, downstream analysis need handle sf objects.
code: # Load the reference boundary communes <- eri_spatial_load("ht", level = 2)  # Attach commune and department names to case records case_data <- eri_spatial_join(   data       = raw_cases,   lat_col    = "latitude",   lon_col    = "longitude",   shapefile  = communes,   admin_cols = c("adm2_name", "adm1_name") )

title: Spatial Workflow
text: composing maps often useful add buffer around study area boundary features fully visible. eri_bbox_expand() takes sf bounding box expands specified distance metres:
code: library(sf)  haiti <- eri_spatial_load("ht", level = 0) bbox  <- sf::st_bbox(haiti) |>   eri_bbox_expand(X = 30000, Y = 30000)   # 30 km padding on all sides  # Use the expanded bbox as a crop/map extent

title: Spatial Workflow
text: Combine eri_spatial_load() eri_brand_ggplot_theme() standard ggplot2 / sf functions produce branded choropleth maps.
code: library(ggplot2) library(sf)  # Aggregate case counts by commune case_counts <- dplyr::count(case_data, adm2_name, name = "n_cases")  # Join to boundaries map_data <- dplyr::left_join(communes, case_counts, by = "adm2_name")  ggplot(map_data) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = n_cases), colour = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +   scale_fill_gradient(     low  = "#E8F4FD",     high = eri_brand_colors()[["navy"]],     na.value = "grey90",     name = "Cases"   ) +   labs(title = "Malaria cases by commune") +   eri_brand_ggplot_theme()

title: Spatial Workflow
text: LandScan global population rasters stored Azure spatial/landscan/. Use eri_spatial_load_landscan() (R/spatial.R) load raster given year, extract population admin unit using exactextractr package:
code: library(terra) library(exactextractr)  pop_raster <- eri_spatial_load_landscan(year = 2023) communes   <- eri_spatial_load("ht", level = 2)  # Sum population pixels within each commune communes$population <- exact_extract(pop_raster, communes, "sum")

title: Spatial Workflow
text: OEPA onchocerciasis data, eri_oncho_status_map() wraps full mapping pipeline, joins status data shapefile, applies standard ERI programme-status colour palette, adds scale bar north arrow:
code: eus     <- eri_spatial_load("oepa", level = 2)  # evaluation unit boundaries status  <- read_dq_result("outputs/oepa_status_2024.parquet")  map <- eri_oncho_status_map(   shapefile   = eus,   status_data = status,   eu_col      = "eu_name",   status_col  = "programme_status",   title       = "OEPA Onchocerciasis Programme Status 2024" )  eri_pptx_create() |>   eri_pptx_add_plot(map, title = "Programme Status") |>   eri_pptx_save("outputs/oepa_status_report.pptx")

title: Troubleshooting card
text: First move Azure/ODK error: confirm ’re connected (eri_list(...), list_odk_projects(con)) .Renviron loaded (Sys.getenv("ERI_ANALYST_ID")). “errors” expired session unset variable.

what: Working the log backlog (task: review errors & logs)
title: Troubleshooting card
text: Every operation leaves structured log Azure. eri_logs() shared backlog, analyst can see failed needs review close . eri_logs() hides handled items default, open list shrinks resolve. Persist DQ result’s flags eri_dq_log(result, country, disease, data_source, data_type = ) (note: data_source 4th argument, measure data_type 5th, mind source/measure order). Full walkthrough: log-triage guide.
code: eri_logs(status = "error")                 # the open error backlog (across the tree) eri_logs(status = "needs_review")          # DQ flags awaiting a human eri_logs(country = "uga", disease = "lf")  # scope to one dataset (faster)  # Inspect one, fix the underlying issue, then mark it handled: eri_logs_resolve(log_path, note = "re-ran ingest after fixing the date column")  eri_logs(include_handled = TRUE)           # confirm it's closed

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: criteria automated reviewer (.github/workflows/claude-review.yaml) uses. Edit file tune review — version-controlled purpose, way ADRs , review standard evolves project.

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: : advisory, independent check change fits project’s design conventions. reviewer write code read canon fresh time. Isn’t: correctness/test gate. R-CMD-check pkgdown CI pass/fail; re-litigate . Never approve merge — human makes final call. Raise questions directly review something ambiguous.

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: CLAUDE.md — conventions + “global vs local solution” guardrail. docs/roadmap.md — V2 phases (isn’t) meant deliver. docs/adr/ — accepted architecture decisions.

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: Reuse re-implementation. change re-implement something already exists (azure_io(), eri_read()/eri_write(), eri_data_path(), DQ/catalog/ research machinery)? Flag duplication; point existing helper. Global vs local solution. problem general (countries/diseases/users hit ), solved generally — one-buried single workflow? ADR-0002: metadata-store writes must concurrency-safe (ETag/optimistic), naive read-modify-write. ADR-0003: approver/actor identity comes verified token, self-declared env var. ADR-0005: pull--process; provenance recorded pull entry points — analytic helpers shouldn’t fetch implicitly. ADR-0006: research/analysis code lives separate repos, package source. change intentionally revisits decision, add/supersede ADR PR — flag doesn’t. Conventions (CLAUDE.md): exported eri_* / internal .eri_*; cli::cli_* user-facing messages; roxygen @examples (live calls \dontrun{}); one domain per file R/; NAMESPACE/man/ regenerated exports/roxygen change. User-first. Users Data Analysts Epidemiologists, developers — public surface clear, documented, hard misuse? Docs sync. change alters plan decision, docs/roadmap.md / ADRs updated PR?

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: Post one PR review. Use inline comments can point specific line. Request changes clear violations; otherwise comment. Keep concise cite specific doc/section (e.g. “ADR-0006”) maintainer can act quickly.

title: ERI team functions
text: Standardized data tools Epidemiology, Research Innovation (ERI) team Carter Center’s NTD malaria programs — way Data Analysts Epidemiologists work TCC’s Azure-centred data system across countries (Haiti, DR, Uganda, OEPA, …) diseases (malaria, oncho, LF, SCH, STH). install , authenticate browser, call functions — don’t edit package. Bringing monthly country report, surveillance dataset, ODK submissions? Standing new country disease? Run eri_do(). ’s guided console wizard — pick country, pick file (ODK project form), confirm month, walks whole upload/archive → stage → review → approve pipeline . also scaffolds brand-new country/disease space (schema template + Azure folders) ’re onboarding one first time. function names memorize, Azure path type hand. sure start otherwise? trying ? generated index ~30 common tasks; pick get call guide. console, eri_task_map() prints list. Otherwise, read Getting started first — ’s one idea every guide assumes.

title: ERI team functions
text: Bring surveillance data, CMR reports, ODK submissions           raw → staged → approved pipeline, keep DQ backlog clean,           turn approved data reports countries leadership can use. Connect Azure, ODK, SharePoint & Teams Onboard country / disease / data type Ingest surveillance dataset Triage log backlog Source approved data study, reconcile messy free-text places admin units,           QC extract epidemiological sense, compute programme indicators need           analysis. Connect Azure, ODK, SharePoint & Teams complete research workflow Reconcile localities admin units Catch anomalies analysis

what: How data moves through the system
title: ERI team functions
text: Every dataset lives data/ Azure blob five-axis path (ADR-0012): data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/. Three layers, one human gate — nothing reaches processed/ without call eri_approve(). Run eri_data_model() see full vocabulary — data_source data_type values known, never guess one.

title: ERI team functions
text: Azure access zero-config — first command needs opens browser sign . See connections guide ODK, SharePoint, Teams setup, full reference index every function grouped ’re reach . See V2 roadmap architecture decision records going reasoning behind key design choices. source, issues, full README (installation details, environment variables, contributing), see GitHub repository.
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions")  # Pin the version in your analysis project (recommended) renv::install("thecartercenter/erifunctions") renv::snapshot()

what: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
text: Evaluates named consistency rules schema's consistency block flags rows rule violated. rule specifies lhs column, comparison op, either rhs column rhs_value constant. Schema format (add consistency: block YAML schema):   Supported operators: <=, >=, ==, <, >, !=. Missing values (NA) either operand skip check row. Works plain tibble (returns tibble violations) dq_result (appends violations $flags).
code: consistency:   positives_le_tested:     lhs: NumMicroPos     op: "<="     rhs: NumTestedMicro     message: "Positive cases exceed tested"   age_non_negative:     lhs: Age     op: ">="     rhs_value: 0     message: "Age is negative"

title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
code: add_anomaly_consistency(data, schema)

title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
text: data tibble dq_result object. schema Named list load_dq_schema().

title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
text: tibble violations columns row, column, value, issue (includes rule name message). input dq_result, violations appended $flags updated dq_result returned. Returns empty tibble rules pass.

title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ schema <- load_dq_schema("haiti", "malaria") run_dq_checks(data, schema) |> add_anomaly_consistency(schema) } # }

what: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
text: Identifies gaps time series inferring full expected sequence periods observed minimum maximum, returning rows missing periods. Works plain tibble dq_result object. Supports two period types: "week" — expects contiguous integers 1–53 within year. gap year boundary (week 52/53 → week 1 next year) handled correctly. "month" — expects contiguous integers 1–12 within year.

title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
code: add_anomaly_gaps(   data,   period_col,   period_type = c("week", "month"),   group_cols = NULL,   year_col = NULL )

title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
text: data tibble dq_result object. period_col str Column containing period value (integer week 1–53 month 1–12). period_type str One "week" "month". group_cols chr vector columns check gaps within group (e.g. c("Province_Residence")). Default NULL checks full dataset. year_col str NULL Column containing year. Required period_type = "week" "month" detect cross-year gaps.

title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
text: tibble missing periods columns year (year_col supplied), period, group_cols, issue = "structural_gap". input dq_result, missing-period rows also appended $flags (row = NA). Returns empty tibble gaps found.

title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gaps <- add_anomaly_gaps(agg_data, "EpiWeek", "week",                           group_cols = "Province_Residence", year_col = "Year") } # }

what: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
text: Computes period--period percent change numeric column flags rows whose absolute change exceeds threshold. Works plain tibble dq_result object returned run_dq_checks(), enabling chaining:   passed dq_result, anomaly rows appended $flags percent-change columns added $data.
code: run_dq_checks(data, schema) |>   add_anomaly_pct_change("n_cases", "EpiWeek", group_cols = "Province_Residence")

title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
code: add_anomaly_pct_change(   data,   value_col,   period_col,   threshold = 0.5,   group_cols = NULL,   year_col = NULL )

title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
text: data tibble dq_result object. value_col str Name numeric column check. period_col str Name column defines time order within group (e.g. "EpiWeek", "month"). threshold num Absolute percent change threshold (proportion). Default 0.5 flags changes greater 50%. group_cols chr vector column names group computing change (e.g. c("Province_Residence", "disease")). Default NULL treats whole dataset one group. year_col str NULL period_col resets year (e.g. "EpiWeek" 1–53), supply year column ordering correct across year boundaries. Default NULL.

title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
text: input object two columns added data: pct_change_{value_col} (numeric) anomaly_pct_change_{value_col} (logical). input dq_result, flagged rows also appended $flags.

title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ agg <- dplyr::count(raw_dr, Year, EpiWeek, Province_Residence, name = "n_cases") agg_flagged <- add_anomaly_pct_change(agg, "n_cases", "EpiWeek",                                        group_cols = "Province_Residence",                                        year_col   = "Year") } # }

text: plain tibble: flags tibble columns row, column, value, issue (structure $flags dq_result). dq_result: object mismatches appended $flags.

what: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
text: function serves primary way interact Azure system R. can read, write, create folders, check whether file folder exists, upload files, list files folder.

title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
code: azure_io(   io,   file_loc = NULL,   obj = NULL,   azcontainer = suppressMessages(get_azure_storage_connection()),   force_delete = FALSE,   local_path = NULL,   progress = FALSE,   ... )

title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
text: io str type operation perform Azure "read" Read file Azure, must rds, csv, rda, xls/xlsx file. "write" Write file Azure, must rds, csv, rda, xls/xlsx file. write Excel file multiple sheets, pass named list containing tibbles interest. See examples. "exists.dir" Returns boolean checking see folder exists. "exists.file"Returns boolean checking see file exists. "create" Creates folder preceding folders. "list" Returns tibble objects folder. "upload" Moves file type Azure "delete" Deletes file. "delete.dir" Deletes folder. file_loc str Location "read", "write", "exists.dir", "exists.file", "create" "list". obj robj Object saved, needed "write". Defaults NULL. azcontainer Azure container object returned get_azure_storage_connection(). force_delete logical Use delete io without confirmation prompt. Default FALSE. local_path str Local file pathway upload file Azure. Default NULL. parameter required passing "upload" io parameter. progress logical Show AzureStor's byte progress bar transfer. Default FALSE (suppressed). Set TRUE large single read/upload needs visible feedback. ... Optional parameters work readr::read_delim(), readxl::read_excel(), ggplot2::ggsave().

title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
text: Output dependent argument passed io parameter.

title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- azure_io("read", file_loc = "df1.csv") df2 <- azure_io("read", file_loc = "df2.xlsx", sheet = 1, skip = 2) list_of_df <- list(df_1 = df, df_2 = df) azure_io("write", file_loc = "Data/test/df.csv", obj = df) azure_io("write", file_loc = "Data/test/df.xlsx", obj = list_of_df) azure_io("exists.dir", "Data/nonexistentfolder") azure_io("exists.file", file_loc = "Data/test/df1.csv") azure_io("create", "Data/nonexistentfolder") azure_io("list") azure_io("upload", file_loc = "Data/test", local_path = "C:/Users/ABC1/Desktop/df2.csv") } # }

title: Resolve the analyst identity for governed actions and audit logs — .eri_analyst_id

Otherwise (service-principal connections user claim) returns ERI_ANALYST_ID set. , behaviour depends ERI_REQUIRE_ANALYST_ID: truthy (1/true/yes/), governed actions refused (error). Otherwise falls back "<os-username> (unverified)" — OS account, explicitly marked shared audit trail records attribution provisional rather real analyst id — warns per R session (via options(erifunctions.warned_analyst_id)).

what: Approve staged data and promote it to processed — eri_approve
title: Approve staged data and promote it to processed — eri_approve
text: human approval gate three-layer pipeline. Finds files staged/ directory whose names contain period, moves processed/, writes YAML approval log alongside . Analyst identity read ERI_ANALYST_ID environment variable. unset, approver recorded "<os-username> (unverified)" (one-time warning emitted fallback silent); set ERI_REQUIRE_ANALYST_ID truthy refuse approving without configured identity instead. operation log capturing every step (including errors) always written {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/logs/ data container, regardless whether approval succeeds fails. log primary debugging artifact pipeline issues.

NULL (default) approves legacy four-axis path {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/... without measure level, catalog entry's measure recorded NA — one-time per-session note points deliberate choice, silent omission. azcontainer Azure container object data/ blob, returned get_azure_storage_connection(). NULL (default), connects automatically using ERIFUNCTIONS_DATA_STORAGE_NAME.

what: Approve every disease/measure one CMR workbook routed to, in one call — eri_approve_cmr
title: Approve every disease/measure one CMR workbook routed to, in one call — eri_approve_cmr
text: eri_approve() promotes one (disease, data_type) time, one CMR workbook fans many via eri_split_cmr(). looks got routed country/period (via eri_cmr_last_plan() plan supplied), checks every measure's DQ-flag log, none outstanding item – either unresolved flags never DQ-checked period – approves every measure one call. anything outstanding, nothing approved. explicit human-review gate CMR data; point DA accidentally approve past unreviewed measure looping blindly. Instead get back task list: one row per measure still needing attention. Review , close eri_logs_resolve() (passing /decided via note argument), re-run function – re-checks scratch time. stale flag keeps blocking explicitly resolved. checks every dq_flags log entry period, just recent one: earlier eri_dq_log() run unresolved flags later rerun period came back "clean", earlier entry still blocks approval eri_logs_resolve() . deliberate (unreviewed flag silently superseded fresh "clean" run), mean truly stale/superseded flag needs explicit note clear, just clean recheck. force = TRUE approves anyway, rare case DA needs promote data despite outstanding measure (e.g. genuine template quirk never resolve cleanly, deadline). requires non-empty justification – confirmation prompt , since scriptable core work unattended scripts/CI; interactive wrapper right place extra human friction (e.g. "type period confirm"), function. Every bypassed measure's dq_flags entry (one exists) annotated handled via eri_logs_resolve() forced = TRUE note pointing back approval's log – open backlog stays clean without pretending flag ever actually reviewed, eri_audit() renders whole thing prominently rather folding ordinary approval.

title: Approve every disease/measure one CMR workbook routed to, in one call — eri_approve_cmr

NULL (default) looks via eri_cmr_last_plan(). data_con Azure container data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically. force lgl Approve even measures outstanding. Default FALSE. Requires justification. justification chr NULL Required (non-empty) force = TRUE: approval going despite outstanding. Recorded approval's log ignored force = FALSE.

title: Approve every disease/measure one CMR workbook routed to, in one call — eri_approve_cmr
text: Invisibly, tibble: everything clean (force = TRUE), one row per (disease, data_type) got approved; anything outstanding force = FALSE, one row per (disease, data_type) still needing attention (log_path/issue) nothing approved.

what: Reconstruct a chronological audit trail for a dataset — eri_audit
title: Reconstruct a chronological audit trail for a dataset — eri_audit
text: Walks every log entry across given axes (eri_logs()'s discovery logic) explodes one row per meaningful event – file staged, CMR workbook split (routing plan), DQ check run, individual flag resolved, whole log entry closed via eri_logs_resolve(), approval – single chronological timeline, oldest first (timeline reads forward; triage backlog eri_logs() reads newest-first — different jobs, different order). eri_approve_cmr() already records dq_flags entries backed approval (dq_reviewed field); function cashes — log_path stays every row power user can still drill raw YAML, nobody follow paths hand answer "happened dataset, signed ." forced approval (eri_approve_cmr()'s force = TRUE) bypass annotations leaves measures overrode render prominently (forced = TRUE, printed red) rather folding ordinary event — one thing trail genuine review. CMR-specific entry point needed: leaving disease/data_source/ data_type NULL (default) already enumerates every disease/channel/ measure country — CMR workbook naturally includes rblf/cmr split/approve logs every fanned-measure's logs.

title: Reconstruct a chronological audit trail for a dataset — eri_audit
code: eri_audit(   country,   disease = NULL,   data_source = NULL,   data_type = NULL,   period = NULL,   data_con = NULL )

title: Reconstruct a chronological audit trail for a dataset — eri_audit
text: country chr Country code (e.g. "sdn"). Required — audit trail country try reconstruct system-wide timeline, job function scoped . disease, data_source, data_type chr NULL Narrow ; left NULL enumerated blob (scoping eri_logs()). period chr NULL Restrict one reporting period. Matched exact string value recorded entry (codebase single canonical period format across sources — CMR periods look like "202605", surveillance periods like "2024-01"); nothing matches scope contain events, message lists periods actually found, format mismatch diagnosable. data_con Azure container data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically.

title: Reconstruct a chronological audit trail for a dataset — eri_audit
text: tibble, oldest first, columns timestamp, event, actor, detail, log_path, country, disease, data_source, data_type, period, source_hash (MD5 identity hash source file entry's operation ran , one recorded — lets trace exact bytes behind given step without opening raw YAML), forced (lgl; TRUE forced eri_approve_cmr() approval bypass annotation left behind). Class eri_audit_trail; printing renders cli-formatted timeline — tibble still API (filter, join, whatever need).

title: Reconstruct a chronological audit trail for a dataset — eri_audit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ eri_audit("sdn", period = "202605")                      # a whole CMR period eri_audit("sdn", "oncho", "programmatic", "treatment")   # one measure, all periods } # }
