what: Reconcile two datasets and report the differences — eri_compare
title: Reconcile two datasets and report the differences — eri_compare
text: Compares new dataset old one reports schema, row, value differences. Built Phase 3 cutover: prove eri_ingest()'s data/staged output matches legacy projects/intermediate (hsp-mal) output parallel run, switch-rests evidence.

title: Reconcile two datasets and report the differences — eri_compare
code: eri_compare(   new,   old,   by = NULL,   ignore = NULL,   tolerance = 0,   strict_schema = TRUE,   new_con = NULL,   old_con = NULL )

title: Reconcile two datasets and report the differences — eri_compare
text: new data frame, single Azure blob path read eri_read() (defaults data blob). candidate / new-pipeline output. old data frame, single Azure blob path read eri_read() (defaults projects blob). reference / legacy output. chr NULL Key column(s) uniquely identify row datasets. Required per-cell value reconciliation; must unique. ignore chr NULL Columns drop sides comparing (e.g. run timestamp expected differ). tolerance num Absolute tolerance numeric columns; |new - old| within tolerance counts equal. Default 0 (exact). strict_schema lgl TRUE (default), added/dropped columns type mismatches count equivalent. FALSE, columns present new tolerated (value/row parity alone gates equivalence); dropped columns type mismatches still count. See Details. new_con, old_con Azure containers used new/old paths. NULL, connect automatically (new → data blob, old → projects blob).

title: Reconcile two datasets and report the differences — eri_compare
text: eri_comparison object (list) equivalent (logical), summary, schema (added/dropped/type_mismatch), rows (added/dropped key tibbles), values (tibble per-cell mismatches). print() method.

title: Reconcile two datasets and report the differences — eri_compare
text: key columns () reconciles row--row: keys added dropped, — keys present — exactly cells differ. Without still reports schema diff set-based row membership, pinpoint per-cell value changes. key present datasets never counted added dropped — possible value mismatch. rows$added / rows$dropped purely key-membership deltas, values holds -key-different-value cells. equivalent always requires rows values match. Whether column difference also breaks equivalence governed strict_schema: default (TRUE) added dropped column, type mismatch, makes FALSE. Set strict_schema = FALSE gate value/row parity alone tolerating extra columns new (e.g. provenance new pipeline adds legacy mirror never ) — dropped columns type mismatches still count, since genuine regressions. Numeric classes (integer/double) treated one type, never flag mismatch .

title: Reconcile two datasets and report the differences — eri_compare
code: a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, n = c(10, 20, 30), site = c("x", "y", "z")) b <- data.frame(id = 1:3, n = c(10, 21, 30), site = c("x", "y", "z")) eri_compare(a, b, by = "id")            # one value mismatch on id 2 #> ✖ Not equivalent. #> Rows: 3 new · 3 old · 3 matched · 0 added · 0 dropped #> Values: 1 cell mismatch across 1 row (column: "n") #> ℹ Inspect `$rows$added`, `$rows$dropped`, `$values`, `$schema`.  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # New staged output vs the legacy mirror, read straight from the blobs eri_compare(   new = "uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/staged/2024_06.parquet",   old = "health-rb-country-expansion-dev/intermediate/uga/2024_06.parquet",   by  = c("admin2", "period") ) } # }

what: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
title: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
text: Prints (returns invisibly) registry known values five-axis canonical path data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/ (ADR-0012): data_source channels, data_type measures, input formats, pipeline layers. New sources/measures added registry onboarding; unregistered value warns rather errors.

title: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
code: eri_data_model()

title: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
text: Invisibly, registry named list.

title: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
code: eri_data_model() #>  #> ── Data-addressing model (ADR-0012) ──────────────────────────────────────────── #> Path: data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/ #>  #> ── data_source (channel / how the data arrives) ── #>  #> • surveillance -- Direct disease-output feed (e.g. a Ministry-of-Health #>   surveillance system). #> • programmatic -- Programmatic activity/coverage data (country-team CMR, MoH #>   MDA feeds); spans diseases. #> • research -- Research surveys/studies (household or community level); #>   DA-managed, flexible measure. #> • cmr -- (transitional, legacy reads only) Old CMR source token; new CMR writes #>   use `programmatic` + format: cmr. #> • odk -- (transitional, legacy reads only) Old ODK source token; new ODK writes #>   use `research` + format: odk. #>  #> ── data_type (the measure / what it captures) ── #>  #> • case -- Individual case records (one row per patient). #> • aggregate -- Aggregated counts (one row per place/period). #> • treatment -- MDA / treatment coverage (target & treated). #> • mmdp -- Morbidity management & disability prevention (e.g. LF #>   hydrocele/lymphoedema). #> • training -- Programmatic training activity (CDD / CS / MMDP). #> • survey -- Programmatic survey (examined / positive). #> • tas -- Transmission assessment survey (LF). #> • prevalence -- Prevalence survey (e.g. Kato-Katz, skin snip). #> • entomology -- Vector / entomological surveillance (e.g. larval prospection). #>  #> ── format (input shape of a programmatic source) ── #>  #> • cmr -- Country Monitoring Report Excel template (a programmatic input #>   format). #> • moh_feed -- A direct Ministry-of-Health data feed (a programmatic input #>   format that is not a CMR). #> • odk -- ODK Central survey instrument (a research collection format). #>  #> ── layer ── #>  #> "raw", "staged", and "processed"

what: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
title: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
text: Constructs canonical blob storage path following erifunctions five-axis data model (ADR-0012): {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/, data_source channel (data arrives) data_type measure (captures). Use instead hard-coding path strings. legacy four-axis form eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, layer[, filename]) still accepted ADR-0012 migration builds measure-less {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{layer}/ path — detected fourth argument layer keyword (data_type measure never ).

title: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
text: country str Country code (e.g. "dr", "ht", "uga"). disease str Disease name (e.g. "malaria", "lf", "oncho"). data_source str channel: "surveillance", "programmatic", "research" (extensible — see eri_data_model(); unknown values warn). data_type str measure: "case", "aggregate", "treatment", "tas", ... (extensible; unknown values warn). layer str Pipeline layer: "raw", "staged", "processed". filename str Optional filename append. NULL (default), returns directory path .

title: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
text: character string canonical blob path.

what: Bring a monthly report into the system, interactively (the guided console front door) — eri_do
title: Bring a monthly report into the system, interactively (the guided console front door) — eri_do
text: menu-driven wizard carries Data Analyst entire pipeline run – country, file, reporting period – calls existing scriptable core behalf. Never asks function name Azure path. mirror_pipeline auto-detected eri_cutover_status() never asked . Every mutation one already-tested function (eri_upload(), eri_stage_cmr(), eri_split_cmr(), eri_ingest(), eri_approve(), eri_odk_register(), eri_odk_sync(), eri_onboard_country(), eri_onboard_cmr(), eri_onboard_disease()), data quality review/approval hands directly loop eri_dq_review() uses (CMR) points scriptable triage tools directly (eri_logs(), eri_dq_flag_resolve(), surveillance ingest) – nothing reimplemented, every function calls stays fully usable directly script CI. Currently covers bringing monthly CMR report, bringing surveillance dataset (CSV/Excel line-list), pulling ODK survey submissions, onboarding new country, disease, data type, end end. ODK sync stops research/raw/ – automated stage--approve path ODK data yet (real guide shows manual eri_write() step) – onboarding stops schema template(s) written (, surveillance/CMR, Azure folders exist – NTD disease's MDA/prevalence schemas local-, eri_onboard_disease()'s design) – filling schema's disease-specific columns, validating , submitting via pull request stay human, judgment-driven step (see vignettes/da-onboard-guide.Rmd's "onboarding scaffolds; finish "). wizard honest handing points rather fabricating steps underlying tooling support automate. Interactive . script CI, use scriptable core directly: eri_upload(), eri_stage_cmr(), eri_split_cmr(), eri_cmr_dq_report(), eri_approve_cmr(), eri_ingest(), eri_approve(), eri_odk_sync(), eri_onboard_country().

what: Triage a single DQ flag within a logged eri_dq_log() entry — eri_dq_flag_resolve
title: Triage a single DQ flag within a logged eri_dq_log() entry — eri_dq_flag_resolve
text: Works one flag time rather entire DQ-log entry: marks specific flag "not_important", "fixed", "noted", optional note, DA can triage multi-flag entry (e.g. one CMR measure several issues) issue issue instead --nothing. Distinct eri_logs_resolve(), closes whole entry (marks handled, dropping open backlog / unblocking eri_approve_cmr()) – resolving every individual flag mark entry handled; call eri_logs_resolve() afterward (auto-summarize per-flag decisions pass note). Known limitation: single-editor assumption. read-modify-write whole log YAML optimistic-concurrency check (ETag/retry, unlike metadata-store writes catalog.R/odk_registry.R/artifacts.R). two people resolve different flags log entry around time, second write can silently overwrite first. Fine current one-DA-per- country-workbook CMR pilot; revisit assuming safe two people triaging measure's flags concurrently.

what: Interactively review and resolve a CMR workbook's DQ flags, then approve — eri_dq_review
title: Interactively review and resolve a CMR workbook's DQ flags, then approve — eri_dq_review
text: interactive front door scriptable DQ core built Phases 2-6 DQ workflow redesign: eri_cmr_dq_report() combined flags tibble, eri_dq_flag_resolve() eri_logs_resolve() triage, eri_dq_schema_edit()/eri_dq_schema_submit() schema fixes, eri_split_cmr() re-running corrected workbook, eri_approve_cmr() (including force = TRUE path) sign-. Every mutation goes one functions immediately – wrapper holds state beyond one -memory, per-call path cache (local workbook fixing session), closing laptop mid-review running eri_dq_review() later picks exactly log YAMLs say things . loop: clean -> offered approval; flagged -> work flags one time (fix source workbook, adjust schema, mark -important/noted), re-run, force-approve, print report, exit. CMR-now – plan machinery dispatches (eri_cmr_last_plan(), eri_split_cmr()) CMR-specific; generalizing ingest shapes later touches orchestration layer. Interactive . script CI, use scriptable core directly: eri_cmr_dq_report(), eri_dq_flag_resolve(), eri_logs_resolve(), eri_approve_cmr().

what: Submit a local DQ schema override for a maintainer to fold in — eri_dq_schema_submit
title: Submit a local DQ schema override for a maintainer to fold in — eri_dq_schema_submit
text: Packages live local schema override (eri_dq_schema_edit()) ticket via eri_feedback(): message auto-drafted, human-readable diff schema forked (maintainer never retype YAML prose description), full override file attached, four ADR-0012 axes plus schema's stem recorded context. Filed area = "dq". Submitting apply change anywhere else — files ticket. Folding means maintainer updates Azure schemas/ .yaml blob directly (load_dq_schema() already prefers Azure copy bundled one), takes effect every DA within minutes, next package release. local override keeps working independently (see eri_dq_schema_status()) reset upstream change retires .

what: Log a piece of feedback to the shared ticket log — eri_feedback
title: Log a piece of feedback to the shared ticket log — eri_feedback
text: Appends ticket _feedback/feedback_log.yaml data/ Azure blob — team's lightweight internal backlog. Use flag anything: bug, rough edge, wish, general comment, either system whole (area = "general") specific part (e.g. area = "odk").

title: Log a piece of feedback to the shared ticket log — eri_feedback
code: eri_feedback(   message,   area = "general",   context = NULL,   attachment = NULL,   data_con = NULL )

title: Log a piece of feedback to the shared ticket log — eri_feedback
text: message chr feedback . single non-empty string. area chr part system . "general" (default) system-wide feedback, specific section — suggested values: ingest, dq, catalog, query, odk, cmr, reporting, research, spatial, auth, docs, . Free text accepted; value lower-cased. context list NULL Optional named list scoping ticket specific dataset object (e.g. list(country = "sdn", disease = "oncho", data_source = "programmatic", data_type = "treatment", period = "202605", schema = "sdn_oncho_programmatic_treatment")). Stored sub-block ticket, new formal arguments, area can scope tickets differently without signature change. NULL (default) omits entirely — ticket context looks exactly like one filed feature existed. attachment chr NULL Optional path local file attach — e.g. full schema override dq ticket. Uploaded _feedback/attachments/{token}/{basename} data/ blob ticket logged, failed upload never leaves ticket referencing file actually . reverse known, accepted, low-probability gap: upload succeeds log append fails (e.g. exhausts concurrency retries), blob left orphaned ticket pointing — see error (nothing silently succeeds ), automatic cleanup sweep orphaned attachment. NULL (default): attachment. data_con Azure container object data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically.

title: Log a piece of feedback to the shared ticket log — eri_feedback
text: logged ticket (invisibly), named list.

title: Log a piece of feedback to the shared ticket log — eri_feedback
text: ticket records filed (verified signed-identity, self-declared name — ADR-0003) , given auto-incrementing id. Writes concurrency-safe (ADR-0002), two people filing never clobber . New tickets start status = "submitted"; moving ticket triage (planned, fixed, ...) handled separate tracking workflow, function.

title: Log a piece of feedback to the shared ticket log — eri_feedback
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # System-wide feedback eri_feedback("The onboarding guide's Week 1 felt too fast.")  # Feedback about a specific section eri_feedback("ODK sync timed out on the big LF form.", area = "odk")  # Scoped to a dataset, with an attachment (see eri_dq_schema_submit() for # the DA-facing wrapper that packages this automatically for schema edits) eri_feedback("District list is missing a valid admin name.", area = "dq",              context = list(country = "sdn", disease = "oncho"),              attachment = "sdn_oncho_programmatic_treatment.yaml") } # }

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
text: general analyst ingest entry point. Reads raw local file, archives original file -data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/raw/ (timestamp-suffixed, re-ingesting filename never collides earlier archive ), runs DQ checks via run_dq_checks(), prints flags, writes cleaned parquet data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/staged/ — feeding eri_approve() matching measure. runs data, including throwaway sandbox: pipeline-registry country gate default. raw archive logged DQ flags carry MD5 hash source file (identity, security) — reproducing old submission, answering "exact bytes review look ", depend whoever still copy original email/upload. legacy projects-blob dual-write (hsp-mal cutover comparison) opt-mirror: pass mirror_pipeline = "hsp-mal" additionally mirror cleaned output projects/{project_folder}/intermediate/{country_subfolder}/. transitional removed Phase-3 cutover (ADR-0012).

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
code: eri_ingest(   path,   country,   disease,   data_source = "surveillance",   data_type = "aggregate",   schema = NULL,   data_con = NULL,   mirror_pipeline = NULL,   projects_con = NULL )

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
text: path str Local path raw file ingest. country str Country code (e.g. "dr", "ht"). disease str Disease name (e.g. "malaria"). data_source str channel ("surveillance", "programmatic", "research"). Default "surveillance". data_type str NULL measure (e.g. "aggregate", "case", "treatment"). Selects DQ schema measure level staged path .../{data_source}/{data_type}/staged/. Default "aggregate". Whatever pass , promote measure — eri_approve(country, disease, data_source, period, data_type = <>) — approve look one level find nothing. NULL stages channel-level (four-axis), rare measure-less case. schema Named list load_dq_schema(). NULL (default), loaded (country, disease, data_source, data_type). data_con Azure container data blob. NULL (default), connects using ERIFUNCTIONS_DATA_STORAGE_NAME. mirror_pipeline str NULL set (e.g. "hsp-mal"), also mirror cleaned output legacy projects blob via pipeline registry entry. Default NULL (mirror; sandbox-safe). projects_con Azure container projects blob; used mirror_pipeline set. NULL, connects automatically.

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
text: Invisibly, dq_result object ($data, $log, $flags).

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default measure is "aggregate", so it stages to # dr/malaria/surveillance/aggregate/staged/ ... result <- eri_ingest("data/raw/dr_malaria_2024W01.xlsx", "dr", "malaria") result$flags  # review before approving # ... and the same measure promotes it: eri_approve("dr", "malaria", "surveillance", "2024W01", data_type = "aggregate") } # }

what: Read and parse a CMR monthly report Excel file — eri_ingest_cmr
title: Read and parse a CMR monthly report Excel file — eri_ingest_cmr
text: Reads single sheet Carter Center RBLF monthly report template, using machine-readable field code row (row 5 template) column names. Field codes (e.g. #rbtrt_year, #rbtrt_adm1) consistent across country templates regardless language, function parses English French templates.

title: Read and parse a CMR monthly report Excel file — eri_ingest_cmr
code: eri_ingest_cmr(path, sheet, country = NULL)

title: Read and parse a CMR monthly report Excel file — eri_ingest_cmr
text: path str Local path CMR Excel file. sheet str int Sheet name, 1-based index, canonical slug (e.g. "rb_treatment"). Slugs resolved actual sheet names via country schema's sheet_aliases block country supplied. country str NULL Optional country code (e.g. "tcd", "uga"). supplied, country code prepended country column slug aliases resolved. Default NULL.

title: Read and parse a CMR monthly report Excel file — eri_ingest_cmr
text: tibble field-code column names data row 6 onward, plus excel_row column recording row's real position workbook (survives -NA spacer-row dropping, stays accurate even rows removed). country supplied prepended country column.

title: Read and parse a CMR monthly report Excel file — eri_ingest_cmr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # English template — sheet name directly df <- eri_ingest_cmr("data/uga_2024_01.xlsx", sheet = "RB Treatment", country = "uga") # French template — canonical slug resolved via schema df <- eri_ingest_cmr("data/tcd_2024_01.xlsx", sheet = "rb_treatment", country = "tcd") } # }

what: Inject controllable anomalies into a clean dataset — eri_inject_anomalies
title: Inject controllable anomalies into a clean dataset — eri_inject_anomalies
text: Perturbs data frame chosen set realistic, reproducible anomalies — simulation-harness counterpart add_anomaly_* detectors. Use stand dirty "new data" otherwise-clean staged files data-quality reconciliation paths (run_dq_checks(), eri_compare()) genuinely exercised (roadmap Phase 3).

title: Inject controllable anomalies into a clean dataset — eri_inject_anomalies
code: eri_inject_anomalies(   data,   types = c("missing", "outlier", "negative", "typo", "duplicate", "drop"),   n = 1L,   cols = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Inject controllable anomalies into a clean dataset — eri_inject_anomalies
text: data data frame perturb. types chr anomalies inject. missing, outlier, negative, typo, duplicate, drop. Defaults . n int many anomalies inject per type (cells cell-level types, rows duplicate/drop). Capped available. Default 1. cols chr NULL Restrict cell-level types (missing, outlier, negative, typo) columns. NULL (default) auto-picks eligible columns per type (numeric outlier/negative, character/factor typo, missing). seed int NULL Optional RNG seed reproducible perturbation (set locally; global RNG state left untouched).

title: Inject controllable anomalies into a clean dataset — eri_inject_anomalies
text: data anomalies injected, plus "eri_anomalies" attribute (tibble changed).

title: Inject controllable anomalies into a clean dataset — eri_inject_anomalies
text: Anomaly types: missing — set cells NA. outlier — replace numeric cells extreme value. negative — make numeric cells implausibly negative (e.g. negative counts). typo — perturb character/factor cells (case, stray characters, whitespace). duplicate — duplicate whole rows. drop — remove whole rows. result carries "eri_anomalies" attribute: tibble logging every injection (type, row, column, original, new) — ground truth simulation can check detection . row row index input data. attribute -session : dropped moment frame written Parquet passed dplyr verbs, capture staging dirty data. duplicate rows appended, drop removes original rows, logged row indices stay valid. dirty data destined eri_compare(), pass cols keep cell-level types join keys — corrupting key changes row-matching rather producing detectable value anomaly.

title: Inject controllable anomalies into a clean dataset — eri_inject_anomalies
code: clean <- data.frame(   id = 1:10, cases = c(5, 8, 3, 6, 9, 4, 7, 2, 5, 8), site = letters[1:10] ) dirty <- eri_inject_anomalies(clean, types = c("missing", "outlier"), n = 2, seed = 1) #> ℹ Injected 4 anomalies: 2 missing and 2 outlier. attr(dirty, "eri_anomalies") #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   type      row column original new   #>   <chr>   <int> <chr>  <chr>    <chr> #> 1 missing     9 id     9        NA    #> 2 missing     2 id     2        NA    #> 3 outlier     4 id     4        4000  #> 4 outlier     1 id     1        1000

what: Pooled prevalence estimator for LF antigen surveys — eri_lf_pooled_prev
title: Pooled prevalence estimator for LF antigen surveys — eri_lf_pooled_prev
text: Implements standard formula estimating individual prevalence pooled test results: 1 - ((1 - npos/npool)^(1/pool_size)).

title: Pooled prevalence estimator for LF antigen surveys — eri_lf_pooled_prev
text: npos num Number positive pools. npool num Total number pools tested. pool_size num Number individuals per pool. chr vector NULL. Column name(s) enclosing data frame (use inside dplyr::mutate()) plain numeric vector equal length npos. NULL (default), returns single scalar.

title: Pooled prevalence estimator for LF antigen surveys — eri_lf_pooled_prev
text: numeric scalar (ungrouped) tibble columns plus pooled_prev (grouped).

title: Pooled prevalence estimator for LF antigen surveys — eri_lf_pooled_prev
text: supplied function returns one prevalence estimate per group (weighted mean pool-level estimates, weighted pool count). Matches formula pooled_prev.R.

what: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs
title: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs
text: Reads structured operation logs (written eri_ingest(), eri_approve(), eri_stage(), eri_odk_sync(), …) DQ-flag logs (written eri_dq_log()) {country}/{disease}/{data_source}[/{data_type}]/logs/ data/ Azure blob, returns triage backlog. Filter failures status = "error" data-quality items status = "needs_review", close items eri_logs_resolve().

title: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs
code: eri_logs(   country = NULL,   disease = NULL,   data_source = NULL,   data_type = NULL,   status = NULL,   operation = NULL,   analyst = NULL,   since = NULL,   include_handled = FALSE,   data_con = NULL )

title: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs
text: country, disease, data_source chr NULL Scope search country, disease, channel; left NULL enumerated blob. data_type chr NULL Scope single measure (five-axis layout). NULL reads channel-level logs every measure beneath . status chr NULL Filter status ("success", "error", "in_progress", "needs_review", "clean"). operation chr NULL Filter operation (e.g. "eri_approve", "dq_flags"). analyst chr NULL Filter analyst ran operation. since Date/chr NULL Keep logs date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD). include_handled lgl Include items already marked handled. Default FALSE. data_con Azure container data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically.

title: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs
text: tibble, newest first, columns: log_path, timestamp, operation, status, analyst, country, disease, data_source, data_type, period, summary, n_issues, handled, handled_by, handled_at.

title: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs
text: function scopes scan whichever axes supply enumerates rest blob; supply (country → disease → data_source → data_type), faster . reads four-axis channel-level logs five-axis measure-level logs (ADR-0012).

title: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs

what: Mark a log entry as handled — eri_logs_resolve
title: Mark a log entry as handled — eri_logs_resolve
text: Records triage note single log YAML (log_path eri_logs()), flagging handled drops open backlog. Adds triage block (handled, handled_by, handled_at, note, forced) file place; original operation record preserved.

title: Mark a log entry as handled — eri_logs_resolve
code: eri_logs_resolve(log_path, note = NULL, data_con = NULL, forced = FALSE)

title: Mark a log entry as handled — eri_logs_resolve
text: log_path chr Blob path log resolve (log_path column eri_logs()). note chr NULL optional note describing handled. data_con Azure container data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically. forced lgl Mark entry handled something else bypassed (e.g. eri_approve_cmr()'s force = TRUE path), actually reviewed resolved. Default FALSE. Distinguishes annotated bypass genuine resolution record – eri_audit() renders two differently.

title: Mark a log entry as handled — eri_logs_resolve
text: Invisibly, TRUE.

title: Mark a log entry as handled — eri_logs_resolve
text: single-editor caveat eri_dq_flag_resolve(): read-modify-write optimistic-concurrency protection, two people resolving log entry around time can silently clobber one another.

title: Mark a log entry as handled — eri_logs_resolve
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ backlog <- eri_logs(status = "error") eri_logs_resolve(backlog$log_path[1], note = "Re-ran after the source fixed the file.") } # }

title: Permanently remove an ODK form from the shared Azure registry — eri_odk_purge

Unlike eri_odk_deregister(), soft-deletes (active: false) preserves sync history, leaves trace. Use clean practice / sandbox registrations (otherwise linger inactive rows shared registry); real form prefer eri_odk_deregister(), keeps audit trail.

Forms repeat groups (real forms) export multiple tables – main submission table plus one child table per repeat group – written Parquet ({form_id}.parquet, {form_id}-{repeat}.parquet, ...); flat form writes single {form_id}.parquet. registry entry's last_synced timestamp updated success.

what: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: inverse eri_odk_sync() / download_odk_form(): take table already-collected records (paper backfill, legacy export, download_odk_form() result) create submissions existing published ODK Central form. One submission POSTed per row. See ADR-0013 design contract.

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
code: eri_odk_upload(   data,   project_id,   form_id,   con = NULL,   url = Sys.getenv("ODK_URL"),   auth = Sys.getenv("ODK_TOKEN"),   key_col = NULL,   mapping = NULL,   dry_run = FALSE,   data_con = NULL )

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: data file path (CSV/Excel, flat forms ), data.frame, named list tables (parent first, "{form_id}-{repeat}" child tables) – download_odk_form()(tables = TRUE) shape. project_id int ODK Central project ID. form_id chr ODK Central form ID (xmlFormId); form must published. con odk_connection init_odk_connection(), NULL use ODK_URL / ODK_TOKEN environment variables. url, auth chr Server URL / bearer token, used con = NULL. key_col chr Column name(s) whose values seed deterministic instanceID. NULL (default) hashes whole parent row. preserve original submission identity round-trip, pass id column (e.g. "KEY"). Names refer columns mapping applied. mapping Named character vector mapping input column headers field-column names, extracts whose headers already match form (e.g. paper CSV): c(village = "site_name", date_seen = "visit-date"). Targets use download_odk_form() flattening (group-field). rename applied parent every child table validation, columns list left -. NULL (default) maps nothing. dry_run lgl TRUE, run validation POST nothing; returns validation-issue tibble. data_con Azure container optional operation logging; NULL skips .

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: Invisibly: dry_run = TRUE, validation tibble (table, column, row, issue); otherwise per-row outcome tibble (instance_id, status created/skipped/failed, http_status, message).

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: Columns matched form fields name, using flattening download_odk_form() emits: field /data/visit/date column visit-date; repeat groups supplied separate child tables named "{form_id}-{repeat}" linked parent PARENT_KEY column whose value matches parent row's KEY (ADR-0010). download_odk_form(tables = TRUE) result therefore valid data argument – download/upload round-trips. submission's meta/instanceID derived deterministically key_col (whole row key_col NULL), re-running extract re-derives ids ODK Central rejects duplicates HTTP 409 (reported skipped) instead double-loading.

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: Attachments attached submission creation (ODK API constraint) scope. Choice-list validation best-effort: values fields backed external/dataset choices checked surface failed rows POST time invalid. Submission XML built without instance namespace, matching XLSForm-generated forms.

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- init_odk_connection()  # Round-trip: pull, correct locally, push back. tabs <- download_odk_form(con = con, project_id = 7,                           form_id = "RiverProspection", tables = TRUE)  # Preview validation without sending anything. eri_odk_upload(tabs, project_id = 7, form_id = "RiverProspection",                con = con, key_col = "KEY", dry_run = TRUE)  # Create the submissions (re-runs skip already-present rows via HTTP 409). eri_odk_upload(tabs, project_id = 7, form_id = "RiverProspection",                con = con, key_col = "KEY")  # A paper CSV whose headers don't match the form: map them. `key_col` names a # column left unmapped (mapping is applied first), so key on `rec`, not `village`. paper <- read.csv("historical_records.csv")   # cols: village, date_seen, stage, rec eri_odk_upload(paper, project_id = 7, form_id = "RiverProspection", con = con,                mapping = c(village = "site_name", date_seen = "prospection_date",                            stage = "river_stage"),                key_col = "rec", dry_run = TRUE) } # }

what: Query processed data with SQL (serverless DuckDB) — eri_query
title: Query processed data with SQL (serverless DuckDB) — eri_query
text: Run SQL across one processed datasets without standing database writing chain per-file reads. eri_query() attaches relevant parquet -process DuckDB session returns result tibble. Azure blob stays system record (ADR-0004).

title: Query processed data with SQL (serverless DuckDB) — eri_query
code: eri_query(   sql,   country = NULL,   disease = NULL,   data_source = NULL,   data_type = NULL,   period = NULL,   table = "data",   tables = NULL,   data_con = NULL )

title: Query processed data with SQL (serverless DuckDB) — eri_query
text: sql chr single SQL statement (DuckDB dialect) referencing -scope table names. country, disease, data_source, data_type, period Catalog filters selecting processed datasets attach table view. combination; NULL (default) means "use tables". table chr Name catalog-driven table SQL (default "data"). tables Named list additional tables register: entry data.frame, local parquet path, data/ blob path. data_con Azure container data/ blob; NULL connects automatically (catalog query blob path actually needed, pure data.frame queries stay offline).

title: Query processed data with SQL (serverless DuckDB) — eri_query
text: tibble query result.

title: Query processed data with SQL (serverless DuckDB) — eri_query
text: two ways put data scope, compose: Catalog-driven (roll-ups). Pass country / disease / data_source / data_type / period; eri_query() looks matching processed files data catalog, reads , stamps row country / disease / data_source / data_type / period, unions single table named table (default "data"). makes cross-country / cross-period aggregation one-liner: SELECT country, SUM(total_cases) data GROUP country. five column names reserved mode — dataset already carries one, overwritten catalog value (path authoritative axes) warning issued. Matched files differing columns unioned name; missing columns become NA. Explicit tables (joins). Pass tables = list(name = x) x data.frame, local .parquet path, data/ blob path. registered name can join, e.g. cases population table.

title: Query processed data with SQL (serverless DuckDB) — eri_query
text: Needs optional duckdb DBI packages (install.packages(c("duckdb", "DBI"))). large cross-dataset scans bounded local memory / download (ADR-0004).

title: Query processed data with SQL (serverless DuckDB) — eri_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Cross-country roll-up from the catalog eri_query(   "SELECT country, SUM(total_cases) AS cases FROM data GROUP BY country ORDER BY cases DESC",   disease = "malaria", data_type = "aggregate" )  # Join an approved dataset to a population table you supply eri_query(   "SELECT d.adm1, SUM(d.cases) * 1000.0 / p.pop AS rate      FROM data d JOIN pop p USING (adm1) GROUP BY d.adm1, p.pop",   disease = "malaria", data_type = "case", tables = list(pop = pop_df) ) } # }

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
text: country chr NULL Country code (e.g. "dr"). Used disease data_source. disease chr NULL Disease name (e.g. "malaria"). Used country data_source. data_source chr NULL channel ("surveillance", "programmatic", "research"). Used country disease. catalog (eri_catalog_query()) reports column, pull uses coordinates discovery query returns. data_type chr NULL measure (e.g. "case", "aggregate", "treatment", "tas"). Optional — NULL pulls four-axis channel-level processed layer. Back-compat: data_type given (pre-ADR-0012 form held channel), treated data_source. path chr NULL Explicit Azure blob path download . Mutually exclusive canonical args. dest chr NULL Local directory download files . Defaults data/ inside getwd(). data_con Azure container object data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically. progress lgl TRUE, show per-file byte progress bar (use large files, e.g. LandScan raster). Default FALSE uses one compact progress bar across files.

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
text: Character vector local file paths downloaded (invisibly).

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
text: Canonical: supply country, disease, data_source (optionally data_type measure) pull standard processed layer ({country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/processed/, per ADR-0012). Path: supply path pull Azure location (e.g. "data/spatial/dom_admin_boundaries/"). non-standard external files yet Azure, upload first eri_artifact_upload(), pull eri_artifact_pull().

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Pull canonical processed data using the coordinates the catalog reports eri_research_pull(country = "dr", disease = "malaria",                   data_source = "surveillance", data_type = "case")  # Four-axis (channel only, no measure) eri_research_pull(country = "dr", disease = "malaria", data_source = "surveillance")  # Pull standard spatial reference files eri_research_pull(path = "spatial/dom_admin_boundaries") } # }

what: Confirm the cutover comparison catches injected divergence — eri_simulate_check
title: Confirm the cutover comparison catches injected divergence — eri_simulate_check
text: Ties Phase-3 simulation harness together: inject known anomalies clean reference dataset (value columns, join keys) confirm eri_compare() — run cutover standard — flags result equivalent. Use build confidence cutover gate (eri_cutover_check()) catch real divergence relying .

title: Confirm the cutover comparison catches injected divergence — eri_simulate_check
code: eri_simulate_check(   reference,   by,   types = c("missing", "outlier", "negative", "typo", "drop"),   n = 1L,   seed = NULL,   tolerance = 0 )

title: Confirm the cutover comparison catches injected divergence — eri_simulate_check
text: reference clean data frame perturb compare . chr Key column(s) uniquely identifying row. Anomalies kept divergence shows detectable row/value deltas. types chr Anomaly types inject (see eri_inject_anomalies()). Defaults except duplicate. Passing duplicate error. n int Anomalies per type. Default 1. seed int NULL Optional RNG seed reproducible run. tolerance num Numeric tolerance passed eri_compare(). Default 0.

title: Confirm the cutover comparison catches injected divergence — eri_simulate_check
text: Invisibly, list detected (TRUE comparison flagged divergence, FALSE missed injected anomalies, NA nothing injected detection exercised), injected (anomaly log eri_inject_anomalies()), comparison (eri_compare() result, inspect deltas caught).

title: Confirm the cutover comparison catches injected divergence — eri_simulate_check
text: duplicate excluded default types: duplicate key reconciled per-cell, makes keyed comparison abort rather report delta (uniqueness check, separate reconciliation).

title: Confirm the cutover comparison catches injected divergence — eri_simulate_check
code: clean <- data.frame(id = 1:8, cases = c(5, 8, 3, 6, 9, 4, 7, 2), site = letters[1:8]) sim <- eri_simulate_check(clean, by = "id", n = 2, seed = 1) #> ✔ Simulation: 10 injected anomalies - `eri_compare()` flagged the divergence. sim$detected            # TRUE — eri_compare flagged the injected anomalies #> [1] TRUE sim$comparison$values   # the per-cell mismatches it caught #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>      id column new    old   #>   <int> <chr>  <chr>  <chr> #> 1     1 cases  "5000" 5     #> 2     2 cases  "-9"   8     #> 3     5 cases  "-10"  9     #> 4     7 cases  "7000" 7     #> 5     2 site    NA    b     #> 6     7 site   "g "   g

title: Load admin boundary from Azure — eri_spatial_load
text: country chr Country code (e.g. "dr", "ht"). level int Admin level (0 = country, 1 = region/department, 2 = province/commune, 3 = municipality/locality, 4 = sub-locality). data_con Azure container object data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically. cache lgl TRUE, cache boundary local research project read instead reading directly Azure. Caching delegates eri_research_pull(), downloads dest records pull research.yaml present – study's spatial inputs reproducible frozen eri_research_tag(). Default FALSE (read directly Azure). See ADR-0007. dest chr Directory cache cache = TRUE. Defaults project data/ directory.

what: Promote a research-project boundary to the canonical /spatial store — eri_spatial_promote
title: Promote a research-project boundary to the canonical /spatial store — eri_spatial_promote
text: explicit gate pushing boundary cleaned research project shared canonical data/spatial/{country}/adm{level}.rds, users studies pull . Unlike eri_spatial_upload() (brand-new boundaries), eri_spatial_promote() deliberate way replace existing canonical boundary, records promotion project's research.yaml provenance. Replacing existing boundary still requires explicit overwrite = TRUE shared data never clobbered accident, prior canonical version first archived spatial/_archive/<timestamp>/ replacement reversible. See ADR-0009.

title: Promote a research-project boundary to the canonical /spatial store — eri_spatial_promote

title: Promote a research-project boundary to the canonical /spatial store — eri_spatial_promote
text: local_path chr Path local boundary file promote (typically cleaned copy project data/ directory). country chr Country code (e.g. "dr", "ht"). level int Admin level (0–4). overwrite lgl TRUE, replace existing canonical boundary. Default FALSE. path chr Local project root (read research.yaml record provenance). Defaults getwd(). research.yaml found, promotion proceeds recorded (warning). data_con Azure container object data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically.

what: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
text: thin, opt-data-sourcing helper maps messy, free-text locality names incoming data canonical admin units authoritative boundary sf object (eri_spatial_load()). name-reconciliation step many studies hand analysis – analysis tool (matching/windowing/modelling stay research repo; see ADR-0006).

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
code: eri_spatial_reconcile(   data,   loc_cols,   shapefile,   admin_cols,   country_name = NULL,   method = "osm",   max_dist = 0L,   status_col = "reconcile_status",   coord_cols = c("longitude", "latitude"),   ... )

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
text: data data frame tibble containing free-text place-name columns. loc_cols chr vector free-text column names, ordered finest coarsest (e.g. c("loc", "mun", "prov")). Used match build geocoding address (finest first). shapefile admin-boundary sf object, e.g. eri_spatial_load(). admin_cols chr vector canonical name columns shapefile, parallel loc_cols (length, finest--coarsest order). ordering load-bearing: passing columns coarsest-first silently produces wrong matches. country_name chr Country name appended geocoding address (e.g. "Dominican Republic"), improving geocoder accuracy. Optional. method chr Geocoding service passed tidygeocoder::geocode() (e.g. "osm", "google"). NULL disables geocoding (match-). Default "osm". max_dist int Maximum edit distance approximate match finest level. 0 (default) requires exact normalized match. status_col chr Name status column added result. Default "reconcile_status"; values "matched", "geocoded", "geocoded_review" (geocoded low-confidence parent-inconsistent – verify use), "unresolved". coord_cols chr length-2 names longitude latitude columns added result. Default c("longitude", "latitude"). Populated row sent geocoder, regardless final status (geocoded points fall outside every polygon still record coordinates). ... Passed tidygeocoder::geocode() (e.g. min_time, api_options).

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
text: tibble: data loc_cols replaced canonical values confidently reconciled (originals kept unresolved flagged "geocoded_review"), plus two coord_cols, status_col, one geocoded_<admin_col> column per admin_cols level. geocoded_* columns hold admin units geocoded point fell (point--polygon), filled every geocoded row landed inside polygon – including "geocoded_review" rows – can see flagged row disagreed (resolve ) without re-running spatial join . NA string-matched, unresolved, outside-polygon rows.

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
text: reconciliation runs two passes (per issue #134): Match first. Free-text names normalized (lower-cased, accent- punctuation-stripped, whitespace-squished) matched canonical names. Coarser levels must match exactly; finest level may match approximately max_dist > 0 (Levenshtein distance via utils::adist()). Matched rows geocoded. Geocode residual. Rows match geocoded address built loc_cols (+ country_name), assigned canonical admin unit point--polygon via eri_spatial_join(). Set method = NULL skip geocoding entirely (match-). geocode trusted (status "geocoded", names assigned) service flag partial/low-confidence match assigned coarser admin units agree parent levels supplied loc_cols. Otherwise row flagged "geocoded_review": coordinates recorded inspection analyst's names left untouched. guards geocoders "best-guess" fabricated unmatched locality plausible nearby point (issue #145). partial-match signal currently read "google" method; methods expose one rely parent-consistency check alone. place-name address strings sent geocoder; data records leave machine. "google" method accurate requires API key billed per call: sign key, store user .Renviron GOOGLEGEOCODE_API_KEY (e.g. via usethis::edit_r_environ()) restart R. function checks key front explains missing. default "osm" (Nominatim) needs key. See tidygeocoder::geocode().

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dr_loc <- eri_spatial_load("dr", level = 4, cache = TRUE) incidence <- eri_spatial_reconcile(   incidence,   loc_cols     = c("loc", "mun", "prov"),   shapefile    = dr_loc,   admin_cols   = c("adm4_name", "adm3_name", "adm2_name"),   country_name = "Dominican Republic",   method       = "google",  # needs GOOGLEGEOCODE_API_KEY   max_dist     = 1 ) table(incidence$reconcile_status) } # }

what: Split a CMR monthly report into per-disease, per-measure staged datasets — eri_split_cmr
title: Split a CMR monthly report into per-disease, per-measure staged datasets — eri_split_cmr
text: Reads every sheet country's CMR schema routes (declaring disease data_type), writes sheet's parsed rows data/{country}/{disease}/programmatic/{data_type}/staged/ data blob (ADR-0012, #175). disease comes sheet (e.g. RB Treatment → oncho, SCH Treatment → sch, LF MMDP → lf); cross-programme Training sheets route together combined rblf disease. per-row #..._disease field — holds program-coverage codes (RB / RBLF / RBLFSCH) — kept data column, split , row duplicated across diseases. Data staged parsed -(machine-readable #field-code columns; reshape, automated DQ — CMR review manual). eri_approve() promotes {disease}/programmatic/{data_type} processed/. country bundled CMR schema, just abort: also writes starter schema template country working directory (template eri_onboard_cmr() produces) failure leaves something edit submit, just dead end.

what: Mirroring to the legacy contractor pipeline
title: Split a CMR monthly report into per-disease, per-measure staged datasets — eri_split_cmr
text: Phase-3 parallel run, countries' CMR still also feeds legacy contractor process reads raw workbook fixed Azure location ({project_folder}/{raw_dir}/{country}/{period}/, e.g. health-rb-country-expansion-dev/raw/filled_templates/ssd/202605/). Passing mirror_pipeline uploads path , DA one step (eri_split_cmr(..., mirror_pipeline = "rb-expansion")) instead also separately dropping file legacy pipeline pick . period defaults YYYYMM prefix parsed basename(path) (real convention observed submitted filenames); pass explicitly filename start way. replace eri_stage_cmr(): function reads raw-drop location copies workbook data/{country}/rblf/cmr/staged/ governed raw archive eri_approve() promotes. mirror_pipeline writes raw-drop location legacy pipeline's benefit — DA fresh-period pilot run may still want : eri_split_cmr(..., mirror_pipeline = ...) eri_stage_cmr() (reverse order; neither depends run first).

what: Re-splitting the same period from a corrected file
title: Split a CMR monthly report into per-disease, per-measure staged datasets — eri_split_cmr
text: fix issue upstream re-run different local file (e.g. _fixed.xlsx copy convention) period already split, prior staged file(s) sheet's destination folder removed first (period known) – otherwise broken original corrected file sit staged/ together, eri_approve()'s period-substring match promote . removal logged supersede_staged step.

title: Split a CMR monthly report into per-disease, per-measure staged datasets — eri_split_cmr
code: eri_split_cmr(   path,   country,   data_con = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   dry_run = FALSE,   mirror_pipeline = NULL,   period = NULL,   projects_con = NULL,   supersede_staged = FALSE )

title: Split a CMR monthly report into per-disease, per-measure staged datasets — eri_split_cmr
text: path str Local path CMR Excel file. country str Three-letter country code (e.g. "uga"); resolves CMR schema via load_cmr_schema(). data_con Azure container data blob. NULL connects using ERIFUNCTIONS_DATA_STORAGE_NAME. overwrite logical FALSE (default), warns overwriting existing staged file. dry_run logical TRUE, returns routing plan writes data. Default FALSE. One exception: dry run finds skipped sheet warning, fact logged (lightweight triage entry, staged data) stable log_path attach eri_logs_resolve() note later – see step 3 CMR guide. mirror_pipeline str NULL Registered pipeline name (e.g. "rb-expansion") whose legacy raw-drop location path also uploaded . Default NULL (mirror; sandbox-safe). period str NULL Reporting period (e.g. "202605"), used tag op-log (eri_cmr_last_plan() can find run ) , mirror_pipeline set, mirror upload. NULL (default) parses leading YYYYMM_ basename(path); required resolvable mirror_pipeline set. projects_con Azure container projects blob; used mirror_pipeline set. NULL, connects automatically. supersede_staged logical Re-splitting period DIFFERENT local file (e.g. _fixed.xlsx correction) can leave prior staged file behind destination folder – eri_approve()'s period match promote processed/. period known, candidate stale files (name starts period, just contains anywhere – real filename convention, collide unrelated file merely mentions six digits) always detected reported. Default FALSE warns – package's first destructive Azure operation opt-, automatic; set TRUE actually delete . Ignored (nothing detected deleted) period resolved.

title: Split a CMR monthly report into per-disease, per-measure staged datasets — eri_split_cmr
text: Invisibly, tibble one row per routed sheet: sheet, disease, data_type, dest, n_rows.

title: Split a CMR monthly report into per-disease, per-measure staged datasets — eri_split_cmr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Preview where each sheet would land eri_split_cmr("uga_2024_06.xlsx", "uga", dry_run = TRUE) # Stage for real, then approve each disease/measure eri_split_cmr("uga_2024_06.xlsx", "uga") eri_approve("uga", "oncho", "programmatic", "2024-06", data_type = "treatment") # One step: also mirror the raw file to the legacy contractor pipeline eri_split_cmr("202605_ssd_report.xlsx", "ssd", mirror_pipeline = "rb-expansion") # Re-splitting a corrected file for a period already staged: opt in to # actually removing the superseded original (default only warns) eri_split_cmr("202605_ssd_report_fixed.xlsx", "ssd", supersede_staged = TRUE) } # }

what: Show the task registry: what are you trying to do? — eri_task_map
title: Show the task registry: what are you trying to do? — eri_task_map
text: Prints (returns invisibly) task registry: tree common DA/Epi tasks grouped intent, naming representative call, guide walks end end (), reference functions touches. shared source generated task index article reads (inst/registry/task_map.yaml).

title: Show the task registry: what are you trying to do? — eri_task_map
code: eri_task_map()

title: Show the task registry: what are you trying to do? — eri_task_map
text: Invisibly, data frame one row per task: branch, id, title, role, call, guide, reference (list-column), next_ids (list-column).

title: Show the task registry: what are you trying to do? — eri_task_map
code: eri_task_map() #>  #> ── Task registry: what are you trying to do? ─────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Get set up ── #>  #> • Install the package and connect to Azure, ODK, SharePoint, and Teams -- #>   `get_azure_storage_connection()` (guide: "connections-guide") #> • Learn the data-addressing vocabulary (channel vs. measure) -- #>   `eri_data_model()` (guide: "data-model-card") #> • Follow the paced new-analyst onboarding path -- `eri_data_model()` (guide: #>   "onboarding") #>  #> ── Bring data into the system ── #>  #> • A monthly country report (CMR Excel workbook) -- `eri_stage_cmr(country, #>   period)` (guide: "da-cmr-guide") #> • A surveillance dataset (csv/xlsx from a country) -- `eri_ingest(path, #>   country, disease, data_source, data_type)` (guide: "da-ingest-guide") #> • ODK Central survey submissions -- `eri_odk_sync(project_id, form_id)` (guide: #>   "da-odk-guide") #> • Manage ODK Central users and roles -- `eri_odk_bulk_users(csv_path)` #> • Admin boundaries or population rasters -- `eri_spatial_load(country, level)` #>   (guide: "spatial-workflow") #> • Compare a new pipeline's output against a legacy one before trusting it -- #>   `eri_compare(new, old, by)` #>  #> ── Check data quality and approve ── #>  #> • Review and approve a CMR workbook, interactively -- `eri_dq_review(country, #>   period)` (guide: "da-dq-review-guide") #> • QC a submission and give a country feedback -- `run_dq_checks(data, schema)` #>   (guide: "da-qc-feedback-guide") #> • Catch epidemiological anomalies (spikes, gaps) before analysis -- #>   `add_anomaly_pct_change(data, value_col, period_col, group_cols, year_col)` #>   (guide: "epi-dq-guide") #> • Fix or extend a DQ schema (allowed values, ranges) -- #>   `eri_dq_schema_edit(country, disease, data_source, data_type)` (guide: #>   "dq-pipeline") #>  #> ── Work the backlog ── #>  #> • Find and close out failed or DQ log entries -- `eri_logs(country, disease, #>   data_source, data_type)` (guide: "da-logs-guide") #> • Reconstruct what happened to a dataset -- `eri_audit(country, disease, #>   data_source, data_type)` #> • File or triage internal feedback tickets -- `eri_feedback(message, area)` #> • Share files via SharePoint or post to Teams -- #>   `eri_sharepoint_upload(local_path, site, folder_path)` (guide: #>   "sharepoint-workflow") #>  #> ── Use approved data: query, analyse, report ── #>  #> • Answer an ad-hoc request with SQL -- `eri_query(sql)` (guide: #>   "da-adhoc-guide") #> • See what's in the catalog, or rebuild it -- `eri_catalog_query(country, #>   disease)` #> • Turn approved data into on-brand tables, plots, or decks -- `eri_table(data, #>   title)` (guide: "da-reporting-guide") #> • Summarise an approved survey (e.g. LF TAS) -- `eri_lf_tas_summary(data, #>   fts_col, rdt_col)` (guide: "da-survey-report-guide") #> • Incidence, epiweeks, and epidemic curves -- `eri_incidence_rate(cases, pop)` #>   (guide: "epi-analytics") #>  #> ── Run a research study ── #>  #> • Start or resume a research project -- `eri_research_init(project_name, #>   country, disease, description)` (guide: "epi-research-guide") #> • Log progress and manage artifacts as you go -- `eri_research_log(note)` #> • Snapshot data and tag a citable, reproducible version -- #>   `eri_research_tag(label)` #> • Pull or contribute a reusable research template -- `eri_template_list()` #>  #> ── Places and maps ── #>  #> • Reconcile free-text localities to admin units -- `eri_spatial_reconcile(data, #>   loc_cols, shapefile, admin_cols)` (guide: "epi-reconcile-guide") #> • Join points to admin units and map them -- `eri_spatial_join(data, lat_col, #>   lon_col, shapefile)` (guide: "spatial-workflow") #> • Get population totals for an area -- `eri_spatial_pop(shapefile)` #>  #> ── Add a new country, disease, or data type ── #>  #> • Onboard a new surveillance country or disease -- #>   `eri_onboard_country(country_code, country_name, disease)` (guide: #>   "da-onboard-guide") #> • Onboard a new CMR-reporting country -- `eri_onboard_cmr(country_code, #>   country_name)` (guide: "da-onboard-guide") #> • Contribute a schema or disease analytics to the package -- #>   `eri_schema_validate(schema_path)` (guide: "adding-a-program")

what: Send a message to Microsoft Teams — eri_teams_send
title: Send a message to Microsoft Teams — eri_teams_send
text: Sends plain-text message Teams channel individual. Supports two delivery paths: incoming webhook (auth required, channel ) Graph API (requires bearer token, supports DMs channels).

title: Send a message to Microsoft Teams — eri_teams_send
code: eri_teams_send(message, to = NULL, team = NULL, channel = NULL, token = NULL)

title: Send a message to Microsoft Teams — eri_teams_send
text: message single character string. message body. Email user ID recipient 1:1 DM. Pass "self" NULL message (Graph API ). team Display name Teams team (channel messages, Graph API). channel Display name channel within team (Graph API), ignored using webhook (webhook URL already encodes channel). token Graph API bearer token. NULL, checks ERIFUNCTIONS_TEAMS_TOKEN; falls back incoming webhook ERIFUNCTIONS_TEAMS_WEBHOOK set.

title: Send a message to Microsoft Teams — eri_teams_send
text: Invisibly returns httr response object.

title: Send a message to Microsoft Teams — eri_teams_send
text: Delivery routing: team + channel supplied, token available \(\rightarrow\) Graph API channel message. supplied (omitted), token available \(\rightarrow\) Graph API DM. ERIFUNCTIONS_TEAMS_WEBHOOK set, token \(\rightarrow\) incoming webhook. Otherwise error raised setup instructions.

what: Legacy I/O handler for the erifunctions data interface — erifunctions_io
title: Legacy I/O handler for the erifunctions data interface — erifunctions_io
text: Manages read/write/list/create/delete functions erifunctions. function adapted tidypolis_io. ergonomic interface tab-completion per-operation help pages, consider verb-named wrappers: eri_read(), eri_write(), eri_list(), eri_file_exists(), eri_dir_exists(), eri_dir_create(), eri_delete(), eri_dir_delete().

title: Legacy I/O handler for the erifunctions data interface — erifunctions_io
code: erifunctions_io(   io,   file_loc = "",   obj = NULL,   azure = TRUE,   azcontainer = suppressMessages(get_azure_storage_connection()),   full_names = TRUE,   progress = FALSE,   ... )

title: Legacy I/O handler for the erifunctions data interface — erifunctions_io
text: io str type operation use. Valid values include: "read": reads data specified file_path. "write": writes data specified file_path. "list": lists files specified file_path. "exists.dir": determines whether directory present. "exists.file": determines whether file present. "create.dir": creates directory specified file_path. "delete": deletes file specified file_path. "delete.dir": deletes folder specified file_path. file_loc str Path file. obj str Object loaded Azure azure logical Whether function interact TCC Azure environment. Defaults TRUE, otherwise, interacts files locally. azcontainer Azure container container object returned get_azure_storage_connection(). full_names logical io="list", include full reference path. Default TRUE. progress logical Show AzureStor's byte progress bar transfer. Default FALSE (suppressed; erifunctions renders output). Set TRUE large single read/upload. ... Optional parameters work readr::read_delim() readxl::read_excel().

title: Legacy I/O handler for the erifunctions data interface — erifunctions_io
text: Conditional io. io "read", return tibble. io "list", return list file names. Otherwise, function return NULL. exists.dir exists.file return logical.

title: Legacy I/O handler for the erifunctions data interface — erifunctions_io
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- erifunctions_io("read", file_loc = "df1.csv") df2 <- erifunctions_io("read", file_loc = "df2.xlsx", sheet = 1, skip = 2) list_of_df <- list(df_1 = df, df_2 = df) erifunctions_io("write", file_loc = "Data/test/df.csv", obj = df) erifunctions_io("write", file_loc = "Data/test/df.xlsx", obj = list_of_df) erifunctions_io("exists.dir", "Data/nonexistentfolder") erifunctions_io("exists.file", file_loc = "Data/test/df1.csv") erifunctions_io("create.dir", "Data/nonexistentfolder") erifunctions_io("list") } # }

title: Validate connection to Azure — get_azure_storage_connection
text: Generate token connects TCC Azure resources validates individual still access.

title: Validate connection to Azure — get_azure_storage_connection
code: get_azure_storage_connection(   tenant_id = Sys.getenv("ERIFUNCTIONS_TENANT_ID", unset = .ERI_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID),   app_id = Sys.getenv("ERIFUNCTIONS_APP_ID", unset = .ERI_DEFAULT_APP_ID),   resource_endpoint = Sys.getenv("ERIFUNCTIONS_RESOURCE_ENDPOINT", unset =     .ERI_DEFAULT_RESOURCE_ENDPOINT),   storage_name = Sys.getenv("ERIFUNCTIONS_STORAGE_NAME"),   auth = "authorization_code",   creds_yaml_path = NULL,   ... )

title: Validate connection to Azure — get_azure_storage_connection
text: tenant_id str Azure tenant. Defaults ERIFUNCTIONS_TENANT_ID env var, TCC ERI Entra tenant unset. app_id str Application (client) ID. Defaults ERIFUNCTIONS_APP_ID env var, – unset – Microsoft's first-party Azure CLI public client ("04b07795-8ddb-461a-bbee-02f9e1bf7b46"), interactive auth works per-user setup. resource_endpoint str Storage endpoint URL. Defaults ERIFUNCTIONS_RESOURCE_ENDPOINT env var, team eridev ADLS endpoint unset. storage_name str Name storage blob. Defaults Sys.getenv("ERIFUNCTIONS_STORAGE_NAME"). auth str Authorization type defaults "authorization_code", can changed service principal. Valid values :"authorization_code", "device_code", "client_credentials", "resource_owner", "on_behalf_of". See Details AzureAuth::get_azure_token() details. creds_yaml_path str Path YAML credentials file containing service principal credentials (tcc_azure$client_id, tcc_azure$client_secret). NULL (default) environment variables ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_ID / ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_SECRET set, used automatically. Otherwise falls back interactive auth via auth. ... additional parameters passed AzureAuth::get_azure_token().

what: Run data quality checks on surveillance data — run_dq_checks
title: Run data quality checks on surveillance data — run_dq_checks
text: Applies sequence automated DQ checks defined schema: preprocessing (smart-quote removal, column-name stripping, empty-row dropping), column alias resolution, required-column validation, type coercion, range checks, categorical translations corrections, NA filling count columns, temporal cross-checks, derived column computation, aggregate consistency checks. Additional analyst-supplied checks can appended via custom_checks.

title: Run data quality checks on surveillance data — run_dq_checks
code: run_dq_checks(data, schema, custom_checks = list())

title: Run data quality checks on surveillance data — run_dq_checks
text: data data.frame tibble raw surveillance data. schema Named list returned load_dq_schema(). custom_checks list functions, signature function(data, log, flags) returning named list three elements. Applied order automated checks.

title: Run data quality checks on surveillance data — run_dq_checks
text: named list three elements: $data: cleaned tibble corrections applied derived columns added $log: tibble automated corrections (columns: row, column, original_value, corrected_value, rule, action) $flags: tibble issues requiring analyst review (columns: row, column, value, issue)

text: vignettes/da--guide.Rmd: interactive-wizard course correction (Phases -C.3) built four flows eri_do() incrementally, getting “prefer just ?” callout pipeline guide, nothing ever documented wizard’s menu whole. new guide missing piece: top-level menu, five paths (CMR, surveillance ingest, ODK, onboarding, DQ-review shortcut) actual prompts decision points, verified directly R/wizard.R’s source (every menu title, confirmation prompt, message string checked real code, accuracy discipline guide-authoring recipe calls ). deliberately stays tour, redo domain-knowledge walkthroughs, section points pipeline’s guide (CMR, ingest, ODK, onboarding, DQ review) real captured output field-level detail one skips. eri_do() interactive-refuses run script, none guide’s transcripts executed inside document way ordinary function call ; every one illustrated rather live-captured, honest convention da-cmr-guide.Rmd da-odk-guide.Rmd already use non-sandbox-safe steps, stated front. Cross-linked _pkgdown.yml (new “Quick reference & onboarding” entry), docs/guides.md, getting-started.Rmd, every existing pipeline guide’s eri_do() callout (da-cmr-guide, da-ingest-guide, da-odk-guide, da-onboard-guide, da-dq-review-guide, da-logs-guide, da-cheatsheet, orientation).

what: eri_guide() retired to a static lookup; doc cut turned out much smaller than planned (Phase C.3 of the interactive-wizard course correction)
title: erifunctions 0.9.32
text: eri_guide() deprecated (r lifecycle::badge("deprecated"), .Deprecated() warning every call). menu-driven console wizard ever run 4 ~32 tasks; everything else describe, vignettes eri_task_map() already . Rather delete exported name outright mid-transition, ’s narrowed: eri_guide(task_id) still shows task’s call, guide, reference functions (menu); called argument, now just prints eri_task_map()’s full listing. Deleted menu: .eri_guide_zero_arg(), .eri_guide_show_task(), .eri_guide_last_branch_id()/.eri_guide_set_last_branch_id() (session memory), .eri_guide_resolve_branch_choice() – none purpose ’s menu drive. documentation-cutting plan turned much smaller design consult estimated, worth explaining rather force-fitting number stale plan guessed . consult (written Phases /B/C.1/C.2 shipped) assumed 7 DA pipeline guides (da-cmr-guide, da-ingest-guide, da-odk-guide, da-onboard-guide, da-dq-review-guide, da-logs-guide, da-qc-feedback-guide) redundant “walkthroughs eri_do() replaces” proposed folding 7 one ~10-vignette reference set. practice: (1) pipeline guide already got “prefer just ? run eri_do()” callout phase shipped, redirect consult wanted already delivered incrementally, deferred phase; (2) da-odk-guide da-onboard-guide cover substantial ground eri_do() automate (monitoring survey, managing ODK app users, filling /validating/submitting schema) – cutting destroyed real, non-redundant domain knowledge; (3) da-dq-review-guide documents exact interactive loop eri_do()’s CMR flow hands , da-logs-guide documents exact tools eri_do() points DA manual backlog triage – reference material wizard already , competing walkthroughs; (4) da-qc-feedback-guide wizard overlap (offline QC + Teams notification, different task entirely). actual redundancy much narrower: two “quick reference” pages (da-cheatsheet, orientation) whose “pipeline use?” decision trees purely duplicated eri_do()’s menu, unique content – trimmed one-line pointer eri_do(), keeping everything else. Also added missing wizard-context callouts da-dq-review-guide/da-logs-guide (point 3 ). Net: 0 vignettes deleted (still 25) – re-audit found 8 real vignettes consult’s plan never mentions (da-adhoc-guide, da-cheatsheet, da-reporting-guide, da-survey-report-guide, dq-pipeline, epi-dq-guide, orientation, sharepoint-workflow), meaning “~26 -> ~11” count already built incomplete picture – forcing cut hit stale number solving guessed target instead genuine redundancy. onboarding.Rmd (paced new-DA path): “ingestion spine” days now point eri_do() primary way task, existing guides reframed “’s happening underneath.” Also removed “Competency checklist (DA + mentor sign )” heading’s mentor-sign-framing (DA rules complaint raised earlier session, superseded time larger course correction – finally addressed since file already revised). pkgdown/index.md/README.md’s eri_guide() mentions reworded eri_task_map().

what: eri_do() now covers onboarding too (Phase C.2 of the interactive-wizard course correction)
title: erifunctions 0.9.31
text: eri_do()’s top menu gained “Onboard new country, disease, data type”, powered new .eri_flow_onboard() (R/wizard.R) covering three real onboarding shapes: surveillance country/disease (eri_onboard_country()), CMR country (eri_onboard_cmr(create_dirs = TRUE)), NTD disease’s MDA/prevalence schemas (eri_onboard_disease(), local-, Azure folders). sub-flow dry-runs first (reusing real function’s dry_run = TRUE preview text), confirms, writes real. Deliberately stops “schema template written folders exist.” da-onboard-guide.Rmd’s golden rule “onboarding scaffolds; doesn’t finish ” – filling schema’s disease-specific columns, validating , submitting via pull request real domain judgment calls wizard fabricate. three eri_onboard_*() functions already print “Next steps” via cli_inform(), flow doesn’t re-print paraphrase text – one copy, two can quietly drift apart. Real bug found fixed building : .eri_wizard_prompt_country_code() (shared Phase B’s ingest flow) validated typed country codes ^[-z]{2,4}$ – package’s sandbox demo country, atlantis (8 letters), exactly example warning message names valid, silently rejected. Onboarding precisely flow DA types brand-new code, surfaced immediately; widened ^[-z]{2,15}$ added regression test locking atlantis case. review-agent caught real scope drift merge: surveillance CMR sub-flows called eri_onboard_country()/eri_onboard_cmr() without ever surfacing language (“en”/“fr”), silently defaulting English – real gap ERI’s Francophone countries (Haiti), exactly design consult called (“language pick-list”). Added shared .eri_wizard_prompt_language() prompt sub-flows. Cross-linked pkgdown/index.md, README.md, callout top vignettes/da-onboard-guide.Rmd pointing eri_do(). tests/testthat/test-wizard.R (113 tests, +27 Phase C.1): routing across three sub-flows, sub-flow’s dry-run -> confirm -> real-write order, decline--confirm cancel--blank-input/language-prompt paths, NTD flow’s menu-choice -> data_types vector mapping.

Built real init_odk_connection() / list_odk_projects() / list_odk_forms() / eri_odk_register() / eri_odk_sync() / eri_odk_list_registered() functions — nothing reimplemented. Rather fabricate approve step underlying tooling doesn’t support, flow honest handing , pointer surveillance ingest guide eri_approve() next step. Confirms header-comment thesis Phase B: CMR, ingest, now ODK built, three flows genuinely share enough top-level shape justify design consult’s proposed declarative flow_map.yaml/kind:-dispatch schema — low-level helper vocabulary (.eri_prompt_pick_country(), .eri_wizard_confirm(), .eri_wizard_step(), etc.) actually shared. R/wizard.R’s header comment now records settled, evidence-based conclusion rather deferred question. Cross-linked pkgdown/index.md, README.md, callout top vignettes/da-odk-guide.Rmd pointing eri_do(). tests/testthat/test-wizard.R (83 tests, +16 Phase B): full connect → discover → register → sync path, already-registered short-circuit (redundant registration call), two clean-failure paths (connection fails, visible projects) — every live-ODK-touching function reachable path mocked directly, per session’s test-mock-safety discipline.

what: eri_do() now covers surveillance ingest too (Phase B of the interactive-wizard course correction)
title: erifunctions 0.9.29
text: eri_do()’s top menu gained “Bring surveillance dataset”, powered new .eri_flow_ingest() (R/wizard.R) shared helpers CMR flow: pick file native dialog, confirm reporting period, one eri_ingest() call raw-archive + DQ-check + stage (unlike CMR’s multi-step upload/stage/split, eri_ingest() single call design). mirror_pipeline auto-detected way CMR’s flow — via eri_cutover_status(), even considered country actually registered hsp-mal legacy mirror (aren’t, wizard checks first rather ever constructing call knows eri_ingest() reject). Two new reusable helpers, needed ingest’s country/disease backing registry way CMR’s rb-expansion pipeline : .eri_prompt_pick_or_type() (pick-list typed “” escape hatch — used disease, ADR-0012’s data model deliberately leaves unregistered) .eri_wizard_prompt_country_code() (validated free-typed country code, since eri_ingest() deliberately country-locked — ’s lets DA guides demo safely made-atlantis country). Deliberately ship ODK PR. Two things turned genuinely different CMR, just superficially: ingest’s approval period free text matched staged filename (eri_approve()’s documented behavior — fixed YYYYMM convention detect), DQ flags land generic eri_logs() backlog, eri_cmr_dq_report()’s per-sheet shape eri_dq_review()’s loop built . flow’s DQ step summary open log entries plus pointer existing scriptable triage tools (eri_logs()/eri_dq_flag_resolve()/eri_logs_resolve()), full interactive per-flag walker — smaller CMR’s DQ stage, stated way rather overstating parity. Building properly, ODK’s registration workflow, real follow-work, rushed alongside . Cross-linked pkgdown/index.md, README.md, callout top vignettes/da-ingest-guide.Rmd pointing eri_do(). tests/testthat/test-wizard.R (67 tests, +21 Phase ): every new helper unit-tested directly (including “never even check cutover status country never registered mirror ” case), full flow integration test, real failure-mode test (DA declines approve open DQ log entries outstanding).

what: eri_do(): a guided console wizard that runs the CMR pipeline for you (Phase A of the interactive-wizard course correction)
title: erifunctions 0.9.28
text: Added eri_do() (R/wizard.R), menu-driven wizard carries Data Analyst bringing monthly country report (CMR) system end end: pick country pick-list (never typed), pick local file native file dialog (rstudioapi::selectFile()/file.choose(), never hand-constructed Azure path), confirm filename-detected reporting month, watch upload → stage → split happen automatically, handing directly review--approve loop eri_dq_review() already uses. function names memorize. direct course correction, extension docs-site redesign . work optimized discoverability (can DA find right guide); actual ask, stated explicitly, execution simplicity (can DA job without learning tool). design consult (commissioned session, full document docs/design/interactive-wizard-consult.md) verified real fix smaller first looked: “9-function ordeal” DAs faced inflated — 2 steps read-inspection last 4 already one function (eri_dq_review()) — genuinely new work just front half (upload → stage → split), handing already works. mirror_pipeline now auto-detected, never asked . eri_do() checks eri_cutover_status()$eligible every disease/measure workbook feeds mirrors legacy pipeline streams haven’t yet proven 3-period parity (ADR-0015) — DA never sees decides flag. unrecorded/errored stream defaults mirroring, safe direction. eri_dq_review()’s main loop now .eri_dq_review_loop(), extracted eri_do() can hand directly (plan just built, extra lookup) instead duplicating check → fix → re-check → approve control flow. eri_dq_review() unchanged — signature, console behavior — now also returns (invisibly) whether session ended "approved", "force_approved", "exited", eri_do() uses decide whether print closing message. Deliberately concrete R, declarative flow schema. consult’s design proposed flow_map.yaml/kind:-dispatch engine generalizing across pipelines start — building single consumer (Phase exactly one flow) exactly premature abstraction whole redesign effort repeatedly avoided. Deferred Phase B gives two real flows (surveillance ingest, ODK) generalize . tests/testthat/test-wizard.R (46 tests): every helper unit-tested directly (country picker, file picker fallback chain, period detection matching eri_split_cmr()’s regex exactly, path derivation real pipeline registry, mirror auto-detection including error-defaults--mirror case), plus full flow integration test asserting real core functions called right order derived path/period/mirror flag — cancel/ decline/resume--progress paths, DA backing interrupted session doesn’t silently wrong thing. Cross-linked pkgdown/index.md, README.md, callout top vignettes/da-cmr-guide.Rmd pointing eri_do() easier path — full guide-cutting pass (~26 articles → ~11) Phase C, done . review pass caught fixed several real gaps merge: eri_dq_review()’s @returns doc still said “invisibly, NULL” loop extraction, now returns exit status (real doc/behavior mismatch, fixed). .eri_flow_cmr() opened projects_con upload step didn’t thread (data_con) eri_stage_cmr()/dry-run eri_split_cmr() call, opened extra live-Azure connection — now threaded throughout. .eri_prompt_pick_file() called file.choose()/rstudioapi::selectFile() inline, can’t safely mocked (base-package locking) — test re-ask/fallback logic risked real, blocking GUI dialog headless CI; extracted .eri_wizard_raw_file_dialog() tests can mock just piece, added tests. top menu’s “review something already staged” shortcut test ; added one. CMR guide’s new callout used .eri-path-nav, CSS class documented strictly-ordered-path footers (guide isn’t part one) — switched plain blockquote matching guide’s existing callout convention instead.

what: Fix: the site homepage logo rendered doubled and at full column width
